SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients whose age at screening is greater than or equal to 20 years
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those whose fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) is greater than or equal to 6.0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 6.0;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL OR f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing values of weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing values of waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants who are missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants who are missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants who are missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants who are missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants who are missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           ELSE 'non white'
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q3
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: ≤25%'
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" > (SELECT q1 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) AND r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and ≤50%'
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" > (SELECT q2 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) AND r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and ≤75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- STEP 4.5.1: Calculate eGFR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_egfr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.2: Calculate UACR (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * (f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl")) AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.3: Determine presence of kidney disease based on eGFR and UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_kidney_disease AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" < 60 OR u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN '1'
           ELSE '0'
       END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_outcome_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  o."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_cov_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_cov_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_predictor_wwi_ratio p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_kidney_disease o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening more than 40, who did not say "No" when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >130..
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening > 40
- whether doctor told you have diabetes is NOT "No"
- fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 130

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things"
- missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
- missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
- missing "feeling tired or having little energy"
- missing "poor appetite or overeating"
- missing "feeling bad about yourself"
- missing "trouble concentrating on things"
- missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
- missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR "low food security"
  - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
  - food secure: if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  total score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question scored as:
    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
  If a question's value is null, assign 0.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients with age at screening more than 40
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" > 40;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who did not say "No" when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IS NULL OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" != 'No';

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 130
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 130;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing values in "have little interest in doing things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling tired or having little energy"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing values in "poor appetite or overeating"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling bad about yourself"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble concentrating on things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing values in "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with missing values in "thought you would be better off dead"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food Insecurity Level (Predictor)
-- Categorize adult food security question into food insecurity levels
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression Outcome (Outcome)
-- Score each of the nine questions based on response, assigning 0 if null
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores_mapped AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy",
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things",
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total score from the nine questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_total_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things" +
    "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" +
    "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" +
    "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" +
    "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating" +
    "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself" +
    "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things" +
    "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" +
    "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores_mapped;

-- Determine depression outcome based on total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fil."food_insecurity_level",
  t_do."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fil ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fil."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome t_do ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_do."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

    END, 0) AS "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things",
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total score from the nine questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_total_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things" +
    "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" +
    "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" +
    "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" +
    "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating" +
    "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself" +
    "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things" +
    "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" +
    "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores_mapped;

-- Determine depression outcome based on total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fil."food_insecurity_level",
  t_do."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fil ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fil."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome t_do ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_do."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 Only include participants who are not determined to be ineligible, where ineligibility is defined as having a weight-adjusted waist ratio (waist circumference (cm) divided by square root of weight (kg)) below the age-group specific mean calculated separately for participants aged less than 60 years and those aged 60 years or older and have waist circumference (cm) value and weight (kg) value; participants below their age-group mean are nevertheless included unless their serum creatinine (mg/dL) exceeds the race/Hispanic origin specific 75th percentile.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Filter for participants with non-missing values required for inclusion criteria calculation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_base_data AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    "waist_circumference_cm",
    "weight_kg",
    "age_in_years_at_screening",
    "creatinine_mg_dl",
    "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE
    "waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND
    "weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND
    "age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL AND
    "creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND
    "race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate weight-adjusted waist ratio (wwi_ratio) and age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_and_age_group AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    "waist_circumference_cm",
    "weight_kg",
    "age_in_years_at_screening",
    "creatinine_mg_dl",
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    "waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT("weight_kg") AS "wwi_ratio",
    CASE
        WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
        ELSE '>=60'
    END AS "age_group"
FROM temp_inclusion_base_data;

-- STEP 1.3: Calculate age-group specific mean for wwi_ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group_mean_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT
    "age_group",
    AVG("wwi_ratio") AS "age_group_mean_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_and_age_group
GROUP BY "age_group";

-- STEP 1.4: Calculate race/Hispanic origin specific 75th percentile for serum creatinine
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_creatinine_percentile AS
SELECT
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "creatinine_mg_dl") AS "race_specific_75th_percentile_creatinine"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_and_age_group
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 1.5: Evaluate the intermediate condition for ineligibility
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_intermediate_ineligibility_condition AS
SELECT
    t."respondent_sequence_number",
    t."wwi_ratio",
    t."creatinine_mg_dl",
    agm."age_group_mean_wwi_ratio",
    rcp."race_specific_75th_percentile_creatinine",
    (t."wwi_ratio" < agm."age_group_mean_wwi_ratio") AND (t."creatinine_mg_dl" > rcp."race_specific_75th_percentile_creatinine") AS "intermediate_condition_and"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_and_age_group t
JOIN temp_age_group_mean_wwi_ratio agm ON t."age_group" = agm."age_group"
JOIN temp_race_creatinine_percentile rcp ON t."race_hispanic_origin" = rcp."race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 1.6: Determine final inclusion based on NOT (intermediate_condition_and)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_intermediate_ineligibility_condition
WHERE NOT "intermediate_condition_and";

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" != 'Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam';

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing values of weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing values of waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants who are missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants who are missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants who are missing urine albumin (mg/L)

SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_race AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",

        ELSE 'non white'
    END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_gender AS

    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_age AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
        ELSE '>=60'
    END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw wwi_ratio for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi_ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign wwi_ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_ratio_quartiles AS
SELECT
    r."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
        ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
    END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- Calculate eGFR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_egfr AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m2" < 60 OR u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN '1'
        ELSE '0'
    END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_outcome_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  o."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_cov_race r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_cov_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_cov_age a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_pred_wwi_ratio_quartiles p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_kidney_disease o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include individuals whose glycohemoglobin (%%) level is not less than one-tenth of their current age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" >= ("age_in_years_at_screening" / 10.0);

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who said 'Yes' to 'doctor told you have diabetes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Predictor: serum albumin (g/dL) binned
-- Extract raw serum albumin for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute percentile cutoffs for serum albumin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.20) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS p20,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.80) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS p80
FROM temp_predictor_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin to 'low', 'normal', 'high' bins
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_albumin_binned AS
SELECT
  r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT p20 FROM temp_predictor_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'low'
    WHEN r."albumin_g_dl" >= (SELECT p20 FROM temp_predictor_albumin_cutoffs) AND r."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT p80 FROM temp_predictor_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'normal'
    ELSE 'high'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_predictor_albumin_raw r;

-- 4.4 Outcome: diabetic retinopathy outcome
-- Calculate 60th percentile of serum albumin (g/dL) within each race/Hispanic origin group for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_percentiles AS
SELECT
  f."race_hispanic_origin",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.60) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."albumin_g_dl") AS percentile_60_by_race
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_albumin_percentiles_by_race AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."albumin_g_dl",
  f."race_hispanic_origin",
  f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy",
  rp.percentile_60_by_race
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_percentiles rp ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = rp."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Determine the diabetic retinopathy outcome based on conditions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy_outcome AS
SELECT
  t."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN t."albumin_g_dl" >= t.percentile_60_by_race AND t."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN '1'
    ELSE '0'
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy_outcome"
FROM temp_outcome_albumin_percentiles_by_race t;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  o."diabetic_retinopathy_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_predictor_albumin_binned p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy_outcome o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

-- Calculate 60th percentile of serum albumin (g/dL) within each race/Hispanic origin group for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_percentiles AS
SELECT
  f."race_hispanic_origin",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.60) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."albumin_g_dl") AS percentile_60_by_race
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_albumin_percentiles_by_race AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."albumin_g_dl",
  f."race_hispanic_origin",
  f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy",
  rp.percentile_60_by_race
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_percentiles rp ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = rp."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Determine the diabetic retinopathy outcome based on conditions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy_outcome AS
SELECT
  t."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN t."albumin_g_dl" >= t.percentile_60_by_race AND t."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN '1'
    ELSE '0'
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy_outcome"
FROM temp_outcome_albumin_percentiles_by_race t;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  o."diabetic_retinopathy_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_predictor_albumin_binned p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy_outcome o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- diabetes defined as meeting any of the following:
    - doctor told you have diabetes = "Yes" OR "Borderline"
    - reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
    - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security question
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR "low food security"
    - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
    - food secure: if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total depression score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  total depression score calculated as the sum of scores from the nine depression questions:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead
  Each question's score:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
    - If value is null, assign 0

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security question
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."hh_adults_cut_size_or_skip_meals" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing values in "have little interest in doing things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling tired or having little energy"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing values in "poor appetite or overeating"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling bad about yourself"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble concentrating on things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing values in "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with missing values in "thought you would be better off dead"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race Hispanic Origin (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food Insecurity Level (Predictor)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."hh_adults_cut_size_or_skip_meals" = 'Yes' THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."hh_adults_cut_size_or_skip_meals" = 'No' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL -- Should not happen due to exclusion, but good practice
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression Outcome (Outcome)
-- Step 1: Map each of the nine depression questions to a score (0-3), assigning 0 for NULLs.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_question_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 -- Should not be NULL due to exclusion, but handles unexpected values
    END, 0) AS score_interest,
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS score_depressed,
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS score_sleep,
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS score_tired,
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS score_appetite,
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS score_bad_self,
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS score_concentrating,
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS score_movement,
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS score_dead_thoughts
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (score_interest + score_depressed + score_sleep + score_tired + score_appetite +

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Determine participants with diabetes based on multiple criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_diabetes_criteria AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes') AS "told_diabetes",
    (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0) AS "high_fpg",
    (f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5) AS "high_hba1c",
    (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes') AS "on_diabetic_pills"
FROM final_master_table f;

-- STEP 1.2: Include participants if any of the diabetes criteria are met
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT
    d."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_diabetes_criteria d
WHERE d."told_diabetes"
   OR d."high_fpg"
   OR d."high_hba1c"
   OR d."on_diabetic_pills";

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing serum creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Calculate WWI for all participants for later use
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_raw_all AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "wwi_value"
FROM final_master_table f
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Define the sub-cohort for WWI and creatinine statistics (inclusion-eligible, >=20 years old, and non-missing relevant data)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_stats_cohort_valid_data AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender",
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    f."creatinine_mg_dl",
    twwi."wwi_value"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_wwi_raw_all twwi ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = twwi."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20
  AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."gender" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.10: Calculate mean WWI within each gender group from the stats cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_wwi_by_gender AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    AVG("wwi_value") AS "mean_wwi_gender"
FROM temp_stats_cohort_valid_data
GROUP BY "gender";

-- STEP 2.11: Calculate 75th percentile of serum creatinine within each race/Hispanic origin group from the stats cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_creatinine_75th_percentile_by_race AS
SELECT
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "creatinine_mg_dl") AS "race_75th_percentile_creatinine"
FROM temp_stats_cohort_valid_data
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 2.12: Exclude participants violating the WWI constraint
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_wwi_raw_all twwi ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = twwi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_mean_wwi_by_gender mwwi ON f."gender" = mwwi."gender"
JOIN temp_creatinine_75th_percentile_by_race pcr ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = pcr."race_hispanic_origin"
WHERE NOT (
    twwi."wwi_value" < mwwi."mean_wwi_gender"
    AND f."creatinine_mg_dl" > pcr."race_75th_percentile_creatinine"
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic Origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Mexican American', 'Non-Hispanic Black', 'Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'non white'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at Screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (WWI) ratio (quartiles)
-- Calculate WWI for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for WWI ratio within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_pred_wwi_raw;

-- Assign WWI ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_quartiles AS
SELECT
    tpr."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN tpr."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
        WHEN tpr."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_pred_wwi_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
        WHEN tpr."wwi_ratio" > (SELECT q1 FROM temp_pred_wwi_cutoffs) AND tpr."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_pred_wwi_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
        WHEN tpr."wwi_ratio" > (SELECT q2 FROM temp_pred_wwi_cutoffs) AND tpr."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_pred_wwi_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
        ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
    END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_pred_wwi_raw tpr;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of Kidney Disease
-- Calculate eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_egfr_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender",
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    f."creatinine_mg_dl",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening",
    CASE
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL AND f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- Calculate UACR (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN (e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" < 60) OR (u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30) THEN '1'
        ELSE '0'
    END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_egfr_raw e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_uacr_raw u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  o."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_pred_wwi_quartiles p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_kidney_disease o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing responses to the adult food security questionnaire or any of the nine depression questions, "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself",  "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead", only if the count of each missing depression items exceeds the 75th percentile of missing-item counts within their age group (defined as 20-49, 50-64, 65 or more years).
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the outcome is relative depression status, defined as 1 if the participant total depression score from the nine questions is greater than or equal to the 60th percentile of depression scores within their age group (stratified by age at screening <50 or >=50), and 0 otherwise; missing responses are assigned a value of 0 prior to score calculation.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following:
    - doctor told you have diabetes = "Yes" OR "Borderline"
    - reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
    - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing responses to the adult food security questionnaire
- count of missing responses to the nine depression questions exceeds the 75th percentile of missing-item counts within their age group (defined as 20-49, 50-64, 65 or more years). The nine depression questions are:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - "severe food insecurity" if adult food security question response is "very low food security" or "low food security"
    - "mild food insecurity" if adult food security question response is "marginal food security"
    - "food secure" if adult food security question response is "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome (relative depression status) = 1 if total depression score is >= 60th percentile of depression scores within their age group; 0 otherwise
  Calculation of total depression score:
    - For each of the nine depression questions ("have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead"):
        - Not at all = 0
        - Several days = 1
        - More than half the days = 2
        - Nearly every day = 3
        - If value is null, assign 0
    - Total depression score = sum of scores from the nine questions
  Age groups for stratification:
    - age at screening < 50
    - age at screening >= 50
