SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes
-- Diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following:
-- - doctor told you have diabetes = 'Yes' or 'Borderline'
-- - reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = 'Yes'
-- - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
    OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
    OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing responses to the adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants if the count of missing depression items exceeds the 75th percentile of missing-item counts within their age group.

-- STEP 2.2.1: Calculate missing indicators for each depression question
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_missing_indicators AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening",
    CASE WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS missing_q1,
    CASE WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS missing_q2,
    CASE WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS missing_q3,
    CASE WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS missing_q4,
    CASE WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS missing_q5,
    CASE WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS missing_q6,
    CASE WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS missing_q7,
    CASE WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS missing_q8,
    CASE WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS missing_q9
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 2.2.2: Calculate total missing depression items count and assign age group for exclusion
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_missing_depression_counts AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (missing_q1 + missing_q2 + missing_q3 + missing_q4 + missing_q5 + missing_q6 + missing_q7 + missing_q8 + missing_q9) AS "missing_depression_items_count",
    CASE
        WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20 AND "age_in_years_at_screening" <= 49 THEN '20-49'
        WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 50 AND "age_in_years_at_screening" <= 64 THEN '50-64'
        WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 65 THEN '65 or more years'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_group_for_exclusion"
FROM temp_depression_missing_indicators;

-- STEP 2.2.3: Calculate 75th percentile of missing-item counts within each age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_missing_counts_percentiles AS
SELECT
    "age_group_for_exclusion",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "missing_depression_items_count") AS p75_missing_count
FROM temp_missing_depression_counts
WHERE "age_group_for_exclusion" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "age_group_for_exclusion";

-- STEP 2.2.4: Exclude participants whose missing count exceeds the 75th percentile
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT m."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_missing_depression_counts m
JOIN temp_missing_counts_percentiles p ON m."age_group_for_exclusion" = p."age_group_for_exclusion"
WHERE m."missing_depression_items_count" <= p.p75_missing_count;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: depression outcome (relative depression status)

-- STEP 4.5.1: Calculate scores for each depression question, assigning 0 for NULLs
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_question_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening",
    CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 -- Assign 0 for NULLs as per instructions
    END AS score_q1,
    CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_q2,
    CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_q3,
    CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_q4,
    CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_q5,
    CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_q6,
    CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_q7,
    CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_q8,
    CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_q9
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.2: Calculate total depression score and assign age group for outcome stratification
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_scores AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    ("score_q1" + "score_q2" + "score_q3" + "score_q4" + "score_q5" + "score_q6" + "score_q7" + "score_q8" + "score_q9") AS "total_depression_score",
    CASE
        WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" < 50 THEN 'age at screening < 50'
        WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 50 THEN 'age at screening >= 50'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_group_for_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_question_scores;

-- STEP 4.5.3: Calculate 60th percentile of total depression scores within each outcome age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_percentiles AS
SELECT
    "age_group_for_outcome",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.60) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "total_depression_score") AS p60_depression_score
FROM temp_total_depression_scores
WHERE "age_group_for_outcome" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "age_group_for_outcome";

-- STEP 4.5.4: Determine depression outcome (relative depression status)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    t."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN t."total_depression_score" >= p.p60_depression_score THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "depression_outcome_relative_depression_status"
FROM temp_total_depression_scores t
JOIN temp_depression_score_percentiles p ON t."age_group_for_outcome" = p."age_group_for_outcome";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  gender."gender",
  race."race_hispanic_origin",
  food_insec."food_insecurity_level",
  dep_out."depression_outcome_relative_depression_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender gender ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = gender."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin race ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = race."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level food_insec ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = food_insec."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome dep_out ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dep_out."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The primary predictor will be serum albumin index ABC, defined by first dividing serum albumin (g/dL) by the mean serum albumin within the participants race/Hispanic origin group calculated from the final analytic cohort, and then dividing that value by the median of the race/Hispanic origin group's serum albumin values within the participants gender group, resulting in a single continuous predictor.
 The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for yes and 0 for no.
 We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/Hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age in years at screening >= 30
- complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
- complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing serum albumin

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycohemoglobin (hba1c) (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude patients if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude patients with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
    f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND
    (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5)
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic Origin (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Serum Albumin Index ABC (Predictor)
-- Get raw albumin, race, and gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_albumin_raw_data AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl",
       f."race_hispanic_origin",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate mean serum albumin (g/dL) within participant's race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_albumin_by_race AS
SELECT "race_hispanic_origin",
       AVG("albumin_g_dl") AS "mean_albumin"
FROM temp_albumin_raw_data
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- Calculate median serum albumin (g/dL) within participant's race/Hispanic origin and gender group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_albumin_by_race_gender AS
SELECT "race_hispanic_origin",
       "gender",
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS "median_albumin"
FROM temp_albumin_raw_data
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin", "gender";

-- Calculate serum albumin index ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_index_abc AS
SELECT ard."respondent_sequence_number",
       (ard."albumin_g_dl" / mabr."mean_albumin") / mmbrg."median_albumin" AS "serum_albumin_index_abc"
FROM temp_albumin_raw_data ard
JOIN temp_mean_albumin_by_race mabr
  ON ard."race_hispanic_origin" = mabr."race_hispanic_origin"
JOIN temp_median_albumin_by_race_gender mmbrg
  ON ard."race_hispanic_origin" = mmbrg."race_hispanic_origin" AND ard."gender" = mmbrg."gender";

-- 4.5 Diabetic Retinopathy (Outcome)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tsai."serum_albumin_index_abc",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_serum_albumin_index_abc tsai ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsai."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75.
 The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for yes and 0 for no.
 We will adjust for gender, age in years at screening, race/Hispanic origin, and two covariates: (1) glycohemoglobin HbA1c deviation covariate defined as the participants glycoprotein HbA1c (%) minus the mean HbA1c (%) within their gender group, and (2) a diabetes-duration deviation covariate defined as (age in years at screening minus age when first told the patient had diabetes) minus the mean of this duration within their race/Hispanic origin group.

Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age in years at screening >= 30

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing serum albumin (g/dL)
- missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
- missing diabetic retinopathy status
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing age in years at screening
- missing age when first told diabetes
- age in years at screening < 30
- age when first told the patient had diabetes < 30
- "Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?" = "borderline" AND (missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) < 6.5%)

Covariates:
- gender
- age in years at screening
- race/Hispanic origin
- glycohemoglobin HbA1c deviation covariate calculated as:
  glycoprotein HbA1c (%) - mean(glycoprotein HbA1c (%) by gender group)
- diabetes-duration deviation covariate calculated as:
  (age in years at screening - age when first told the patient had diabetes) - mean((age in years at screening - age when first told the patient had diabetes) by race/Hispanic origin group)

Predictor:
- serum albumin (g/dL) categorized into quartiles:
  Q1: <25%
  Q2: ≥25% and <50%
  Q3: ≥50% and <75%
  Q4: ≥75%

Outcome:
- diabetic retinopathy = 1 if "diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?" = "yes"; 0 if "diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?" = "no"

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening (years)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told diabetes (years)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
-- Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline"
-- AND either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value OR an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
    f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
    AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5)
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening_years"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Glycohemoglobin HbA1c deviation covariate
-- Calculate mean glycohemoglobin by gender group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_glycohemoglobin_by_gender AS
SELECT f."gender",
       AVG(f."glycohemoglobin") AS mean_glycohemoglobin
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."gender";

-- Calculate glycohemoglobin HbA1c deviation covariate
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_glycohemoglobin_hba1c_deviation_covariate AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."glycohemoglobin" - mg."mean_glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin_hba1c_deviation_covariate"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_mean_glycohemoglobin_by_gender mg ON f."gender" = mg."gender";

-- 4.5 Diabetes-duration deviation covariate
-- Calculate diabetes duration
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" - f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS diabetes_duration
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate mean diabetes duration by race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_diabetes_duration_by_race AS
SELECT f."race_hispanic_origin",
       AVG(dd.diabetes_duration) AS mean_diabetes_duration
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetes_duration dd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dd."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Calculate diabetes-duration deviation covariate
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration_deviation_covariate AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       dd.diabetes_duration - mdr.mean_diabetes_duration AS "diabetes_duration_deviation_covariate"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_diabetes_duration dd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dd."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_mean_diabetes_duration_by_race mdr ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = mdr."race_hispanic_origin";

-- 4.6 Serum albumin (g/dl) - Predictor
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >=25% and <50%'
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >=50% and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >=75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.7 Diabetic retinopathy - Outcome
-- Map diabetic retinopathy status to 1/0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
    WHEN 'Yes' THEN 1
    WHEN 'No' THEN 0
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening_years",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  gh."glycohemoglobin_hba1c_deviation_covariate",
  ddd."diabetes_duration_deviation_covariate",
  saq."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  dr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_glycohemoglobin_hba1c_deviation_covariate gh ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = gh."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetes_duration_deviation_covariate ddd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ddd."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles saq ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = saq."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy dr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75.
 The outcome variable will be binary diabetic retinopathy status coded as 1 only if diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy is yes and glycoprotein HbA1c (%) is 7.0 or greater, and coded as 0 only if diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy is no and glycoprotein HbA1c (%) is less than 7.0; all other combinations will be coded as 0.
 We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/Hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age in years at screening >= 30
- complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
- complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing serum albumin
- missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
- missing diabetic retinopathy status
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing age at screening
- missing age when first told diabetes
- age in years at screening < 30
- age when first told the patient had diabetes < 30
- "Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?" = "borderline" AND (glycoprotein HbA1c (%) is missing OR glycoprotein HbA1c (%) < 6.5%)

Covariates:
- gender
- age in years at screening
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
    - White
    - Black
    - Mexican American
    - Other

Predictor:
- serum albumin (g/dL) categorized into quartiles:
    - Q1: <25%
    - Q2: ≥25% and <50%
    - Q3: ≥50% and <75%
    - Q4: ≥75%

Outcome:
- diabetic retinopathy status = 1 if diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy = "yes" AND glycoprotein HbA1c (%) >= 7.0
- diabetic retinopathy status = 0 if diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy = "no" AND glycoprotein HbA1c (%) < 7.0
- diabetic retinopathy status = 0 for all other combinations

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients with age in years at screening >= 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with non-missing serum albumin (g/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with non-missing diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients with age in years at screening < 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude patients with age when first told the patient had diabetes < 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude patients with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10_hba1c_or_condition AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5) AS "hba1c_or_condition"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10_diabetes_status_exclusion_criteria AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND h."hba1c_or_condition") AS "diabetes_status_exclusion_criteria"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_exclusion_step10_hba1c_or_condition h ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = h."respondent_sequence_number";

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN temp_exclusion_step10_diabetes_status_exclusion_criteria dsec ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = dsec."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE dsec."diabetes_status_exclusion_criteria" IS FALSE;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening
-- Extract age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Map race/hispanic origin to specified categories
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Hispanic' THEN 'other'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial' THEN 'other'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Serum albumin (g/dl)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5)  WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25% and <50%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50% and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- 4.5 Diabetic retinopathy status
-- Derive is_dr_positive
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_dr_is_dr_positive AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' AND f."glycohemoglobin" >= 7.0) AS "is_dr_positive"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Derive is_dr_negative_explicit
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_dr_is_dr_negative_explicit AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' AND f."glycohemoglobin" < 7.0) AS "is_dr_negative_explicit"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Assign diabetic retinopathy status_intermediate
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_dr_intermediate AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN p."is_dr_positive" IS TRUE THEN 1
    WHEN n."is_dr_negative_explicit" IS TRUE THEN 0
    ELSE 0
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy_status_intermediate"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_dr_is_dr_positive p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_dr_is_dr_negative_explicit n ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = n."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Final mapping for diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy_status AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE "diabetic_retinopathy_status_intermediate"
    WHEN 1 THEN '1'
    WHEN 0 THEN '0'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy_status"
FROM temp_dr_intermediate;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  sa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  dr."diabetic_retinopathy_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy_status dr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75.
 The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for yes and 0 for no.
 The analysis will adjust for gender, race/Hispanic origin categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other, and age in years at screening. Age in years at screening will be incorporated both as a continuous variable and as a hierarchical indicator distinguishing participants who are not younger than 60 years from those younger than 60 years. In addition, the model will adjust for two covariates that are defined only for participants who remain in the final analytic cohort: first, an age-group standardized glycoprotein HbA1c (%) deviation, defined as the participants HbA1c (%) minus the mean HbA1c (%) of participants in the same age group; and second, a race/Hispanic origin standardized diabetes-duration deviation, defined as the participants diabetes duration (age at screening minus age when first told the patient had diabetes) minus the mean diabetes duration of participants in the same race/Hispanic origin group.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing serum albumin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5));

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.0 Derive age_group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_derived_age_group AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" BETWEEN 20 AND 39 THEN '20-39'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" BETWEEN 40 AND 59 THEN '40-59'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '60+'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_group"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Race/Hispanic Origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to 'white', 'black', 'mexican american', 'other'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age in years at screening (continuous)
-- Extract age in years at screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Age in years at screening (binary indicator)
-- Create binary indicator for age >= 60
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening_binary_indicator AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '1'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '0'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_in_years_at_screening_binary_indicator"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Age-group standardized glycoprotein HbA1c (%%) deviation
-- Calculate mean HbA1c by age group within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_mean_by_age_group AS
SELECT tag."age_group",
       AVG(f."glycohemoglobin") AS mean_hba1c
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_derived_age_group tag ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tag."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY tag."age_group";

-- Calculate age-group standardized glycoprotein HbA1c (%%) deviation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c_deviation AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."glycohemoglobin" - m.mean_hba1c AS "age_group_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c_deviation"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_derived_age_group tag ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tag."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_hba1c_mean_by_age_group m ON tag."age_group" = m."age_group";

-- 4.6 Race/Hispanic origin standardized diabetes-duration deviation
-- Calculate diabetes duration for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin",
       (f."age_in_years_at_screening" - f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes") AS "diabetes_duration"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate mean diabetes duration by race/Hispanic origin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration_mean_by_race AS
SELECT "race_hispanic_origin",
       AVG("diabetes_duration") AS mean_diabetes_duration
FROM temp_diabetes_duration
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- Calculate race/Hispanic origin standardized diabetes-duration deviation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin_standardized_diabetes_duration_deviation AS
SELECT td."respondent_sequence_number",
       td."diabetes_duration" - tm.mean_diabetes_duration AS "race_hispanic_origin_standardized_diabetes_duration_deviation"
FROM temp_diabetes_duration td
LEFT JOIN temp_diabetes_duration_mean_by_race tm ON td."race_hispanic_origin" = tm."race_hispanic_origin";

-- 4.7 Serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Extract serum albumin for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5)  WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_quartiles AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25% and <50%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50% and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_raw;

-- 4.8 Diabetic retinopathy
-- Map diabetic retinopathy to binary (1 for yes, 0 for no)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN '1'
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN '0'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tabi."age_in_years_at_screening_binary_indicator",
  tashd."age_group_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c_deviation",
  trhodd."race_hispanic_origin_standardized_diabetes_duration_deviation",
  tsaq."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening_binary_indicator tabi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tabi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_group_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c_deviation tashd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tashd."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin_standardized_diabetes_duration_deviation trhodd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trhodd."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_quartiles tsaq ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsaq."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age in years at screening (continuous)
-- Extract age in years at screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Age in years at screening (binary indicator)
-- Create binary indicator for age >= 60
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening_binary_indicator AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '1'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '0'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_in_years_at_screening_binary_indicator"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Age-group standardized glycoprotein HbA1c (%%) deviation
-- Calculate mean HbA1c by age group within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_mean_by_age_group AS
SELECT tag."age_group",
       AVG(f."glycohemoglobin") AS mean_hba1c
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_derived_age_group tag ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tag."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY tag."age_group";

-- Calculate age-group standardized glycoprotein HbA1c (%%) deviation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c_deviation AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."glycohemoglobin" - m.mean_hba1c AS "age_group_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c_deviation"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_derived_age_group tag ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tag."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_hba1c_mean_by_age_group m ON tag."age_group" = m."age_group";

-- 4.6 Race/Hispanic origin standardized diabetes-duration deviation
-- Calculate diabetes duration for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin",
       (f."age_in_years_at_screening" - f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes") AS "diabetes_duration"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate mean diabetes duration by race/Hispanic origin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration_mean_by_race AS
SELECT "race_hispanic_origin",
       AVG("diabetes_duration") AS mean_diabetes_duration
FROM temp_diabetes_duration
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- Calculate race/Hispanic origin standardized diabetes-duration deviation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin_standardized_diabetes_duration_deviation AS
SELECT td."respondent_sequence_number",
       td."diabetes_duration" - tm.mean_diabetes_duration AS "race_hispanic_origin_standardized_diabetes_duration_deviation"
FROM temp_diabetes_duration td
LEFT JOIN temp_diabetes_duration_mean_by_race tm ON td."race_hispanic_origin" = tm."race_hispanic_origin";

-- 4.7 Serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Extract serum albumin for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5)  WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_quartiles AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25% and <50%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50% and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_raw;

-- 4.8 Diabetic retinopathy
-- Map diabetic retinopathy to binary (1 for yes, 0 for no)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN '1'
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN '0'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tabi."age_in_years_at_screening_binary_indicator",
  tashd."age_group_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c_deviation",
  trhodd."race_hispanic_origin_standardized_diabetes_duration_deviation",
  tsaq."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening_binary_indicator tabi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tabi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_group_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c_deviation tashd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tashd."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin_standardized_diabetes_duration_deviation trhodd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trhodd."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_g_dl_quartiles tsaq ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsaq."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome is coded as 1 only if the participant satisfies both of the following benchmark-dependent conditions. First, the participants UACR (mg/g), calculated as 100 times urine albumin (mg/L) divided by urine creatinine (mg/dL), must be greater than or equal to the age-group specific 70th percentile of UACR calculated separately among participants aged less than 60 years and those aged 60 years or older who are in the cohort. Among participants meeting this albuminuria condition, the participants ratio of serum creatinine (mg/dL) to age at screening must be greater than or equal to the race/Hispanic origin specific median of this ratio, calculated from the same cohort. Participants who do not satisfy both conditions are coded as 0.
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR
- fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L OR
- glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5% OR
- takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar

Exclusion Criteria:
- age at screening < 20
- pregnant at the exam
- missing weight (kg)
- missing waist circumference (cm)
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing urine albumin (mg/L)
- missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
- missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)

Covariates:
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
  white,
  non white
- gender
- age at screening categorized as:
  <60,
  >=60

Predictor:
- weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio calculated as:
  waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight (kg))
  categorized as quartiles:
    Q1: <=25%
    Q2: >25% and <=50%
    Q3: >50% and <=75%
    Q4: >75%

Outcome:
- outcome = 1 if (Condition 1 AND Condition 2) is met; 0 otherwise
  Condition 1: UACR (mg/g) >= age-group specific 70th percentile of UACR
    UACR (mg/g) calculated as:
      100 * urine albumin (mg/L) / urine creatinine (mg/dL)
    Age-group specific 70th percentile of UACR calculated separately within the cohort for:
      age at screening < 60 years,
      age at screening >= 60 years
  AND
  Condition 2: ratio of serum creatinine (mg/dL) to age at screening >= race/Hispanic origin specific median of this ratio
    ratio of serum creatinine (mg/dL) to age at screening calculated as:
      serum creatinine (mg/dL) / age at screening
    Race/Hispanic origin specific median of this ratio calculated within the cohort for each race/Hispanic origin group.
