-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who reported 'Yes' to doctor told you have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.2: Include participants with fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0;

-- STEP 1.3: Include participants with glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5%
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5;

-- STEP 1.4: Include participants who take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step4 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- Combine all inclusion criteria using UNION
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step1
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step2
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step3
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step4;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL OR f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who are pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" != 'Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam';

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to 'white' and 'non white'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    ELSE 'non white'
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
-- Extract gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: age at screening
-- Bin age at screening into '<60' and '>=60'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign wwi ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: outcome
-- Step 1: Calculate uacr (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" > 0;

-- Step 2: Calculate age-group specific 70th percentile of UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group_uacr_70th_percentile AS
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
    ELSE '>=60'
  END AS "age_group",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.70) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "uacr_mg_g") AS "uacr_70th_percentile"
FROM temp_uacr_raw
GROUP BY "age_group";

-- Step 3: Condition 1: UACR (mg/g) >= age-group specific 70th percentile of UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_condition1 AS
SELECT u."respondent_sequence_number",
       u."uacr_mg_g",
       u."age_in_years_at_screening",
       (u."uacr_mg_g" >= ag."uacr_70th_percentile") AS "condition1_met"
FROM temp_uacr_raw u
JOIN temp_age_group_uacr_70th_percentile ag
  ON (u."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 AND ag."age_group" = '<60')
  OR (u."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 AND ag."age_group" = '>=60');

-- Step 4: Calculate ratio of serum creatinine (mg/dL) to age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_creatinine_age_ratio_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."creatinine_mg_dl" / f."age_in_years_at_screening") AS "serum_creatinine_age_ratio",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 0;

-- Step 5: Calculate Race/Hispanic origin specific median of this ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_group_serum_creatinine_age_ratio_median AS
SELECT
  "race_hispanic_origin",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_creatinine_age_ratio") AS "ratio_median"
FROM temp_serum_creatinine_age_ratio_raw
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 6: Condition 2: ratio of serum creatinine (mg/dL) to age at screening >= race/Hispanic origin specific median of this ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_condition2 AS
SELECT r."respondent_sequence_number",
       r."serum_creatinine_age_ratio",
       (r."serum_creatinine_age_ratio" >= rm."ratio_median") AS "condition2_met"
FROM temp_serum_creatinine_age_ratio_raw r
JOIN temp_race_group_serum_creatinine_age_ratio_median rm
  ON r."race_hispanic_origin" = rm."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 7: Combine conditions for outcome_boolean
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_boolean AS
SELECT c1."respondent_sequence_number",
       (c1."condition1_met" AND c2."condition2_met") AS "outcome_boolean"
FROM temp_condition1 c1
JOIN temp_condition2 c2 ON c1."respondent_sequence_number" = c2."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 8: Map outcome_boolean to '1' or '0'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome AS
SELECT ob."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN ob."outcome_boolean" IS TRUE THEN '1'
    WHEN ob."outcome_boolean" IS FALSE THEN '0'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "outcome"
FROM temp_outcome_boolean ob;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  tg."gender",
  taas."age_at_screening",
  twwir."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  to_."outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening taas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = taas."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio twwir ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = twwir."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome to_ ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = to_."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing serum albumin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
    f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
    AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5)
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Serum albumin (g/dL) categorized into quartiles
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25% and <50%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50% and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Diabetic retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
           ELSE 0 -- Participants with any response other than yes or no will be 0
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tsa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles tsa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

 The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75.
 The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for yes and 0 for no.
 We will adjust for gender, race/Hispanic origin categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other, age in years at screening included as a continuous variable and as an indicator for age 60 or older, and a diabetes-duration covariate calculated as age in years at screening minus age when first told the patient had diabetes, with diabetes duration truncated to a maximum of 40 years and participants with negative duration excluded.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
-- Derive glycohemoglobin_condition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_glycohemoglobin_condition AS
SELECT f."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5) AS "glycohemoglobin_condition"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step9 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Derive uncertain_diabetes_status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uncertain_diabetes_status AS
SELECT f."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND tgc."glycohemoglobin_condition") AS "uncertain_diabetes_status"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step9 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_glycohemoglobin_condition tgc ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = tgc."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Exclude if uncertain_diabetes_status is TRUE
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN temp_uncertain_diabetes_status uds ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = uds."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE uds."uncertain_diabetes_status" = FALSE;

-- STEP 2.11: Exclude participants with negative diabetes duration
-- Derive diabetes-duration (years)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration_raw_for_exclusion AS
SELECT f."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."age_in_years_at_screening" - f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes") AS "diabetes_duration_years"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step10 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Exclude if diabetes_duration_years < 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step11 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10 i
JOIN temp_diabetes_duration_raw_for_exclusion ddr ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = ddr."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE ddr."diabetes_duration_years" >= 0;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step11;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Race/Hispanic Origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to 'White', 'Black', 'Mexican American', 'Other'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'White'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'Black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'Mexican American'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'Other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age in years at screening (years)
-- Extract age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Age 60 or older
-- Map age in years at screening to 1 if >= 60, else 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_60_or_older AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "age_60_or_older"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Diabetes-duration (years)
-- Calculate diabetes-duration (years) = age in years at screening - age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."age_in_years_at_screening" - f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes") AS "diabetes_duration_years"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Truncate diabetes-duration (years) to a maximum of 40 years
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration_years AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       LEAST("diabetes_duration_years", 40) AS "diabetes_duration_years"
FROM temp_diabetes_duration_raw;

-- 4.6 Serum Albumin (g/dL) - Predictor
-- Extract serum albumin (g/dL) for cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25th percentile'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25th percentile and <50th percentile'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50th percentile and <75th percentile'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75th percentile'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- 4.7 Diabetic Retinopathy - Outcome
-- Map diabetic retinopathy status to 1 for 'Yes', 0 for 'No'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tao."age_60_or_older",
  tdd."diabetes_duration_years",
  tsa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_60_or_older tao ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tao."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_diabetes_duration_years tdd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdd."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_serum_albumin_g_dl tsa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsa."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Calculate gender-specific median fasting plasma glucose
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_median_glucose AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "fasting_glucose_mg_dl") AS "median_glucose"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND "gender" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "gender";

-- STEP 1.2: Apply complex inclusion criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT f."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_gender_median_glucose g ON f."gender" = g."gender"
WHERE
    -- Condition 1: Not low-risk by primary definition (fasting glucose >= gender-specific median)
    (f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= g."median_glucose")
    OR
    -- Condition 2: Low-risk by primary definition, but not simultaneously 'never told diabetes' AND 'no thoughts of being better off dead'
    (
        f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" < g."median_glucose"
        AND
        (
            f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IS NULL OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" != 'No'
            OR
            f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NULL OR f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" != 'Not at all'
        )
    );

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security category
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing "have little interest in doing things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"

-- 4.5.10 Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    si."score_interest" + sd."score_depressed" + ss."score_sleep" + se."score_energy" + sa."score_appetite" +
    ssb."score_self_bad" + sc."score_concentrating" + sm."score_movement" + sde."score_dead" AS "total_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_score_interest si ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = si."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_depressed sd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sd."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_sleep ss ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ss."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_energy se ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = se."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_appetite sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"

JOIN temp_score_movement sm ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sm."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_dead sde ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sde."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5.11 Determine depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS

    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1

FROM temp_total_depression_score;

LEFT JOIN temp_depression_outcome d_o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = d_o."respondent_sequence_number";

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Consider race/Hispanic origin, gender, and age at screening, and additionally adjust for a renal-age scaling covariate defined as serum creatinine (mg/dL) divided by age at screening, then multiplied by 1.2 if urine albumin (mg/L) is greater than or equal to 30 and multiplied by 1.0 otherwise.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who reported 'Yes' to "doctor told you have diabetes"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_diabetes_reported AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.2: Include participants with fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_fpg_high AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0;

-- STEP 1.3: Include participants with glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5%
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_hba1c_high AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5;

-- STEP 1.4: Include participants who take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_diabetic_pills AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.5: Combine all inclusion criteria using OR logic
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_diabetes_reported
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_fpg_high
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_hba1c_high
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_diabetic_pills;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Race/Hispanic Origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Renal-age scaling covariate
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_renal_age_scaling_covariate AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."albumin_urine_mg_l" >= 30 THEN (f."creatinine_mg_dl" / f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.2
           WHEN f."albumin_urine_mg_l" < 30 THEN (f."creatinine_mg_dl" / f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "renal_age_scaling_covariate"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw wwi ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign wwi ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25 and <=50%'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50 and <=75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.6 Presence of kidney disease
-- STEP 4.6.1: Calculate eGFR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POW(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POW(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POW(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POW(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POW(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POW(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POW(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POW(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POW(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POW(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POW(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POW(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.6.2: Calculate Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.6.3: Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m2" < 60 OR u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_egfr_raw e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_uacr_raw u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tro."race_hispanic_origin",
  tg."gender",
  tas."age_at_screening",
  trasc."renal_age_scaling_covariate",
  twwir."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  tpkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tro ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tro."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_renal_age_scaling_covariate trasc ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trasc."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio twwir ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = twwir."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease tpkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tpkd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor in the analysis will be gender-based serum albumin (g/dL), which is calculated as  serum albumin (g/dL)*2 for participants whose gender is female and serum albumin (g/dL)*3 for participants whose gender is male.
 The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for yes and 0 for no.
 We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/Hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

- age in years at screening >= 30

- missing serum albumin
- missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
- missing diabetic retinopathy status
- missing gender

- missing age at screening
- missing age when first told diabetes
- age in years at screening < 30
- age when first told the patient had diabetes < 30
- uncertain diabetes status, defined as:
    "Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?" = "borderline" AND (glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) is missing OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) < 6.5%)

Covariates:
- gender
- age in years at screening
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
    White,
    Black,
    Mexican American,
    Other

Predictor:
- gender-based serum albumin (g/dL) calculated as:
    If gender = female: serum albumin (g/dL) * 2
    If gender = male: serum albumin (g/dL) * 3

Outcome:
- diabetic retinopathy = 1 if "diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?" = "yes"; 0 if "diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?" = "no"

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing age when first told the patient had diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
-- First, determine hba1c_or_condition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_or_condition AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5) AS "hba1c_or_condition"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step9 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Then, determine uncertain diabetes status and exclude
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_hba1c_or_condition h ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = h."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND h."hba1c_or_condition");

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening
-- Extract age in years at screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to 'white', 'black', 'mexican american', 'other'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Gender-based serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Calculate gender-based serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_based_serum_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' THEN f."albumin_g_dl" * 2.0
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' THEN f."albumin_g_dl" * 3.0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "gender_based_serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Diabetic retinopathy
-- Map diabetic retinopathy to 1 for 'Yes' and 0 for 'No'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tgs."gender_based_serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender_based_serum_albumin_g_dl tgs ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tgs."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 To be included, participants must be at least 20 years old at screening and have a fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) greater than the gender specific mean, calculated among participants with non-missing fasting plasma glucose and gender, and must additionally report at least two physical symptoms, defined as responses other than "Not at all" to feeling tired, poor appetite or overeating, or moving or speaking slowly or too fast.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age in years at screening >= 20
- fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) > gender specific mean of fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) (calculated among participants with non-missing fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) and gender)
- at least two physical symptoms reported (response other than "Not at all" to "feeling tired", "poor appetite or overeating", or "moving or speaking slowly or too fast")

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead."

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: adult food security question = "very low food security" OR "low food security"
    - mild food insecurity: adult food security question = "marginal food security"
    - food secure: adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  total score calculated by summing the mapped values of the following nine questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead."
  Each question's value is mapped as:
    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
    - If value is null: 0

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate gender specific mean of fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) among participants with non-missing fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) and gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_specific_fasting_glucose_mean AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    AVG("fasting_glucose_mg_dl") AS "gender_mean_fasting_glucose_mg_dl"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND "gender" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "gender";

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those who have a fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) greater than the gender specific mean
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender_specific_fasting_glucose_mean gsm ON f."gender" = gsm."gender"
WHERE f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."gender" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > gsm."gender_mean_fasting_glucose_mg_dl";

-- STEP 1.4: Calculate indicators for physical symptoms
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_physical_symptom_indicators AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" != 'Not at all' THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "feeling_tired_indicator",
    CASE
        WHEN "poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN "poor_appetite_or_overeating" != 'Not at all' THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "poor_appetite_or_overeating_indicator",
    CASE
        WHEN "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" != 'Not at all' THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_indicator"
FROM final_master_table;

-- STEP 1.5: Calculate sum of physical symptoms
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_sum_of_physical_symptoms AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    ("feeling_tired_indicator" + "poor_appetite_or_overeating_indicator" + "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_indicator") AS "sum_of_symptoms"
FROM temp_physical_symptom_indicators;

-- STEP 1.6: From the above group, include only those who report at least two physical symptoms
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN temp_sum_of_physical_symptoms s ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = s."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE s."sum_of_symptoms" >= 2;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Identify participants with any missing depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_any_depression_question_missing AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (
        ("have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NULL)::INT
    ) AS "num_missing_depression_questions"
FROM final_master_table;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with any missing depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN temp_any_depression_question_missing dqm ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = dqm."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE dqm."num_missing_depression_questions" = 0;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE f."adult_food_security_category"
        WHEN 'AD very low food security: 6-10' THEN 'severe food insecurity'
        WHEN 'AD low food security: 3-5' THEN 'severe food insecurity'
        WHEN 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
        WHEN 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: depression outcome
-- Map each depression question response to a numerical score (0-3, NULL to 0)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "poor_appetite_or_overeating_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "feeling_bad_about_yourself_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "trouble_concentrating_on_things_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    ("have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score" +
     "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score" +
     "trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score" +
     "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score" +
     "poor_appetite_or_overeating_score" +
     "feeling_bad_about_yourself_score" +
     "trouble_concentrating_on_things_score" +
     "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score" +
     "thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Assign depression outcome based on total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fil."food_insecurity_level",
  tdo."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fil ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fil."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome tdo ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdo."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;
