        ("feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NULL)::INT +
        ("thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NULL)::INT
    ) AS "num_missing_depression_questions"
FROM final_master_table;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with any missing depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN temp_any_depression_question_missing dqm ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = dqm."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE dqm."num_missing_depression_questions" = 0;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE f."adult_food_security_category"
        WHEN 'AD very low food security: 6-10' THEN 'severe food insecurity'
        WHEN 'AD low food security: 3-5' THEN 'severe food insecurity'
        WHEN 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
        WHEN 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: depression outcome
-- Map each depression question response to a numerical score (0-3, NULL to 0)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "poor_appetite_or_overeating_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "feeling_bad_about_yourself_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "trouble_concentrating_on_things_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    ("have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score" +
     "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score" +
     "trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score" +
     "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score" +
     "poor_appetite_or_overeating_score" +
     "feeling_bad_about_yourself_score" +
     "trouble_concentrating_on_things_score" +
     "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score" +
     "thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Assign depression outcome based on total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fil."food_insecurity_level",
  tdo."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fil ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fil."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome tdo ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdo."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75.
 The outcome variable will be a retinopathy risk status coded as 1 only if the participant is not in the protected state; the protected state is true if it is not false that the participant cannot be determined to not be low-risk, where low-risk is defined as either answering No to diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy or having glycoprotein HbA1c (%) not greater than the median HbA1c (%) within their race/Hispanic origin group within the analytic cohort; however, even if a participant would otherwise be coded as 1, they are immediately coded as 0 unless it is simultaneously true that they answered yes to doctor told you have diabetes and their age in years at screening is not less than 45.
 We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/Hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

  If retinopathy risk status was initially calculated as 1:
    If NOT ( (Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes? = "Yes") AND (age in years at screening >= 45) ):
      retinopathy risk status = 0

-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age in years at screening 30 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with non-missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with non-missing diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients with age in years at screening less than 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude patients with age when first told diabetes less than 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude patients with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
    f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND
    (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5)
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'White'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'Black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'Mexican American'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'Other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25% and <50%'
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50% and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.5 Outcome: retinopathy risk status
-- Step 1: Calculate median glycoprotein HbA1c (%) within race/hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_hba1c_median_per_race AS
SELECT
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."glycohemoglobin") AS "group_median_hba1c"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 2: Check if glycoprotein HbA1c (%) <= group_median_hba1c
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_hba1c_vs_median AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL AND t. "group_median_hba1c" IS NOT NULL AND f."glycohemoglobin" <= t."group_median_hba1c" THEN TRUE
        WHEN f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL AND t. "group_median_hba1c" IS NOT NULL AND f."glycohemoglobin" > t."group_median_hba1c" THEN FALSE
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "hba1c_vs_median"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_hba1c_median_per_race t ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = t."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 3: Check if diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy = 'No'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_diabetes_eyes_no AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN TRUE
        WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN FALSE
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "diabetes_eyes_no"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4: Determine low_risk_status (diabetes_eyes_no OR hba1c_vs_median)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_low_risk_status AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (den."diabetes_eyes_no" OR hvm."hba1c_vs_median") AS "low_risk_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_outcome_diabetes_eyes_no den ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = den."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_hba1c_vs_median hvm ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = hvm."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 5: Map initial_retinopathy_risk_status (1 if NOT low_risk_status; 0 if low_risk_status)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_initial_retinopathy_risk_status AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "low_risk_status" IS TRUE THEN 0
        WHEN "low_risk_status" IS FALSE THEN 1
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "initial_retinopathy_risk_status"
FROM temp_outcome_low_risk_status;

-- Step 6: Check if has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes? = 'Yes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_doctor_told_diabetes_yes AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' THEN TRUE
        WHEN f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('No', 'Borderline') THEN FALSE
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "doctor_told_diabetes_yes"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 7: Check if age in years at screening >= 45
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_age_ge_45 AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 45 THEN TRUE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 45 THEN FALSE
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_ge_45"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 8: Determine override_condition_inner (doctor_told_diabetes_yes AND age_ge_45)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_override_condition_inner AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (dtdy."doctor_told_diabetes_yes" AND age45."age_ge_45") AS "override_condition_inner"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_outcome_doctor_told_diabetes_yes dtdy ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dtdy."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_age_ge_45 age45 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age45."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 9: Determine final_override_trigger (NOT override_condition_inner)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_final_override_trigger AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    NOT "override_condition_inner" AS "final_override_trigger"
FROM temp_outcome_override_condition_inner;

-- Step 10: Apply conditional assignment for retinopathy risk status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_retinopathy_risk_status AS
SELECT
    irs."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN irs."initial_retinopathy_risk_status" = 1 AND fot."final_override_trigger" IS TRUE THEN 0
        ELSE irs."initial_retinopathy_risk_status"
    END AS "retinopathy_risk_status"
FROM temp_outcome_initial_retinopathy_risk_status irs
JOIN temp_outcome_final_override_trigger fot ON irs."respondent_sequence_number" = fot."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  tas."age_in_years_at_screening",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  tsa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  trrs."retinopathy_risk_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles tsa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsa."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_retinopathy_risk_status trrs ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trrs."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 Exclude participants who had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm), gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20 years or whose serum creatinine (mg/dL) is greater than or equal to 2.0.
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).

-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who reported 'Yes' to doctor told you have diabetes OR fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5% OR takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = 'Yes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
   OR "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
   OR "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20 years
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants whose serum creatinine (mg/dL) is greater than or equal to 2.0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" < 2.0;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic Origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           ELSE 'non white'
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (WWI) ratio
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign wwi ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25 and <=50%'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50 and <=75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- Calculate eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate UACR (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0;

-- Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" < 60 OR u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tg."gender",
  ta."age_at_screening",
  tw."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  tp."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio tw ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tw."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease tp ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tp."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead. We will also exclude people whose gender is female and age at screening is less than 35.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following:
    - doctor told you have diabetes = "yes" OR "borderline"
    - reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
    - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
- gender is female AND age at screening is less than 35

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR "low food security"
    - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
    - food secure: if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score from nine questions >= 10; 0 otherwise
  Total score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question is scored as:
    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
  If a question value is null, assign 0.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude people whose gender is female and age at screening is less than 35
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 35);

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression outcome
-- Score each of the nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things,
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless,
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much,
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy,
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_poor_appetite_or_overeating,
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_feeling_bad_about_yourself,
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_trouble_concentrating_on_things,
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast,
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total score from nine questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things +
     score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless +
     score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much +
     score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy +
     score_poor_appetite_or_overeating +
     score_feeling_bad_about_yourself +
     score_trouble_concentrating_on_things +
     score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast +
     score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead) AS "total_depression_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Determine depression outcome (1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_depression_score" >= 10 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  gen."gender",
  race."race_hispanic_origin",
  fis."food_insecurity_level",
  dep."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender gen ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = gen."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin race ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = race."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fis ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fis."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome dep ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dep."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome is defined as a gender-urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 60 mg/g or greater. Gender-urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL), multiplied by 3 for whose gender is female and multiplied by 2 whose gender is male .
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- diabetes defined as meeting any of the following requirements:
    - doctor told you have diabetes = Yes
    OR
    - fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
    OR
    - glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5%
    OR
    - takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar

Exclusion Criteria:
- age at screening < 20
- pregnant at the exam
- missing weight (kg)
- missing waist circumference (cm)
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing urine albumin (mg/L)
- missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
- missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)

Covariates:
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
    - white
    - non white
- gender
- age at screening categorized as:
    - <60
    - >=60

Predictor:
- weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio calculated as:
  waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight(kg))
  categorized as quartiles:
    Q1: <=25%
    Q2: >25% and <=50%
    Q3: >50% and <=75%
    Q4: >75%

Outcome:
- gender-urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) = 1 if >= 60 mg/g; 0 otherwise
  gender-urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) calculated as:
    If gender = female:
      100 * (urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) * 3
    If gender = male:
      100 * (urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) * 2

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who meet the diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT f."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table f
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes')
   OR (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NOT NULL AND f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0)
   OR (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL AND f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5)
   OR (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes');

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who are pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" != 'Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam';

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Mexican American', 'Non-Hispanic Black', 'Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'non white'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL THEN NULL
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
           ELSE '>=60'
       END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0 AND f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL
           THEN f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw
WHERE "wwi_ratio" IS NOT NULL;

-- Assign wwi ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT r."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN r."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
           WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
           WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
           WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
           ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
       END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Gender-urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (uacr) (mg/g)
-- Calculate raw gender-urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0
           THEN 100.0 * (f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") * 3.0
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0
           THEN 100.0 * (f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") * 2.0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Map UACR to binary outcome (>= 60 mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g AS
SELECT u."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN u."uacr_mg_g" IS NULL THEN NULL
           WHEN u."uacr_mg_g" >= 60.0 THEN '1'
           ELSE '0'
       END AS "gender_urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_uacr_raw u;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  o."gender_urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender_urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants who are not in the set of individuals who cannot be determined to not have complete psychosocial data, where complete psychosocial data is defined as having a non-missing adult food security questionnaire and not failing to provide responses to each of the nine depression questions ("have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself",  "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead"); nevertheless, if a participant would otherwise be excluded for incompleteness, the participant is immediately retained unless it is simultaneously true that they are not female, are not younger than 60 years, and are not taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = "Yes" OR doctor told you have diabetes = "Borderline") OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = "Yes" OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- (adult food security questionnaire is missing OR "have little interest in doing things" is missing OR "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" is missing OR "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much" is missing OR "feeling tired or having little energy" is missing OR "poor appetite or overeating" is missing OR "feeling bad about yourself" is missing OR "trouble concentrating on things" is missing OR "moving or speaking slowly or too fast" is missing OR "thought you would be better off dead" is missing)
  AND
  (gender != "female" AND age in years at screening >= 60 AND reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = "No")

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  - severe food insecurity if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR adult food security question = "low food security"
  - mild food insecurity if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
  - food secure if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if PHQ-9 Total Score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  PHQ-9 Total Score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
  - "have little interest in doing things"
  - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
  - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
  - "feeling tired or having little energy"
  - "poor appetite or overeating"
  - "feeling bad about yourself"
  - "trouble concentrating on things"
  - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
  - "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question is scored as:
    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
  If a question's value is null, it is assigned a score of 0.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who meet diabetes inclusion criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126.0;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Determine missing_questionnaire_data for each respondent
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_missing_questionnaire_data AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    (
        f."adult_food_security_category" IS NULL OR
        f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NULL OR
        f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NULL OR
        f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NULL OR
        f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NULL OR
        f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NULL OR
        f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NULL OR
        f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NULL OR
        f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NULL OR
        f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NULL
    ) AS "missing_questionnaire_data"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_inclusion_step2 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 2.2: Determine specific_demographic_and_diabetes_status for each respondent
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_specific_demographic_and_diabetes_status AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    (
        f."gender" != 'Female' AND
        f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 AND
        f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'No'
    ) AS "specific_demographic_and_diabetes_status"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_inclusion_step2 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants based on the combined exclusion criteria
-- Exclude if (missing_questionnaire_data IS TRUE AND specific_demographic_and_diabetes_status IS TRUE)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN temp_missing_questionnaire_data mqd ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = mqd."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_specific_demographic_and_diabetes_status sdds ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = sdds."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (mqd."missing_questionnaire_data" IS TRUE AND sdds."specific_demographic_and_diabetes_status" IS TRUE);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food insecurity level (Predictor)
-- Map adult food security category to food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
        WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
        WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression outcome (Outcome)
-- Map PHQ-9 question responses to scores (0-3), assign 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_question_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy",
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things",
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate PHQ-9 Total Score = sum of scores from nine PHQ-9 questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_total_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (
        "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things" +
        "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" +
        "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" +
        "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" +
        "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating" +
        "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself" +
        "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things" +
        "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" +
        "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    ) AS "phq9_total_score"
FROM temp_phq9_question_scores;

-- depression outcome = 1 if PHQ-9 Total Score >= 10; 0 otherwise
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "phq9_total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_phq9_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  gen."gender",
  race."race_hispanic_origin",
  fi."food_insecurity_level",
  dep."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender gen ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = gen."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin race ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = race."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome dep ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dep."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 Only include individuals whose glycohemoglobin (%) level is not less than one-tenth of their current age in years at screening.
 We will not include people who did not say yes to a question doctor told you have diabetes or failed to have values for serum albumin (g/dL) or diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 The predictor is the risk level: risk-1 is when if the absence of the failure to meet poor control (glycohemoglobin Hba1c (%) greater than or equal to 7.0 (%)) is not present, and diabetes duration (age in years at screening - age when first told the patient had diabetes) is not longer than the cohort-wide 25th percentile duration, unless serum albumin (g/dl) value is not less than the 90th percentile of the distribution for their respective race/Hispanic origin group. risk-2 is if not risk-1 and gender is not prevented from being non-female and the record is not free from the absence of retinopathy (diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy = no) being present, unless age in years at screening is not prevented from being below the cohort median age. risk-3 is if not risk-1 or risk-2 and their calculated metabolic ratio (glycoprotein hba1c (%) / serum albumin (g/dl)) is not lower than the 60th percentile of the ratio distribution for their specific race/Hispanic origin group, and the absence of a diabetes diagnosis (doctor told you have diabetes = no) is not false when age in years at screening is 65 or older. risk-4: assigned if not risk-1, risk-2, or risk-3, and whose age when first told the patient had diabetes is not unknown, will not fail to be assigned risk-4.
 The outcome will be diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Consider gender and age at screening in this analysis.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- glycohemoglobin (%) >= (age in years at screening / 10)
- doctor told you have diabetes = "yes"

Exclusion Criteria:
- serum albumin (g/dL) is missing
- diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy is missing

Covariates:
- gender
- age in years at screening

Predictor:
- risk level, categorized as:
  Derivations:
    - diabetes duration = age in years at screening - age when first told the patient had diabetes
    - cohort-wide 25th percentile duration: Calculate the 25th percentile of diabetes duration among all included participants.
    - 90th percentile of serum albumin (g/dl) for race/Hispanic origin group: Calculate the 90th percentile of serum albumin (g/dl) for each race/Hispanic origin group among all included participants.
    - cohort median age: Calculate the median of age in years at screening among all included participants.
    - metabolic ratio = glycoprotein hba1c (%) / serum albumin (g/dl)
    - 60th percentile of metabolic ratio for race/Hispanic origin group: Calculate the 60th percentile of metabolic ratio for each race/Hispanic origin group among all included participants.
  Categorization:
    - Assign risk-1 if:
      Let A = glycohemoglobin Hba1c (%) >= 7.0 (%)
      Let B = diabetes duration <= cohort-wide 25th percentile duration
      Let C = serum albumin (g/dl) >= 90th percentile of serum albumin (g/dl) for race/Hispanic origin group
      ( (A AND B) AND NOT C ) OR C
    - Else, assign risk-2 if:
      Let D = NOT risk-1
      Let E = gender != "female"
      Let F = diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy = "no"
      Let G = age in years at screening < cohort median age
      D AND ( ( (E AND F) AND NOT G ) OR G )
    - Else, assign risk-3 if:
      Let H = NOT (risk-1 OR risk-2)
      Let I = metabolic ratio >= 60th percentile of metabolic ratio for race/Hispanic origin group
      Let J = (age in years at screening >= 65 AND doctor told you have diabetes = "no")
      H AND I AND J
    - Else, assign risk-4 if:
      Let K = NOT (risk-1 OR risk-2 OR risk-3)
      Let L = age when first told the patient had diabetes is not missing
      K AND L

Outcome:
- diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy mapped to:
  1 if diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy = "yes"
  0 if diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy = "no"
