SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include individuals whose glycohemoglobin (%) level is not less than one-tenth of their current age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" >= ("age_in_years_at_screening" / 10.0);

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who said 'Yes' to 'doctor told you have diabetes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Risk Level (Predictor)

-- Step 4.3.1: Calculate diabetes duration
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_duration AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" - f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS "diabetes_duration"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.3.2: Calculate cohort-wide 25th percentile duration
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_duration_25th_percentile AS
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "diabetes_duration") AS "cohort_wide_25th_percentile_duration"
FROM temp_diabetes_duration
WHERE "diabetes_duration" IS NOT NULL;

-- Step 4.3.3: Calculate 90th percentile of serum albumin (g/dl) for each race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_albumin_90th_percentile_by_race AS
SELECT f."race_hispanic_origin",
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.90) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."albumin_g_dl") AS "albumin_90th_percentile"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 4.3.4: Calculate cohort median age
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort_median_age AS
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "age_in_years_at_screening") AS "cohort_median_age"
FROM temp_age_at_screening
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- Step 4.3.5: Calculate metabolic ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_metabolic_ratio AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."glycohemoglobin" / f."albumin_g_dl" AS "metabolic_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL AND f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."albumin_g_dl" != 0;

-- Step 4.3.6: Calculate 60th percentile of metabolic ratio for each race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_metabolic_ratio_60th_percentile_by_race AS
SELECT f."race_hispanic_origin",
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.60) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY tmr."metabolic_ratio") AS "metabolic_ratio_60th_percentile"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_metabolic_ratio tmr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tmr."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 4.3.7: Assign risk-1
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_risk_1 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN (f."glycohemoglobin" >= 7.0 AND td."diabetes_duration" <= (SELECT "cohort_wide_25th_percentile_duration" FROM temp_duration_25th_percentile))
                OR f."albumin_g_dl" >= (SELECT "albumin_90th_percentile" FROM temp_albumin_90th_percentile_by_race WHERE "race_hispanic_origin" = f."race_hispanic_origin")
           THEN TRUE
           ELSE FALSE
       END AS "risk_1"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_diabetes_duration td ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = td."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.3.8: Assign risk-2
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_risk_2 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN NOT tr1."risk_1"
                AND (
                    (f."gender" != 'Female' AND f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No')
                    OR f."age_in_years_at_screening" < (SELECT "cohort_median_age" FROM temp_cohort_median_age)
                )
           THEN TRUE
           ELSE FALSE
       END AS "risk_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_1 tr1 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr1."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.3.9: Assign risk-3
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_risk_3 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN NOT (tr1."risk_1" OR tr2."risk_2")
                AND tmr."metabolic_ratio" >= (SELECT "metabolic_ratio_60th_percentile" FROM temp_metabolic_ratio_60th_percentile_by_race WHERE "race_hispanic_origin" = f."race_hispanic_origin")
                AND (f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 65 AND f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'No')
           THEN TRUE
           ELSE FALSE
       END AS "risk_3"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_1 tr1 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr1."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_2 tr2 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr2."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_metabolic_ratio tmr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tmr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.3.10: Assign risk-4
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_risk_4 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN NOT (tr1."risk_1" OR tr2."risk_2" OR tr3."risk_3")
                AND f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL
           THEN TRUE
           ELSE FALSE
       END AS "risk_4"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_1 tr1 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr1."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_2 tr2 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr2."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_3 tr3 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr3."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.3.11: Final risk level mapping
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_risk_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN tr1."risk_1" THEN 'risk-1'
           WHEN tr2."risk_2" THEN 'risk-2'
           WHEN tr3."risk_3" THEN 'risk-3'
           WHEN tr4."risk_4" THEN 'risk-4'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "risk_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_risk_1 tr1 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr1."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_2 tr2 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr2."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_3 tr3 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr3."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_4 tr4 ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr4."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Diabetes Affected Eyes/Had Retinopathy (Outcome)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  tas."age_in_years_at_screening",
  trl."risk_level",
  tor."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_risk_level trl ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trl."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_retinopathy tor ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tor."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;
AI Complementary Review:
The condition for `risk-1` related to glycohemoglobin is incorrect.
The problem states: "the absence of the failure to meet poor control (glycohemoglobin Hba1c (%) greater than or equal to 7.0 (%)) is not present".
*   "Poor control" is `Hba1c >= 7.0`.
*   "Failure to meet poor control" means `Hba1c < 7.0`.
*   "Absence of the failure to meet poor control" means `NOT (Hba1c < 7.0)`, which simplifies to `Hba1c >= 7.0`.
*   "Absence of the failure to meet poor control is not present" means `NOT (Hba1c >= 7.0)`, which simplifies to `Hba1c < 7.0`.

The SQL implements this as `f."glycohemoglobin" >= 7.0`, which is the opposite of the correct interpretation. It should be `f."glycohemoglobin" < 7.0`.

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is gender-depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not. Multiply the total score by 3 for whose gender is female, and multiply the total score by 2 for whose gender is male.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline) OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity if adult food security question = very low food security OR adult food security question = low food security
    - mild food insecurity if adult food security question = marginal food security
    - food secure if adult food security question = full food security

Outcome:
- gender-depression outcome = 1 if modified total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  Calculation of modified total score:
  1. For each of the nine depression questions:
     - Not at all = 0
     - Several days = 1
     - More than half the days = 2
     - Nearly every day = 3
     - If value is null, assign 0
  2. Calculate raw total score by summing the values from the nine depression questions.
  3. Calculate modified total score:
     - If gender = female: modified total score = raw total score * 3
     - If gender = male: modified total score = raw total score * 2

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes or high glucose condition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
-- Extract age_in_years_at_screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Extract race_hispanic_origin for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food insecurity level
-- Map adult food security category to food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Gender-depression outcome
-- Step 1: Score each of the nine depression questions, assigning 0 for null values
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender",
  COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things,
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless,
  COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much,
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy,
  COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS score_poor_appetite_or_overeating,
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS score_feeling_bad_about_yourself,
  COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS score_trouble_concentrating_on_things,
  COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast,
  COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate raw total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_raw_total_score AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  "gender",
  (score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things +
   score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless +
   score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much +
   score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy +
   score_poor_appetite_or_overeating +
   score_feeling_bad_about_yourself +
   score_trouble_concentrating_on_things +
   score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast +
   score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead) AS "raw_total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Step 3: Calculate modified total score based on gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_modified_total_score AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "gender" = 'Female' THEN "raw_total_score" * 3
    WHEN "gender" = 'Male' THEN "raw_total_score" * 2
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "modified_total_score"
FROM temp_raw_total_score;

-- Step 4: Map gender-depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "modified_total_score" >= 10 THEN '1'
    WHEN "modified_total_score" < 10 THEN '0'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "gender_depression_outcome"
FROM temp_modified_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fi."food_insecurity_level",
  gdo."gender_depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender_depression_outcome gdo ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = gdo."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 Include participants whose self-reported doctor diagnosis of diabetes is yes and whose fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) is not less than the median fasting plasma glucose calculated among participants of the same gender who were 20 years or older at screening.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- self-reported doctor diagnosis of diabetes = yes
- fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) >= median fasting plasma glucose calculated among participants of the same gender who were 20 years or older at screening

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: if reported very low food security or low food security
    - mild food insecurity: if reported marginal food security
    - food secure: if reported full food security

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score from nine questions >= 10; 0 otherwise
  The nine questions are:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead
  Each question is scored as:
    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
  If a question's value is null, assign 0.
  Total score is the sum of scores from the nine questions.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants whose self-reported doctor diagnosis of diabetes is 'Yes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate median fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) among participants of the same gender who were 20 years or older at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_fasting_glucose AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "fasting_glucose_mg_dl") AS median_fasting_glucose
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20
  AND "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND "gender" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "gender";

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include those whose fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) is not less than the calculated median
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_median_fasting_glucose mfg ON f."gender" = mfg."gender"
WHERE f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= mfg.median_fasting_glucose;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."household_food_security_category" IN ('HH very low food security: 6-10 (HH w/o child) / 8-18 (HH w/ child)', 'HH low food security: 3-5 (HH w/o child) / 3-7 (HH w/ child)') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
    WHEN f."household_food_security_category" = 'HH marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
    WHEN f."household_food_security_category" = 'HH full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: Depression outcome
-- Score each of the nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0 -- As per schema, if value is null assign (0)
  END AS q1_score,
  CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS q2_score,
  CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS q3_score,
  CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS q4_score,
  CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS q5_score,
  CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS q6_score,
  CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS q7_score,
  CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS q8_score,
  CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS q9_score
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total score from nine questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_total_score AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  (q1_score + q2_score + q3_score + q4_score + q5_score + q6_score + q7_score + q8_score + q9_score) AS total_score
FROM temp_depression_scores_raw;

-- Determine depression outcome based on total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN total_score >= 10 THEN '1'
    ELSE '0'
  END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fi."food_insecurity_level",
  d."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome d ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Consider race/Hispanic origin, gender, age at screening, and define the covariate Confirmation Score ABC as follows: compute a base glycemic load as fasting plasma glucose (mmol/L) plus glycoprotein HbA1c (%); define diabetes-confirmed as having doctor told you have diabetes = Yes or take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes; Confirmation Score ABC equals the base glycemic load minus the race/Hispanic origin specific median base glycemic load calculated among participants who satisfy the study inclusion criteria, except when it is not true that the participant cannot be determined to not be diabetes-confirmed, in which case the Confirmation Score ABC is instead set to 0.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- diabetes defined as meeting any of the following requirements:
    - doctor told you have diabetes = Yes
    OR
    - fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
    OR
    - glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5%
    OR
    - takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes

Exclusion Criteria:
- age at screening < 20
- pregnant at the exam
- missing weight (kg)
- missing waist circumference (cm)
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing urine albumin (mg/L)
- missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
- missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)

Covariates:
- race/Hispanic origin
- gender
- age at screening
- Confirmation Score ABC calculated as:
  - base glycemic load = fasting plasma glucose (mmol/L) + glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
  - diabetes-confirmed = doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes
  - If diabetes-confirmed is True:
    Confirmation Score ABC = 0
  - Else (diabetes-confirmed is False):
    Confirmation Score ABC = base glycemic load - race/Hispanic origin specific median base glycemic load (calculated among participants satisfying study inclusion criteria)

Predictor:
- weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio calculated as:
  waist circumference (cm) / square root of weight(kg)
  categorized as quartiles:
    Q1: <= 25th percentile
    Q2: > 25th percentile and <= 50th percentile
    Q3: > 50th percentile and <= 75th percentile
    Q4: > 75th percentile

Outcome:
- presence of kidney disease = 1 if (eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 OR UACR (mg/g) >= 30 mg/g); 0 otherwise
  - eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2) calculated as:
    - If gender = female AND race/Hispanic origin = Non-Hispanic Black:
      141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
    - If gender = female AND race/Hispanic origin != Non-Hispanic Black:
      141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
    - If gender = male AND race/Hispanic origin = Non-Hispanic Black:
      141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
    - If gender = male AND race/Hispanic origin != Non-Hispanic Black:
      141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
  - UACR (mg/g) calculated as:
    100 * (urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL)

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Determine diabetes status for all participants
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_status_all AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' THEN TRUE
    WHEN "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0 THEN TRUE
    WHEN "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5 THEN TRUE
    WHEN "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS "has_diabetes"
FROM final_master_table;

-- STEP 1.2: Include participants who have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_diabetes_status_all
WHERE "has_diabetes" = TRUE;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Race/Hispanic Origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age at Screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Confirmation Score ABC
-- STEP 4.4.1: Calculate base glycemic load
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_base_glycemic_load AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" + f."glycohemoglobin" AS "base_glycemic_load",
  f."race_hispanic_origin",
  f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
  f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.4.2: Define diabetes-confirmed
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_confirmed AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  "base_glycemic_load",
  "race_hispanic_origin",
  CASE
    WHEN "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS "diabetes_confirmed"
FROM temp_base_glycemic_load;

-- STEP 4.4.3: Calculate race/Hispanic origin specific median base glycemic load among the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_base_glycemic_load_by_race AS
SELECT
  "race_hispanic_origin",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "base_glycemic_load") AS "median_base_glycemic_load"
FROM temp_diabetes_confirmed
WHERE "base_glycemic_load" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 4.4.4: Calculate Confirmation Score ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_confirmation_score_abc AS
SELECT
  tdc."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN tdc."diabetes_confirmed" = TRUE THEN 0.0
    ELSE tdc."base_glycemic_load" - tmbl."median_base_glycemic_load"
  END AS "confirmation_score_abc"
FROM temp_diabetes_confirmed tdc
LEFT JOIN temp_median_base_glycemic_load_by_race tmbl
  ON tdc."race_hispanic_origin" = tmbl."race_hispanic_origin";

-- 4.5 Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- STEP 4.5.1: Calculate raw wwi ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- STEP 4.5.2: Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- STEP 4.5.3: Assign wwi ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT
  twr."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN twr."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN twr."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <= 25th percentile'
    WHEN twr."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: > 25th percentile and <= 50th percentile'
    WHEN twr."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: > 50th percentile and <= 75th percentile'
    ELSE 'Q4: > 75th percentile'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw twr;

-- 4.6 Presence of kidney disease
-- STEP 4.6.1: Calculate eGFR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "eGFR"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.6.2: Calculate UACR (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "UACR"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0;

-- STEP 4.6.3: Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN t_egfr."eGFR" < 60 OR t_uacr."UACR" >= 30 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_egfr t_egfr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_egfr."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_uacr t_uacr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_uacr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  tg."gender",
  tas."age_at_screening",
  tcsa."confirmation_score_abc",
  twwr."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  tpkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_confirmation_score_abc tcsa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tcsa."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio twwr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = twwr."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease tpkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tpkd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 The study population will consist of individuals who cannot be classified as ineligible after sequential evaluation of eligibility rules, where ineligibility is defined as being younger than 30 years at screening or having an age when first told the patient had diabetes that exceeds age at screening; among those not ineligible, inclusion further requires that the participant is not below the race/Hispanic origin specific median glycoprotein HbA1c (%) calculated from the previously included cohort unless the participant answered yes to a question has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75.
 The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for yes and 0 for no.
 We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/Hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Exclusion Criteria:

- missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
- missing diabetic retinopathy status
- missing gender

- missing age at screening
- missing age when first told diabetes

- (Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes? = "borderline") AND (missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) < 6.5%)

Covariates:
- gender
- age in years at screening
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:

    Black,
    Mexican American,
    Other

Predictor:
- serum albumin (g/dL) categorized into quartiles:
    Q1: <25%
    Q2: ≥25 and <50%
    Q3: ≥50 and <75%
    Q4: ≥75%

Outcome:
- diabetic retinopathy status = 1 if “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?” = Yes; 0 if “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?” = No

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes individuals age_in_years_at_screening >= 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those where age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes <= age_in_years_at_screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" <= f."age_in_years_at_screening";

-- STEP 1.3: Inclusion for diabetes diagnosis criterion
-- First, gather necessary data for median calculation from the current cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_cohort_for_median AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."glycohemoglobin",
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate race/Hispanic origin specific median glycoprotein HbA1c (%) from the previously included cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hba1c_median AS
SELECT
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "glycohemoglobin") AS median_hba1c
FROM temp_inclusion_cohort_for_median
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL -- Only consider non-null HbA1c for median calculation
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- Apply the diabetes diagnosis criterion: glycohemoglobin >= race/hispanic origin specific median OR doctor_told_you_have_diabetes = 'Yes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT t."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_cohort_for_median t
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hba1c_median m ON t."race_hispanic_origin" = m."race_hispanic_origin"
WHERE (t."glycohemoglobin" >= m.median_hba1c OR t."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes');

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told the patient had diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients if their age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude patients with uncertain diabetes status (borderline diabetes exclusion)
-- First, determine the 'glycoprotein hba1c condition'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_glycohemoglobin_condition AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5) AS "glycoprotein_hba1c_condition"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step8 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Then, apply the 'borderline diabetes exclusion' criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_glycohemoglobin_condition ghc ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = ghc."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND ghc."glycoprotein_hba1c_condition");

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening (years)
-- Extract age_in_years_at_screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to 'white', 'black', 'mexican american', 'other'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE f."race_hispanic_origin"
    WHEN 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
    WHEN 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
    WHEN 'Other Hispanic' THEN 'other'
    WHEN 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial' THEN 'other'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Serum albumin (g/dL) - Predictor
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5)  WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw
WHERE "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL; -- Exclude NULLs from cutoff calculation

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25 and <50%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50 and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- 4.5 Diabetic retinopathy status - Outcome
-- Map diabetic retinopathy status to 1 for 'Yes' and 0 for 'No'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy_status AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
    WHEN 'Yes' THEN 1
    WHEN 'No' THEN 0
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tsa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles tsa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy_status tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Calculate the glycohemoglobin threshold based on age
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_glycohemoglobin_threshold AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  "glycohemoglobin",
  "age_in_years_at_screening",
  ("age_in_years_at_screening" / 10.0) AS "threshold"
FROM final_master_table;

-- STEP 1.2: Include individuals whose glycohemoglobin (%) level is not less than one-tenth of their current age
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT t."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_glycohemoglobin_threshold t
WHERE t."glycohemoglobin" >= t."threshold";

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude people who did not say 'Yes' to "doctor told you have diabetes"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude people with missing values for "serum albumin (g/dL)"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude people with missing values for "diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Serum albumin (g/dL) (Predictor)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate percentile cutoffs for serum albumin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.20) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS p20,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.80) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS p80
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin categories (Low, Normal, High)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_binned AS
SELECT
  s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT p20 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Low'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" >= (SELECT p20 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) AND s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT p80 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Normal'
    ELSE 'High'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.4 Outcome
-- Get race/hispanic origin counts to find the third largest group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_race_counts AS
SELECT
  f."race_hispanic_origin",
  COUNT(c."respondent_sequence_number") AS "count"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin"
ORDER BY "count" DESC;

-- Identify the third largest race/hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_third_largest_race AS
SELECT "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_outcome_race_counts
OFFSET 2
LIMIT 1;

-- Apply boolean AND criteria for the outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_boolean AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 60 AND
   f."race_hispanic_origin" = (SELECT "race_hispanic_origin" FROM temp_outcome_third_largest_race) AND
   f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes') AS "outcome_boolean"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Map boolean outcome to '1' or '0'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_mapped AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "outcome_boolean" THEN '1'
    ELSE '0'
  END AS "outcome"
FROM temp_outcome_boolean;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  s."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  o."outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_binned s ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = s."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_mapped o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;
