SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all individuals whose age in years at screening is 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate conditional medians for glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_conditional_median_hba1c AS
SELECT
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    f."gender",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."glycohemoglobin") AS median_glycohemoglobin
FROM final_master_table f
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin", f."gender";

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include those whose glycoprotein HbA1c (%) is not less than the conditional median
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_conditional_median_hba1c m ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = m."race_hispanic_origin" AND f."gender" = m."gender"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" >= m.median_glycohemoglobin;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing serum albumin (g/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5));

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Hispanic' THEN 'other'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial' THEN 'other'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: serum albumin (g/dl)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile labels
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT
  s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25th percentile'
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25th percentile and <50th percentile'
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50th percentile and <75th percentile'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75th percentile'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.5 Outcome: diabetic retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN '1'
    WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN '0'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  sa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  dr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy dr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 Only include participants who has information on gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin mg/L, urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl),  weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). Only include individuals whose weight-adjusted waist ratio (WWI) is not less than the mean WWI calculated within their race/Hispanic origin group, multiplied by the ratio of the count of their race/Hispanic origin group to the count of the second-largest race/Hispanic origin group in the cohort.
 Exclude patients who do not have diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the criteria: who reported yes to doctor told you have diabetes  or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) not less than 7.0 mmol/L or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) not smaller than 6.5%, or take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 The predictor is modified weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg) for gender that is less common and we will do *2 to this value for gender that is more common..
 The outcome is the presence of kidney disease, defined as a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater  when the urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).
 Consider race/Hispanic origin, gender, and age at screening in the analysis.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- gender is not missing
- race/Hispanic origin is not missing
- urine albumin mg/L is not missing
- urine creatinine (mg/dL) is not missing
- serum creatinine (mg/dl) is not missing
- weight (kg) is not missing
- waist circumference (cm) is not missing
- weight-adjusted waist ratio (WWI) is not less than the mean WWI calculated within their race/Hispanic origin group, multiplied by the ratio of the count of their race/Hispanic origin group to the count of the second-largest race/Hispanic origin group in the cohort, where:
    - WWI = waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight (kg))
    - For each race/Hispanic origin group:
        - Calculate the mean WWI for participants within that group.
        - Calculate the count of participants within that group.
    - Identify the second-largest count among all race/Hispanic origin groups.
    - Condition: WWI >= (mean WWI of participant's race/Hispanic origin group * (count of participant's race/Hispanic origin group / count of the second-largest race/Hispanic origin group))

Exclusion Criteria:
- (doctor told you have diabetes = No AND fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) < 7.0 AND glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) < 6.5 AND take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = No)

Covariates:
- race/Hispanic origin
- gender
- age at screening

Predictor:
- modified weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio calculated as:
  1. Determine the count of participants for each gender in the cohort.
  2. Identify the gender with the minimum count (less common gender) and the gender with the maximum count (more common gender).
  3. If gender is the less common gender:
     modified weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio = waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight (kg))
  4. If gender is the more common gender:
     modified weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio = (waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight (kg))) * 2

Outcome:
- presence of kidney disease = 1 if urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) >= 30; 0 otherwise, where:
  urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) = 100 * (urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL)

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants with information on gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include those with information on race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include those with information on urine albumin mg/L
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.4: From the above group, include those with information on urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.5: From the above group, include those with information on serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.6: From the above group, include those with information on weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.7: From the above group, include those with information on waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.8: Include individuals whose weight-adjusted waist ratio (WWI) is not less than the mean WWI calculated within their race/Hispanic origin group, multiplied by the ratio of the count of their race/Hispanic origin group to the count of the second-largest race/Hispanic origin group in the cohort.

-- Calculate raw WWI for the current inclusion cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_raw AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    f."waist_circumference_cm",
    f."weight_kg",
    f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "wwi"
FROM temp_inclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate mean WWI and count for each race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_wwi_stats AS
SELECT
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    AVG("wwi") AS "mean_wwi_by_race",
    COUNT("respondent_sequence_number") AS "count_by_race"
FROM temp_wwi_raw
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- Determine the count of the second-largest race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_second_largest_race_count AS
SELECT "count_by_race"
FROM temp_race_wwi_stats
ORDER BY "count_by_race" DESC
OFFSET 1
LIMIT 1;

-- Apply the derived WWI inclusion condition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step8 AS
SELECT t."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_wwi_raw t
JOIN temp_race_wwi_stats s ON t."race_hispanic_origin" = s."race_hispanic_origin"
CROSS JOIN temp_second_largest_race_count sl
WHERE t."wwi" >= (s."mean_wwi_by_race" * (s."count_by_race"::NUMERIC / sl."count_by_race"::NUMERIC));

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients who do not have diabetes
-- Diabetes is defined as meeting any of the criteria:
-- doctor told you have diabetes = 'Yes' OR
-- fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 OR
-- glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5 OR
-- take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = 'Yes'
-- We exclude those who do NOT meet ANY of these criteria.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR
     f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0 OR
     f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5 OR
     f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes');

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Modified weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate base wwi
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_base_wwi AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender",
    f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "base_wwi"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Determine gender counts in the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_counts AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    COUNT("respondent_sequence_number") AS "gender_count"
FROM temp_predictor_base_wwi
GROUP BY "gender";

-- Identify the more and less common gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_commonality AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    "gender_count",
    CASE
        WHEN "gender_count" = (SELECT MIN("gender_count") FROM temp_gender_counts) THEN 'less_common'
        WHEN "gender_count" = (SELECT MAX("gender_count") FROM temp_gender_counts) THEN 'more_common'
        ELSE NULL -- Should not happen with only two genders
    END AS "commonality_status"
FROM temp_gender_counts;

-- Calculate modified weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_modified_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT
    p."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN gc."commonality_status" = 'less_common' THEN p."base_wwi"
        WHEN gc."commonality_status" = 'more_common' THEN p."base_wwi" * 2
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "modified_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_predictor_base_wwi p
JOIN temp_gender_commonality gc ON p."gender" = gc."gender";

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    100.0 * (f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Map UACR to presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN 1
        WHEN "uacr_mg_g" < 30 THEN 0
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_uacr_raw;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    trho."race_hispanic_origin",
    tg."gender",
    tas."age_at_screening",
    tmwr."modified_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
    tpkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_modified_wwi_ratio tmwr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tmwr."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease tpkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tpkd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 Exclude participants who had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm), gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). Exclude participants who cannot be classified as remaining eligible after applying the following rule: eligibility holds if glycoprotein HbA1c (%) is not greater than the race/Hispanic origin specific 90th percentile calculated among participants who satisfy the study inclusion criteria and are aged 20 years or older; however, participants who would otherwise be excluded under this rule are retained unless it is simultaneously true that fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) is greater than or equal to 7.0 and whether they take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar is yes.
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5% OR takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing weight (kg)
- missing waist circumference (cm)
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing urine albumin (mg/L)
- missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
- missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
- (glycoprotein HbA1c (%) > race/Hispanic origin specific 90th percentile) AND (fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 AND takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes)
  where race/Hispanic origin specific 90th percentile is calculated for glycoprotein HbA1c (%) among participants satisfying the study inclusion criteria and aged 20 years or older, grouped by race/Hispanic origin

Covariates:
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
  white,
  non white
- gender

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who meet any of the diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
   OR "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
   OR "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Calculate race/Hispanic origin specific 90th percentile for HbA1c for sub-population
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_percentiles AS
SELECT
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.90) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."glycohemoglobin") AS "hba1c_90th_percentile"
FROM final_master_table f
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20
  AND (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
       OR f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
       OR f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
       OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes')
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants based on the complex HbA1c rule
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_hba1c_percentiles p ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = p."race_hispanic_origin"
WHERE NOT (
    f."glycohemoglobin" > p."hba1c_90th_percentile"
    AND f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
    AND f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic Origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Mexican American', 'Non-Hispanic Black', 'Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'non white'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at Screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (WWI) ratio
-- Calculate raw WWI ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for WWI ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign WWI ratio to quartiles
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT
  r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" > (SELECT q1 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) AND r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25 and <=50'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" > (SELECT q2 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) AND r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50 and <=75'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of Kidney Disease
-- Calculate eGFR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_egfr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate Urine Albumin-to-Creatinine Ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0;

-- Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" < 60 OR u."urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening and gender, and additionally include Indicator ABC that equals 1 if the participant is not in the set of individuals who cannot be determined to not be 60 years or older and equals 0 otherwise; however, this indicator is immediately set to 0 unless it is simultaneously true that the participant is not female and is not taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = "Yes" OR doctor told you have diabetes = "Borderline") OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = "Yes" OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things" OR missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" OR missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much" OR missing "feeling tired or having little energy" OR missing "poor appetite or overeating" OR missing "feeling bad about yourself" OR missing "trouble concentrating on things" OR missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast" OR missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- Indicator ABC calculated as:
  Indicator ABC = 1 if (age in years at screening < 60 AND gender != "female" AND reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar != "Yes")
  Indicator ABC = 0 otherwise

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  severe food insecurity: if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR adult food security question = "low food security"
  mild food insecurity: if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
  food secure: if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total depression score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  Total depression score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    "have little interest in doing things"
    "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    "feeling tired or having little energy"
    "poor appetite or overeating"
    "feeling bad about yourself"
    "trouble concentrating on things"
    "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question is scored as:
    Not at all = 0
    Several days = 1
    More than half the days = 2
    Nearly every day = 3
  If a question's value is null, assign 0 for that question.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who meet diabetes diagnosis criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
    OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
    OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
    OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Indicator ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_indicator_abc AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60
            AND f."gender" != 'Female'
            AND f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" != 'Yes' THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "indicator_abc"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food Insecurity Level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD very low food security: 6-10' OR f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD low food security: 3-5' THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression Outcome
-- Step 1: Score each depression question, assigning 0 if null
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Not at all' OR f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 -- Should not happen given exclusion, but for safety
    END AS score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things,
    CASE
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Not at all' OR f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless,
    CASE
        WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Not at all' OR f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much,
    CASE
        WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Not at all' OR f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy,
    CASE
        WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Not at all' OR f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_poor_appetite_or_overeating,
    CASE
        WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Not at all' OR f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_feeling_bad_about_yourself,
    CASE
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Not at all' OR f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_trouble_concentrating_on_things,
    CASE
        WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Not at all' OR f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast,
    CASE
        WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Not at all' OR f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NULL THEN 0
        WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things +
     score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless +
     score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much +
     score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy +
     score_poor_appetite_or_overeating +
     score_feeling_bad_about_yourself +
     score_trouble_concentrating_on_things +
     score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast +
     score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead) AS "total_depression_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Step 3: Determine depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS

    CASE

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if age when first told diabetes is less than 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told diabetes is greater than age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" <= f."age_in_years_at_screening";

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants if glycohemoglobin HbA1c (%) is less than 5.0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" >= 5.0;

-- STEP 2.11: Exclude participants if glycohemoglobin HbA1c (%) is greater than 20.0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step11 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" <= 20.0;

-- STEP 2.12: Exclude participants if serum albumin (g/dL) is less than 1.0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step12 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step11 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" >= 1.0;

-- STEP 2.13: Exclude participants if serum albumin (g/dL) is greater than 6.0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step13 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step12 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" <= 6.0;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step13;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           ELSE 'other'
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Serum albumin (g/dL) categorized into quartiles
-- Get raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25 and <50%'
    WHEN s."serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50 and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Diabetic retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  sa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  dr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy dr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor will be a Index ABC defined as serum albumin (g/dL) divided by the mean serum albumin (g/dL) within the participants race/Hispanic origin group. Then, within each gender group, the average of these race-adjusted albumin values is calculated across all participants of that gender. Finally, each participant's race-adjusted albumin value is divided by this gender specific average, and the resulting value is defined as Index ABC.
 The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for yes and 0 for no.
 We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/Hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:

- complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
- complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing serum albumin (g/dL)
- missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
- missing diabetic retinopathy status
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing age in years at screening
- missing age when first told diabetes
- age in years at screening < 30
- age when first told the patient had diabetes < 30
- uncertain diabetes status, defined as:
    "Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?" = "borderline" AND (glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) is missing OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) < 6.5%)

Covariates:
- gender
- age in years at screening
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
    White
    Black
    Mexican American
    Other

Predictor:
- Index ABC calculated as:
    1. Calculate mean serum albumin (g/dL) within each race/Hispanic origin group.
    2. Calculate race-adjusted albumin for each participant: serum albumin (g/dL) / mean serum albumin (g/dL) within their race/Hispanic origin group.
    3. Within each gender group, calculate the average of these race-adjusted albumin values.
    4. Index ABC = participant's race-adjusted albumin value / gender-specific average of race-adjusted albumin values.

Outcome:
- diabetic retinopathy = 1 if "diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?" = "yes"; 0 if "diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?" = "no"

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude patients if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude patients with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5));

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening
-- Extract age in years at screening for cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to 'white', 'black', 'mexican american', 'other'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Index ABC
-- STEP 4.4.1: Calculate mean serum albumin (g/dL) within each race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_step1_mean_albumin_by_race AS
SELECT
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    AVG(f."albumin_g_dl") AS "mean_serum_albumin_by_race"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 4.4.2: Calculate race-adjusted albumin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_step2_race_adjusted_albumin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender",
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    f."albumin_g_dl" / m."mean_serum_albumin_by_race" AS "race_adjusted_albumin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_predictor_step1_mean_albumin_by_race m ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = m."race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 4.4.3: Calculate gender-specific average of race-adjusted albumin values
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_step3_mean_race_adjusted_albumin_by_gender AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    AVG("race_adjusted_albumin") AS "gender_specific_avg_race_adjusted_albumin"
FROM temp_predictor_step2_race_adjusted_albumin
GROUP BY "gender";

-- STEP 4.4.4: Calculate Index ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_step4_index_abc AS
SELECT
    r."respondent_sequence_number",
    r."race_adjusted_albumin" / g."gender_specific_avg_race_adjusted_albumin" AS "index_abc"
FROM temp_predictor_step2_race_adjusted_albumin r
JOIN temp_predictor_step3_mean_race_adjusted_albumin_by_gender g ON r."gender" = g."gender";

-- 4.5 Diabetic Retinopathy
-- Map diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy? to 1 for yes, 0 for no
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
    WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tpr."index_abc",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_predictor_step4_index_abc tpr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tpr."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include gender and an age standardized fasting plasma glucose value, defined as the participants fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) minus the mean fasting plasma glucose among participants in the same age group (stratified as 20-49, 50-64, 65 or more years).
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = "yes" OR doctor told you have diabetes = "borderline") OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = "yes" OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- gender
- age standardized fasting plasma glucose value calculated as:
  fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) - mean fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) among participants in the same age group
  Age groups for standardization:
    - 20-49 years
    - 50-64 years
    - 65 or more years

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR adult food security question = "low food security"
  - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
  - food secure: if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  Total score calculated from the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
  - "have little interest in doing things"
  - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
  - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
  - "feeling tired or having little energy"
  - "poor appetite or overeating"
  - "feeling bad about yourself"
  - "trouble concentrating on things"
  - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
  - "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question scored as:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
    - If value is null, assign 0
