SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include those with diabetes or high glucose status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with any missing depression question
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age standardized fasting plasma glucose value (mg/dl)
-- Categorize age at screening into specified age groups
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 50 THEN '20-49 years'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 50 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 65 THEN '50-64 years'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 65 THEN '65 or more years'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_group"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate mean fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) among participants in the same age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_glucose_by_age_group AS
SELECT "age_group",
       AVG("fasting_glucose_mg_dl") AS "mean_fasting_glucose"
FROM temp_age_group
GROUP BY "age_group";

-- Calculate age standardized fasting plasma glucose value (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_standardized_fasting_plasma_glucose_value_mg_dl AS
SELECT ag."respondent_sequence_number",
       (ag."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" - mg."mean_fasting_glucose") AS "age_standardized_fasting_plasma_glucose_value_mg_dl"
FROM temp_age_group ag
JOIN temp_mean_glucose_by_age_group mg ON ag."age_group" = mg."age_group";

-- 4.3 Food insecurity level
-- Map adult food security category to food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Depression outcome
-- Score each depression question
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_interest",
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_depressed",
       COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_sleep",
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_energy",
       COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_appetite",
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_bad_self",
       COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_concentrating",
       COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_moving",
       COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       ("score_interest" + "score_depressed" + "score_sleep" + "score_energy" + "score_appetite" + "score_bad_self" + "score_concentrating" + "score_moving" + "score_dead") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Map total score to depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN '1'
           ELSE '0'
       END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  asfg."age_standardized_fasting_plasma_glucose_value_mg_dl",
  fil."food_insecurity_level",
  "do"."depression_outcome" -- Corrected: Quoted the alias "do"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_standardized_fasting_plasma_glucose_value_mg_dl asfg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = asfg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fil ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fil."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome "do" ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = "do"."respondent_sequence_number"; -- Corrected: Quoted the alias "do"

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

       COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       ("score_interest" + "score_depressed" + "score_sleep" + "score_energy" + "score_appetite" + "score_bad_self" + "score_concentrating" + "score_moving" + "score_dead") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Map total score to depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN '1'
           ELSE '0'
       END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  asfg."age_standardized_fasting_plasma_glucose_value_mg_dl",
  fil."food_insecurity_level",
  "do"."depression_outcome" -- Corrected: Quoted the alias "do"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_standardized_fasting_plasma_glucose_value_mg_dl asfg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = asfg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fil ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fil."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome "do" ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = "do"."respondent_sequence_number"; -- Corrected: Quoted the alias "do"

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 Define the predictor as Hierarchically Adjusted Food Insecurity Exposure, where participants are first assigned a base food insecurity category from the adult food security questionnaire (severe food insecurity if very low or low food security, mild food insecurity if marginal food security, and food secure if full food security), and this category is not left unmodified unless it is not true that the participant cannot be determined to not be diabetes-positive; diabetes-positive is defined as not failing to meet at least one of the diabetes criteria (self-reported diabetes is “Yes” or “Borderline,” taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, or fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) > 126); if diabetes-positive, the participants food insecurity category is escalated by one level (food secure to mild, mild to severe), except that this escalation is immediately reversed if the participant reports taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.

      - Else if adult food security questionnaire = "marginal food security", then `base_food_insecurity_category` = "mild food insecurity".
      - Else if adult food security questionnaire = "full food security", then `base_food_insecurity_category` = "food secure".
  2.  **Derive `is_diabetes_positive`:**
      - `is_diabetes_positive` = TRUE if (doctor told you have diabetes = "Yes" OR "Borderline") OR (reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = TRUE) OR (fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) > 126).
      - `is_diabetes_positive` = FALSE otherwise.

      - Initialize `adjusted_category = base_food_insecurity_category`.
      - If `is_diabetes_positive` is TRUE:
          - If `base_food_insecurity_category` = "food secure", then `adjusted_category` = "mild food insecurity".
          - Else if `base_food_insecurity_category` = "mild food insecurity", then `adjusted_category` = "severe food insecurity".
          - Else if `base_food_insecurity_category` = "severe food insecurity", then `adjusted_category` = "severe food insecurity".
      - If `reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar` is TRUE AND `adjusted_category` is different from `base_food_insecurity_category`:
          - If `adjusted_category` = "mild food insecurity" (meaning it was escalated from "food secure"), then `Hierarchically Adjusted Food Insecurity Exposure` = "food secure".
          - Else if `adjusted_category` = "severe food insecurity" (meaning it was escalated from "mild food insecurity"), then `Hierarchically Adjusted Food Insecurity Exposure` = "mild food insecurity".
      - Else, `Hierarchically Adjusted Food Insecurity Exposure` = `adjusted_category`.

  - **Nine depression questions:**
    - "have little interest in doing things"

    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
  - **Value mapping for each question:**
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
    - If value is null, assign 0.
  - **Total score:** Sum of the mapped values from the nine questions.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate diabetes inclusion criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_inclusion_criteria AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number",
  (
    (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline')
    OR (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes')
    OR (f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126)
  ) AS "diabetes_inclusion_criteria"
FROM final_master_table f;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those who meet diabetes inclusion criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN temp_diabetes_inclusion_criteria d ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE d."diabetes_inclusion_criteria" IS TRUE;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: hierarchically adjusted food insecurity exposure
-- STEP 4.4.1: Derive base_food_insecurity_category
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_base_food_insecurity_category AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "base_food_insecurity_category"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.4.2: Derive is_diabetes_positive
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_is_diabetes_positive AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number",
  (
    (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline')
    OR (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes')
    OR (f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126)
  ) AS "is_diabetes_positive"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.4.3: Initialize adjusted_category with base_food_insecurity_category and then adjust based on is_diabetes_positive
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_adjusted_category_intermediate AS
SELECT
  b."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN d."is_diabetes_positive" IS TRUE AND b."base_food_insecurity_category" = 'food secure' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
    WHEN d."is_diabetes_positive" IS TRUE AND b."base_food_insecurity_category" = 'mild food insecurity' THEN 'severe food insecurity'
    WHEN d."is_diabetes_positive" IS TRUE AND b."base_food_insecurity_category" = 'severe food insecurity' THEN 'severe food insecurity'
    ELSE b."base_food_insecurity_category"
  END AS "adjusted_category_intermediate"
FROM temp_base_food_insecurity_category b
JOIN temp_is_diabetes_positive d ON b."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.4.4: Final adjustment for Hierarchically Adjusted Food Insecurity Exposure
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hierarchically_adjusted_food_insecurity_exposure AS
SELECT
  aci."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' AND aci."adjusted_category_intermediate" = 'mild food insecurity' AND b."base_food_insecurity_category" = 'food secure' THEN 'food secure'
    WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' AND aci."adjusted_category_intermediate" = 'severe food insecurity' AND b."base_food_insecurity_category" = 'mild food insecurity' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
    ELSE aci."adjusted_category_intermediate"
  END AS "hierarchically_adjusted_food_insecurity_exposure"
FROM temp_adjusted_category_intermediate aci
JOIN temp_base_food_insecurity_category b ON aci."respondent_sequence_number" = b."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN final_master_table f ON aci."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: depression outcome
-- STEP 4.5.1: Map each of the nine depression question responses to numerical scores (0-3), assigning 0 for null values.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things",
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless",
  COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much",
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy",
  COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating",
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself",
  COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things",
  COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
  COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.2: Calculate total score from nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  (
    "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things" +
    "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" +
    "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" +
    "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" +
    "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating" +
    "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself" +
    "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things" +
    "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" +
    "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
  ) AS "total_score_from_nine_depression_questions"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- STEP 4.5.3: Derive depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "total_score_from_nine_depression_questions" >= 10 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  gender."gender",
  race."race_hispanic_origin",
  food_insecurity."hierarchically_adjusted_food_insecurity_exposure",
  dep_outcome."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender gender ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = gender."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin race ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = race."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_hierarchically_adjusted_food_insecurity_exposure food_insecurity ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = food_insecurity."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_depression_outcome dep_outcome ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dep_outcome."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is coded as 1 for participants not determined to belong to the "Low Risk Metabolic Mood" class. This class requires a depression total score (which is based on the total score from the nine depression questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3)) is less than 10 and a fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) below the age group specific median (stratified by age at screening <50 or >=50). Participants failing this are coded as 1 unless it is additionally true that their adult food security questionaire is "Marginal" or "Full" and they report "Not at all" (0) for "thoughts of being better off dead", in which case they are hierarchically reassigned to 0.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following:
    - doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR Borderline
    - reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
    - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: adult food security question = "very low food security" OR "low food security"
    - mild food insecurity: adult food security question = "marginal food security"
    - food secure: adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- Outcome = 1 if not in "Low Risk Metabolic Mood" class, unless reassigned to 0; 0 otherwise.

  **"Low Risk Metabolic Mood" class definition:**
  (depression total score < 10) AND (fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) < age group specific median)

  **Derivation of depression total score:**
  Sum of scores from the nine depression questions, where each question is scored:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
  The nine depression questions are:
    1. "have little interest in doing things"
    2. "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    3. "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    4. "feeling tired or having little energy"
    5. "poor appetite or overeating"
    6. "feeling bad about yourself"
    7. "trouble concentrating on things"
    8. "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    9. "thought you would be better off dead"

  **Derivation of age group specific median for fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl):**
  Median fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) is calculated separately for two age groups:
    - age at screening < 50
    - age at screening >= 50

  **Outcome assignment logic:**
  1. Calculate `depression total score`.
  2. Determine `age group specific median` for `fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl)`.
  3. Define `is_low_risk_metabolic_mood` as TRUE if (`depression total score` < 10) AND (`fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl)` < `age group specific median`); FALSE otherwise.
  4. Initialize `outcome` = 1 if `is_low_risk_metabolic_mood` is FALSE; 0 otherwise.
  5. **Hierarchical Reassignment:** If `outcome` is 1 AND (`adult food security questionaire` = "Marginal" OR "Full") AND (`"thoughts of being better off dead"` = "Not at all" (0)), then reassign `outcome` to 0.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')) OR
    (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes') OR
    (f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: outcome
-- STEP 4.5.1: Calculate depression total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores_mapped AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "score_interest",
  CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "score_depressed",
  CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "score_sleep",
  CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "score_energy",
  CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "score_appetite",
  CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "score_bad_self",
  CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "score_concentrating",
  CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "score_moving",
  CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "score_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_total_score AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  (
    "score_interest" + "score_depressed" + "score_sleep" + "score_energy" +
    "score_appetite" + "score_bad_self" + "score_concentrating" + "score_moving" +
    "score_dead"
  ) AS "depression_total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores_mapped;

-- STEP 4.5.2: Calculate age group specific median for fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group_medians AS
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 50 THEN 'age < 50'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 50 THEN 'age >= 50'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_group",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl") AS "median_fasting_glucose"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 50 THEN 'age < 50'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 50 THEN 'age >= 50'
    ELSE NULL
  END;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_fasting_glucose_with_median AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
  f."age_in_years_at_screening",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 50 THEN (SELECT "median_fasting_glucose" FROM temp_age_group_medians WHERE "age_group" = 'age < 50')
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 50 THEN (SELECT "median_fasting_glucose" FROM temp_age_group_medians WHERE "age_group" = 'age >= 50')
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_group_specific_median_glucose"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.3: Determine is_low_risk_metabolic_mood
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_is_low_risk_metabolic_mood AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (
    (tds."depression_total_score" < 10) AND
    (tfg."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" < tfg."age_group_specific_median_glucose")
  ) AS "is_low_risk_metabolic_mood"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_depression_total_score tds ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tds."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_fasting_glucose_with_median tfg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tfg."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.4: Initial outcome assignment
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_initial_outcome AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN NOT tlr."is_low_risk_metabolic_mood" THEN 1
    WHEN tlr."is_low_risk_metabolic_mood" THEN 0
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "initial_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_is_low_risk_metabolic_mood tlr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tlr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.5: Hierarchical reassignment of outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN tio."initial_outcome" = 1 AND
         f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD marginal food security: 1-2', 'AD full food security: 0') AND
         f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Not at all'
      THEN 0
    ELSE tio."initial_outcome"
  END AS "outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_initial_outcome tio ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tio."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tage."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tg."gender",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tfi."food_insecurity_level",
  to_final."outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening tage ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tage."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level tfi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tfi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome to_final ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = to_final."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 Exclude participants who had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm), gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). Exclude participants whose urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR, mg/g) calculated as 100 times urine albumin (mg/L) divided by urine creatinine (mg/dL) is greater than the gender specific 85th percentile of UACR calculated among inclusion-eligible participants aged less than 60 years.
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- doctor told you have diabetes = Yes
OR
- fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
OR
- glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5%
OR

    100 * urine albumin (mg/L) / urine creatinine (mg/dL)

Covariates:
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
    white

    <60 if age at screening < 60
    >=60 if age at screening >= 60

Predictor:

    Q2: >25% and <=50%
    Q3: >50% and <=75%
    Q4: >75%

Outcome:
- presence of kidney disease = 1 if (eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 OR UACR (mg/g) >= 30 mg/g); 0 otherwise
  eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2) calculated for 4 different cases:
  1. If gender = female AND race/Hispanic origin = Non-Hispanic Black:
     141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
  2. If gender = female AND race/Hispanic origin ≠ Non-Hispanic Black:
     141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
  3. If gender = male AND race/Hispanic origin = Non-Hispanic Black:
     141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
  4. If gender = male AND race/Hispanic origin ≠ Non-Hispanic Black:
     141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who reported 'Yes' to doctor told you have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_diabetes_reported AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.2: Include participants with fasting plasma glucose (FPG) >= 7.0 mmol/L
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_fpg_high AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0;

-- STEP 1.3: Include participants with glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) >= 6.5%
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_hba1c_high AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5;

-- STEP 1.4: Include participants who take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_diabetic_pills AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.5: Combine all diabetes inclusion criteria using OR logic
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_diabetes_reported
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_fpg_high
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_hba1c_high
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_diabetic_pills;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Calculate UACR for inclusion-eligible participants aged < 60 years to determine percentiles
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_percentile_base_cohort AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender",
    (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60
  AND f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Calculate gender-specific 85th percentile of UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_gender_85th_percentiles AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.85) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "uacr_mg_g") AS "uacr_85th_percentile"
FROM temp_uacr_percentile_base_cohort
GROUP BY "gender";

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants whose UACR is greater than the gender-specific 85th percentile
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_uacr_gender_85th_percentiles p ON f."gender" = p."gender"
WHERE (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") <= p."uacr_85th_percentile"
   OR p."uacr_85th_percentile" IS NULL; -- Keep if percentile cannot be determined (e.g., gender not in percentile calculation base)

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Mexican American', 'Non-Hispanic Black', 'Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'non white'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw wwi ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q3
FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign wwi ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_ratio_quartiles AS
SELECT
    r."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" IS NULL THEN NULL
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
        ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
    END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- Calculate eGFR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_egfr_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."gender" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- Calculate UACR for outcome definition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN (e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m2" < 60) OR (u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30) THEN '1'
        ELSE '0'
    END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_egfr_raw e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_uacr_raw u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m2" IS NOT NULL OR u."uacr_mg_g" IS NOT NULL); -- Only include if at least one component is calculable

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  o."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_pred_wwi_ratio_quartiles p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_kidney_disease o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The predictor is Score ABC, defined as the participants food insecurity level derived from the adult food security questionnaire (coded as 4 for very low food insecurity, 3 for low food insecurity, 1 for marginal food insecurity, and 0 for full food security), divided by the mean food insecurity level among participants of the same gender.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline) OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things" OR missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" OR missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much" OR missing "feeling tired or having little energy" OR missing "poor appetite or overeating" OR missing "feeling bad about yourself" OR missing "trouble concentrating on things" OR missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast" OR missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- Score ABC calculated as:
  (adult food security questionnaire coded as: 4 for very low food insecurity, 3 for low food insecurity, 1 for marginal food insecurity, 0 for full food security) / mean(adult food security questionnaire coded as: 4 for very low food insecurity, 3 for low food insecurity, 1 for marginal food insecurity, 0 for full food security) grouped by gender

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  total score calculated as:
    SUM of scores from the following nine questions:
      "have little interest in doing things"
      "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
      "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
      "feeling tired or having little energy"
      "poor appetite or overeating"
      "feeling bad about yourself"
      "trouble concentrating on things"
      "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
      "thought you would be better off dead
    Each question's score is assigned as:
      Not at all: 0
      Several days: 1
      More than half the days: 2
      Nearly every day: 3
    If a question's value is null, assign 0 for that question's score.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes or high fasting plasma glucose
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Score ABC
-- STEP 4.4.1: Map adult food security questionnaire to numeric scores
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_security_coded AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender",
       CASE f."adult_food_security_category"
           WHEN 'AD very low food security: 6-10' THEN 4
           WHEN 'AD low food security: 3-5' THEN 3
           WHEN 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 1
           WHEN 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "adult_food_security_coded"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.4.2: Calculate mean food insecurity level grouped by gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_food_security_by_gender AS
SELECT "gender",
       AVG("adult_food_security_coded") AS "mean_adult_food_security_coded_by_gender"
FROM temp_food_security_coded
GROUP BY "gender";

-- STEP 4.4.3: Calculate score abc
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_abc AS
SELECT fsc."respondent_sequence_number",
       fsc."adult_food_security_coded" / mfs."mean_adult_food_security_coded_by_gender" AS "score_abc"
FROM temp_food_security_coded fsc
JOIN temp_mean_food_security_by_gender mfs ON fsc."gender" = mfs."gender";

-- 4.5 Depression Outcome
-- STEP 4.5.1: Score for 'have little interest in doing things'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- null_value_assignment
       END AS "have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.2: Score for 'feeling down, depressed, or hopeless'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- null_value_assignment
       END AS "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.3: Score for 'trouble sleeping or sleeping too much'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- null_value_assignment
       END AS "trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.4: Score for 'feeling tired or having little energy'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- null_value_assignment
       END AS "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.5: Score for 'poor appetite or overeating'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_poor_appetite_or_overeating_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- null_value_assignment
       END AS "poor_appetite_or_overeating_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.6: Score for 'feeling bad about yourself'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_feeling_bad_about_yourself_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- null_value_assignment
       END AS "feeling_bad_about_yourself_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.7: Score for 'trouble concentrating on things'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_trouble_concentrating_on_things_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- null_value_assignment
       END AS "trouble_concentrating_on_things_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.8: Score for 'moving or speaking slowly or too fast'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- null_value_assignment
       END AS "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.9: Score for 'thought you would be better off dead'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- null_value_assignment
       END AS "thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.10: Calculate total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(hli."have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score", 0) +
    COALESCE(fdd."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score", 0) +
    COALESCE(tss."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score", 0) +
    COALESCE(fte."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score", 0) +
    COALESCE(pao."poor_appetite_or_overeating_score", 0) +
    COALESCE(fby."feeling_bad_about_yourself_score", 0) +
    COALESCE(tc."trouble_concentrating_on_things_score", 0) +
    COALESCE(mss."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score", 0) +
    COALESCE(tyb."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score", 0) AS "total_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score hli ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = hli."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score fdd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fdd."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score tss ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tss."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score fte ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fte."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_poor_appetite_or_overeating_score pao ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = pao."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_feeling_bad_about_yourself_score fby ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fby."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_trouble_concentrating_on_things_score tc ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tc."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score mss ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = mss."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score tyb ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tyb."respondent_sequence_number";


-- STEP 4.5.11: Determine depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  gen."gender",
  race."race_hispanic_origin",
  pred."score_abc",
  outc."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender gen ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = gen."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin race ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = race."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_abc pred ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = pred."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome outc ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = outc."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 Only include individuals whose glycohemoglobin (%) level is not less than one-tenth of their current age in years at screening.
 We will not include people who did not say yes to a question doctor told you have diabetes or failed to have values for serum albumin (g/dL) or diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 The predictor will be serum albumin (g/dL) categorized as low, normal, and high based on percentages and make normal has middle 60%.
 The outcome will be diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy but we will label the outcome as positive(1) only if the participants have diabetic retinopathy and serum albumin (g/dL) is not less than the 50(%)of the distribution for their gender and the calculated cohort ratio (respective race/Hispanic origin group/total count multiplied by 5) is larger than a predefined external constant of 1, otherwise the outcome is negative (0).
