SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes individuals whose glycohemoglobin (%) level is not less than one-tenth of their current age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" >= ("age_in_years_at_screening" / 10.0);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude people who did not say yes to "doctor told you have diabetes"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude people who failed to have values for serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude people who failed to have values for diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Predictor: Serum Albumin (g/dL) - Binned
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute 20th and 80th percentile cutoffs for serum albumin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.20) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS p20,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.80) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS p80
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin to low, normal, or high bins
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT p20 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'low: < 20th percentile of serum albumin (g/dL)'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" >= (SELECT p20 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) AND s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT p80 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'normal: >= 20th percentile of serum albumin (g/dL) and < 80th percentile of serum albumin (g/dL)'
    ELSE 'high: >= 80th percentile of serum albumin (g/dL)'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.4 Outcome: Diabetes Affected Eyes/Had Retinopathy - Derived

-- 4.4.1: Calculate serum_albumin_above_50th_percentile_by_gender
-- Get serum albumin and gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_albumin_by_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender",
       f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate 50th percentile of serum albumin for each gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_albumin_50th_percentiles AS
SELECT
  "gender",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS p50_albumin
FROM temp_albumin_by_gender
GROUP BY "gender";

-- Determine if serum albumin is above the 50th percentile for the participant's gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_above_50th_percentile_by_gender AS
SELECT t."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
         WHEN t."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
         WHEN t."albumin_g_dl" >= p.p50_albumin THEN TRUE
         ELSE FALSE
       END AS "serum_albumin_above_50th_percentile_by_gender"
FROM temp_albumin_by_gender t
JOIN temp_gender_albumin_50th_percentiles p ON t."gender" = p."gender";

-- 4.4.2: Calculate cohort ratio
-- Get race/Hispanic origin and total_number_of_people_in_the_family for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort_race_data AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin",
       f."total_number_of_people_in_the_family"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate count of participants in each race/Hispanic origin group within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_origin_group_counts AS
SELECT
  "race_hispanic_origin",
  COUNT("respondent_sequence_number") AS group_count
FROM temp_cohort_race_data
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- Calculate cohort ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort_ratio AS
SELECT t."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
         WHEN t."total_number_of_people_in_the_family" IS NULL OR t."total_number_of_people_in_the_family" = 0 THEN NULL
         WHEN grp.group_count IS NULL THEN NULL
         ELSE (grp.group_count::NUMERIC / t."total_number_of_people_in_the_family") * 5.0
       END AS "cohort_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort_race_data t
LEFT JOIN temp_race_origin_group_counts grp ON t."race_hispanic_origin" = grp."race_hispanic_origin";

-- 4.4.3: Prepare diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy for boolean AND
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_retinopathy_binary AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
         WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN TRUE
         WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN FALSE
         ELSE NULL
       END AS "has_diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4.4: Apply boolean AND logic for the outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_boolean_and AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (r.has_diabetic_retinopathy AND s.serum_albumin_above_50th_percentile_by_gender AND cr.cohort_ratio > 1) AS outcome_condition_met
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_retinopathy_binary r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_above_50th_percentile_by_gender s ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = s."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_cohort_ratio cr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = cr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4.5: Map the boolean result to '1' or '0'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
         WHEN outcome_condition_met IS TRUE THEN '1'
         WHEN outcome_condition_met IS FALSE THEN '0'
         ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM temp_outcome_boolean_and;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening",
  sa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  dr."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_g_dl sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy dr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

**Issue:**
The problem statement defines the `cohort ratio` as "(respective race/Hispanic origin group/total count multiplied by 5)". The SQL interprets "total count" as `f."total_number_of_people_in_the_family"`, which typically refers to the number of people in an individual's household/family unit, not the total count of participants in the cohort or study. The "total count" in the context of a "cohort ratio" should refer to the total number of participants in the *final cohort* being analyzed.

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead. SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who meet diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing values in any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude male participants over 60 years of age
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 60);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."household_food_security_category" IN ('HH very low food security: 6-10 (HH w/o child) / 8-18 (HH w/ child)', 'HH low food security: 3-5 (HH w/o child) / 3-7 (HH w/ child)') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."household_food_security_category" = 'HH marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."household_food_security_category" = 'HH full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression outcome
-- Calculate total score from nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
                   WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
                   WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
                   WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
                   WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
                   ELSE 0 END, 0) +
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
                   WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
                   WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
                   WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
                   WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
                   ELSE 0 END, 0) +
       COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
                   WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
                   WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
                   WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
                   WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
                   ELSE 0 END, 0) +
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
                   WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
                   WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
                   WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
                   WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
                   ELSE 0 END, 0) +
       COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
                   WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
                   WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
                   WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
                   WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
                   ELSE 0 END, 0) +
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
                   WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
                   WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
                   WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
                   WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
                   ELSE 0 END, 0) +
       COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
                   WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
                   WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
                   WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
                   WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
                   ELSE 0 END, 0) +
       COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
                   WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
                   WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
                   WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
                   WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
                   ELSE 0 END, 0) +
       COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
                   WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
                   WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
                   WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
                   WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
                   ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "total_score_from_nine_questions"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Determine depression outcome based on total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "total_score_from_nine_questions" >= 10 THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fi."food_insecurity_level",
  d."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome d ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire, missing any of the depression questions:"have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself",  "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead. Exclude participants who are not in the set of individuals who cannot be determined to not violate the metabolic mood constraint, where the constraint is defined as having a fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) not greater than the age specific mean; participants above this mean are nevertheless retained unless it is simultaneously true that they report some level of "little interest in doing things" and are classified in the lowest category of the adult food security questionnaire.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline) OR (reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes) OR (fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126)

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead"
- (fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > age specific mean) AND (little interest in doing things = Several days OR little interest in doing things = More than half the days OR little interest in doing things = Nearly every day) AND (adult food security questionnaire = very low food security)

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  - severe food insecurity if adult food security question = very low food security OR adult food security question = low food security
  - mild food insecurity if adult food security question = marginal food security
  - food secure if adult food security question = full food security

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  - total score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead"
  - each question scored as:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
    - if value is null, assign 0

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: Determine diabetes status or glucose level for inclusion
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_status_inclusion AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline') THEN TRUE
        WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' THEN TRUE
        WHEN f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126 THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "has_diabetes_condition"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_inclusion_step1 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT d."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_diabetes_status_inclusion d
WHERE d."has_diabetes_condition" = TRUE;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL AND
    f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants who violate the metabolic mood constraint
-- Constraint violation: (fasting_glucose_mg_dl > age_in_years_at_screening) AND (little_interest_in_doing_things is frequent) AND (adult_food_security_category = 'AD very low food security: 6-10')
-- Frequent little_interest_in_doing_things is defined as 'Several days', 'More than half the days', 'Nearly every day'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
    f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > f."age_in_years_at_screening" AND
    f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IN ('Several days', 'More than half the days', 'Nearly every day') AND
    f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD very low food security: 6-10'
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: Depression outcome
-- Score each of the nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_scored_depression_questions AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_q1",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_q2",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_q3",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_q4",
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_q5",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_q6",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_q7",
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_q8",
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 END, 0) AS "score_q9"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total score from the nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    ("score_q1" + "score_q2" + "score_q3" + "score_q4" + "score_q5" + "score_q6" + "score_q7" + "score_q8" + "score_q9") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_scored_depression_questions;

-- Determine depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN '1'
        ELSE '0'
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tas."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tg."gender",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  tfil."food_insecurity_level",
  tdo."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level tfil ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tfil."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome tdo ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdo."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;
AI Complementary Review:
The SQL is mostly correct, but there is one significant error in the exclusion criteria for the "metabolic mood constraint".

**Where it was wrong:**

The problem states: "having a fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) not greater than the age specific mean".
The SQL implements this as: `f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > f."age_in_years_at_screening"`.

This is incorrect. "age specific mean" refers to the average fasting plasma glucose for individuals of a particular age, not the participant's age value itself. Calculating an "age specific mean" would require a separate aggregation step (e.g., `AVG(fasting_glucose_mg_dl) GROUP BY age_in_years_at_screening`) and then comparing the individual's glucose to that calculated mean. The current SQL compares a glucose level (mg/dl) directly to an age (years), which is not what the problem describes.

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire, missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself",  "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead. Exclude participants whose fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) <120 but reported borderline to ever being told by a doctor they have diabetes.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.

    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
    - If value is null: 0

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who meet diabetes or high glucose criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing adult food security category
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with any missing depression question
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants whose fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) <120 but reported borderline to ever being told by a doctor they have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" < 120 AND f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline');

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression outcome
-- Step 1: Map each of the nine depression question responses to a numerical score (0-3), treating null as 0.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_interest,
    CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_depressed,
    CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_sleep,
    CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_energy,
    CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_appetite,
    CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_self_bad,
    CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_concentrating,
    CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_movement,
    CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS score_dead
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Total score = sum of the nine individual depression question scores.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_total_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (score_interest + score_depressed + score_sleep + score_energy + score_appetite + score_self_bad + score_concentrating + score_movement + score_dead) AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Step 3 & 4: Assign 1 if Total score is 10 or greater, otherwise assign 0. Map to labels '1' or '0'.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN '1'
        ELSE '0'
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  gender."gender",
  race."race_hispanic_origin",
  food_insec."food_insecurity_level",
  dep_out."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender gender ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = gender."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin race ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = race."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level food_insec ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = food_insec."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome dep_out ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dep_out."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 Include individuals whose age in years at screening is 35 or older who take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar but still show a fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) above 110 mg/dl and who have a non-missing response to “feeling down, depressed, or hopeless”; among this subgroup, include only those whose item score (0=Not at all, 1=Several days, 2=More than half the days, 3=Nearly every day) is greater than the subgroup median.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age in years at screening >= 35
- take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
- fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 110
- "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" is not missing
- "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" score > median("feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" score) within the subgroup defined by the above criteria.
  The "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" score is mapped as:
    Not at all: 0
    Several days: 1
    More than half the days: 2
    Nearly every day: 3

Exclusion Criteria:
- adult food security questionaire is missing
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question reported "very low food security" OR "low food security"
    - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question reported "marginal food security"
    - food secure: if adult food security question reported "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  Total score is calculated from the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    1. "have little interest in doing things"
    2. "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    3. "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    4. "feeling tired or having little energy"
    5. "poor appetite or overeating"
    6. "feeling bad about yourself"
    7. "trouble concentrating on things"
    8. "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    9. "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question's score is mapped as:
    Not at all: 0
    Several days: 1
    More than half the days: 2
    Nearly every day: 3
  If a question's value is null, assign 0.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes individuals whose age in years at screening is 35 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 35;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include those who take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include those who still show a fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) above 110 mg/dl
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 110;

-- STEP 1.4: From the above group, include those who have a non-missing response to “feeling down, depressed, or hopeless”
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.5: Calculate the item score for "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" for the current subgroup
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_feeling_down_score AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL -- Should not happen due to previous IS NOT NULL filter
       END AS "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score"
FROM temp_inclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 1.6: Calculate the median of the "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" score for the current subgroup
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_feeling_down_score AS
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score") AS median_score
FROM temp_inclusion_feeling_down_score;

-- STEP 1.7: From the above group, include only those whose item score is greater than the subgroup median
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_feeling_down_score s, temp_median_feeling_down_score m
WHERE s."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score" > m.median_score;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the 9 depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: depression outcome
-- Step 1: Score for 'have little interest in doing things' (0-3), 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_q1 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Score for 'feeling down, depressed, or hopeless' (0-3), 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_q2 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 3: Score for 'trouble sleeping or sleeping too much' (0-3), 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_q3 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4: Score for 'feeling tired or having little energy' (0-3), 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_q4 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 5: Score for 'poor appetite or overeating' (0-3), 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_q5 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 6: Score for 'feeling bad about yourself' (0-3), 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_q6 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 7: Score for 'trouble concentrating on things' (0-3), 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_q7 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 8: Score for 'moving or speaking slowly or too fast' (0-3), 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_q8 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 9: Score for 'thought you would be better off dead' (0-3), 0 if null.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_q9 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 10: Total score is the sum of the 9 individual question scores.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    q1."respondent_sequence_number",
    (q1."score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things" +
     q2."score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" +
     q3."score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" +
     q4."score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" +
     q5."score_poor_appetite_or_overeating" +
     q6."score_feeling_bad_about_yourself" +
     q7."score_trouble_concentrating_on_things" +
     q8."score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" +
     q9."score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_score_q1 q1
JOIN temp_score_q2 q2 ON q1."respondent_sequence_number" = q2."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_q3 q3 ON q1."respondent_sequence_number" = q3."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_q4 q4 ON q1."respondent_sequence_number" = q4."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_q5 q5 ON q1."respondent_sequence_number" = q5."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_q6 q6 ON q1."respondent_sequence_number" = q6."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_q7 q7 ON q1."respondent_sequence_number" = q7."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_q8 q8 ON q1."respondent_sequence_number" = q8."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_q9 q9 ON q1."respondent_sequence_number" = q9."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 11 & 12: Condition for depression: total score is 10 or greater, map to 1 or 0.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN s."total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score s;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fi."food_insecurity_level",
  d."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome d ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity metric, defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question: categorized as 0 for who reported very low food security or low food security, 1 for  who reported marginal food security, and 3 for who reported full food security. Then mutiply this number by 100.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not. Multiply the total score by 2.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = "Yes" OR doctor told you have diabetes = "Borderline") OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = "Yes" OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things"
- missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
- missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
- missing "feeling tired or having little energy"
- missing "poor appetite or overeating"
- missing "feeling bad about yourself"
- missing "trouble concentrating on things"
- missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
- missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity metric calculated as:
  If adult food security question = "very low food security" or "low food security":
    food insecurity metric = 0
  If adult food security question = "marginal food security":
    food insecurity metric = 1
  If adult food security question = "full food security":
    food insecurity metric = 3
  Then, food insecurity metric = food insecurity metric * 100

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if (total score * 2) >= 10; 0 otherwise
  Total score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question scored as:
    Not at all = 0
    Several days = 1
    More than half the days = 2
    Nearly every day = 3
  If value is null, assign 0.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes related conditions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing values in "have little interest in doing things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling tired or having little energy"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing values in "poor appetite or overeating"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling bad about yourself"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble concentrating on things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing values in "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with missing values in "thought you would be better off dead"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food insecurity metric
-- Assign intermediate values based on adult food security question
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_intermediate AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 0
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 1
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL -- Should not happen due to exclusion, but good practice
  END AS "intermediate_food_insecurity_metric"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate final food insecurity metric
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_metric AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       ("intermediate_food_insecurity_metric" * 100) AS "food_insecurity_metric"
FROM temp_food_insecurity_intermediate;

-- 4.5 Depression outcome
-- Score each of the nine depression questions, assigning 0 for NULLs
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END, 0) AS "score_interest",
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END, 0) AS "score_depressed",
  COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END, 0) AS "score_sleep",
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END, 0) AS "score_energy",
  COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END, 0) AS "score_appetite",
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END, 0) AS "score_bad_self",
  COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END, 0) AS "score_concentrating",
  COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END, 0) AS "score_movement",
  COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END, 0) AS "score_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total score from the nine questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_total_score AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       ("score_interest" + "score_depressed" + "score_sleep" + "score_energy" + "score_appetite" + "score_bad_self" + "score_concentrating" + "score_movement" + "score_dead") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores_raw;

  CASE
    WHEN ("total_score" * 2) >= 10 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_total_score;

  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fim."food_insecurity_metric",

JOIN temp_depression_outcome t_do ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_do."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Calculate the threshold for glycohemoglobin based on age.
-- NOTE: The schema's derivation description for 'threshold_for_glycohemoglobin' includes 'ratio of their respective race/Hispanic orgin in all categories'.
-- However, the column 'race_hispanic_origin_w_nh_asian' has string examples and cannot be directly multiplied numerically.
-- Therefore, the 'ratio' part of the formula is omitted from the calculation due to schema ambiguity and lack of numerical mapping.
-- The threshold is calculated as (0.1 * age in years at screening).
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_glycohemoglobin_threshold AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (0.1 * "age_in_years_at_screening") AS "threshold_for_glycohemoglobin"
FROM final_master_table;

-- STEP 1.2: Include individuals whose glycohemoglobin (%) level is not less than the calculated threshold.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_glycohemoglobin_threshold t ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = t."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" >= t."threshold_for_glycohemoglobin";

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude people who did not say 'Yes' to "doctor told you have diabetes".
-- This means keeping only those who said 'Yes'.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing "serum albumin (g/dL)".
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing "diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy".
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age at screening (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Serum albumin (g/dL) (Predictor)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quantile cutoffs for serum albumin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.20) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS p20,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.80) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS p80
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin categories (Low, Normal, High)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_categorized AS
SELECT
    s."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT p20 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Low: <20th percentile'
        WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" >= (SELECT p20 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) AND s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT p80 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Normal: ≥20th percentile and <80th percentile'
        WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" >= (SELECT p80 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'High: ≥80th percentile'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.4 Diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy (Outcome)
-- Map "diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy" to 1 or 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_retinopathy_outcome AS
SELECT
