SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who meet diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing adult_food_security_category
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing have_little_interest_in_doing_things
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing poor_appetite_or_overeating
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing feeling_bad_about_yourself
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients with missing trouble_concentrating_on_things
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude patients with missing moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude patients with missing thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening (covariate)
-- Categorize age into custom bins
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 30 THEN '<30'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 31 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 40 THEN '31-40'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 40 THEN '40>'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender (covariate)
-- Extract gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin (covariate)
-- Map race/hispanic origin to 'white' or 'else'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    ELSE 'else'
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food insecurity level (predictor)
-- Map adult food security category to food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression outcome (outcome)
-- Step 1: Map 'have little interest in doing things' to score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_1 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Map 'feeling down, depressed, or hopeless' to score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_2 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 3: Map 'trouble sleeping or sleeping too much' to score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_3 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4: Map 'feeling tired or having little energy' to score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_4 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 5: Map 'poor appetite or overeating' to score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_5 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "poor_appetite_or_overeating_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 6: Map 'feeling bad about yourself' to score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_6 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "feeling_bad_about_yourself_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 7: Map 'trouble concentrating on things' to score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_7 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "trouble_concentrating_on_things_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 8: Map 'moving or speaking slowly or too fast' to score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_8 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 9: Map 'thought you would be better off dead' to score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score_9 AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END, 0) AS "thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 10: Calculate total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_total_score AS
SELECT
  s1."respondent_sequence_number",
  (s1."have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score" +
   s2."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score" +
   s3."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score" +
   s4."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score" +
   s5."poor_appetite_or_overeating_score" +
   s6."feeling_bad_about_yourself_score" +
   s7."trouble_concentrating_on_things_score" +
   s8."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score" +
   s9."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_score_1 s1
JOIN temp_depression_score_2 s2 ON s1."respondent_sequence_number" = s2."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_score_3 s3 ON s1."respondent_sequence_number" = s3."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_score_4 s4 ON s1."respondent_sequence_number" = s4."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_score_5 s5 ON s1."respondent_sequence_number" = s5."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_score_6 s6 ON s1."respondent_sequence_number" = s6."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_score_7 s7 ON s1."respondent_sequence_number" = s7."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_score_8 s8 ON s1."respondent_sequence_number" = s8."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_score_9 s9 ON s1."respondent_sequence_number" = s9."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 11: Map total score to depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fi."food_insecurity_level",
  d."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fi."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_depression_outcome d ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 Only include participants who are not determined to be ineligible, where ineligibility is defined as having a combined score for "feeling tired" and "poor appetite" below the age group specific mean when age group is categorized as age in years at screening <30 and >= 30; participants below that mean are nevertheless included unless their fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) is below 126 and they are not taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- NOT (
    (combined score for "feeling tired" and "poor appetite" < age group specific mean)
    AND (fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) < 126)
    AND (NOT taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar)
  )
  where:
    - age group is categorized as:
      - age in years at screening < 30
      - age in years at screening >= 30
    - combined score for "feeling tired" and "poor appetite" is calculated as:
      score for "feeling tired" + score for "poor appetite"
    - age group specific mean is the mean of the combined score for "feeling tired" and "poor appetite" within each age group

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
  - "have little interest in doing things"
  - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
  - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
  - "feeling tired or having little energy"
  - "poor appetite or overeating"
  - "feeling bad about yourself"
  - "trouble concentrating on things"
  - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
  - "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  - severe food insecurity: if reported "very low food security" or "low food security" in adult food security question
  - mild food insecurity: if reported "marginal food security" in adult food security question
  - food secure: if reported "full food security" in adult food security question

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score from nine depression questions >= 10; 0 otherwise
  where:
    - total score is the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
      - "have little interest in doing things"
      - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
      - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
      - "feeling tired or having little energy"
      - "poor appetite or overeating"
      - "feeling bad about yourself"
      - "trouble concentrating on things"
      - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
      - "thought you would be better off dead"
    - each question is scored as:
      - Not at all: 0
      - Several days: 1
      - More than half the days: 2
      - Nearly every day: 3
      - if value is null: 0

-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Map categorical scores for 'feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy' to numerical values
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_feeling_tired_score_mapped AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  "age_in_years_at_screening",
  "fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
  "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar",
  CASE
    WHEN "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0 -- As per schema, if value is null assign 0 for scoring
  END AS "feeling_tired_score"
FROM final_master_table;

-- STEP 1.2: Map categorical scores for 'poor_appetite_or_overeating' to numerical values
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_poor_appetite_score_mapped AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN "poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN "poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN "poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0 -- As per schema, if value is null assign 0 for scoring
  END AS "poor_appetite_score"
FROM final_master_table;

-- STEP 1.3: Calculate combined score for feeling tired and poor appetite and categorize age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_combined_score_and_age_group AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."age_in_years_at_screening",
  f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
  f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar",
  (ft."feeling_tired_score" + pa."poor_appetite_score") AS "combined_score_for_tired_appetite",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 30 THEN 'age in years at screening < 30'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30 THEN 'age in years at screening >= 30'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_group"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_feeling_tired_score_mapped ft ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = ft."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_poor_appetite_score_mapped pa ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = pa."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 1.4: Calculate age group specific mean of combined score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group_specific_mean AS
SELECT
  "age_group",
  AVG("combined_score_for_tired_appetite") AS "age_group_specific_mean"
FROM temp_combined_score_and_age_group
WHERE "age_group" IS NOT NULL -- Only calculate mean for valid age groups
GROUP BY "age_group";

-- STEP 1.5: Apply the inclusion criteria logic
-- Inclusion criteria: NOT ((combined score < age group specific mean) AND (fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) < 126) AND (NOT taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar))
-- This is equivalent to: (combined score >= age group specific mean) OR (fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >= 126) OR (taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = 'Yes')
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_combined_score_and_age_group c
LEFT JOIN temp_age_group_specific_mean m ON c."age_group" = m."age_group"
WHERE
  (c."combined_score_for_tired_appetite" >= m."age_group_specific_mean" OR m."age_group_specific_mean" IS NULL) -- Handle cases where age_group_specific_mean might be null if age_group is null
  OR (c."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= 126)
  OR (c."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes');

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL AND
  f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL AND
  f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL AND
  f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL AND
  f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL AND
  f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL AND
  f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL AND
  f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL AND
  f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age_in_years_at_screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race_hispanic_origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: food_insecurity_level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
    WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: depression_outcome
-- 4.5.1 Map scores for each of the nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0 -- Excluded missing values, but for robustness, assign 0
  END AS "score_interest",
  CASE
    WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS "score_depressed",
  CASE
    WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS "score_sleep",
  CASE
    WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS "score_tired",
  CASE
    WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS "score_appetite",
  CASE
    WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS "score_bad_self",
  CASE
    WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS "score_concentrating",
  CASE
    WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS "score_moving",
  CASE
    WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS "score_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5.2 Calculate total score from nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  ("score_interest" + "score_depressed" + "score_sleep" + "score_tired" + "score_appetite" + "score_bad_self" + "score_concentrating" + "score_moving" + "score_dead") AS "total_depression_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- 4.5.3 Determine depression outcome based on total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "total_depression_score" >= 10 THEN '1'
    ELSE '0'
  END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fi."food_insecurity_level",
  dep_out."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome dep_out ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dep_out."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

  ("score_interest" + "score_depressed" + "score_sleep" + "score_tired" + "score_appetite" + "score_bad_self" + "score_concentrating" + "score_moving" + "score_dead") AS "total_depression_score"

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS

  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE

    ELSE '0'

  age."age_in_years_at_screening",

JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 Include participants who are not excluded from the eligible cohort, where a participant is excluded only if it is not true that they cannot be determined to not satisfy both of the following requirements: age in years at screening is not less than 20, and diabetes is not absent, where diabetes is considered not absent if the participant does not fail to meet at least one of these criteria: doctor told you have diabetes questionnaire response is yes or borderline, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, or fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) is not less than 126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.

Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

- missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  - severe food insecurity: adult food security question = "very low food security" OR "low food security"
  - mild food insecurity: adult food security question = "marginal food security"
  - food secure: adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  - total score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"

    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"

    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
  - Each question's score is assigned as:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
    - If value is null = 0

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants aged 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include participants with diabetes present.
-- Diabetes is considered present if:
-- doctor_told_you_have_diabetes questionnaire response is 'Yes' or 'Borderline'
-- OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar is 'Yes'
-- OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL) is >= 126
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
    OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
    OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
    OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with any missing depression question responses
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Race Hispanic Origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Food Insecurity Level
-- Categorized as severe food insecurity, mild food insecurity, or food secure
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL -- Should not be reached due to exclusion criteria
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: Depression Outcome
-- Step 4.5.1: Assign scores for each depression question (0-3, NULL=0)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_question_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 -- Default to 0 for any non-mapped values, though excluded NULLs
    END, 0) AS "have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "poor_appetite_or_overeating_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "feeling_bad_about_yourself_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "trouble_concentrating_on_things_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score",
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.2: Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    "have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score" +
    "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score" +
    "trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score" +
    "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score" +
    "poor_appetite_or_overeating_score" +
    "feeling_bad_about_yourself_score" +
    "trouble_concentrating_on_things_score" +
    "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score" +
    "thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score" AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_question_scores;

-- Step 4.5.3: Determine final depression outcome (1 if total score >= 10, else 0)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  gen."gender",
  race."race_hispanic_origin",
  fi."food_insecurity_level",
  dep."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender gen ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = gen."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin race ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = race."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome dep ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dep."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.

Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin, gender, and age at screening (<40, 41-50, and >50.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who meet any of the diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT f."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table f
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
   OR f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (binned)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 40 THEN '<40'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 41 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 50 THEN '41-50'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 50 THEN '>50'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5)  WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25'
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" > (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) AND r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25 and <=50'
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" > (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) AND r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50 and <=75'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- Calculate eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr_raw AS

       CASE

               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
               141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018

           ELSE NULL
       END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g)

-- Determine presence of kidney disease

       CASE

           ELSE 0
       END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c

LEFT JOIN temp_uacr_raw u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS

  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  o."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include individuals whose fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) is not missing
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       "fasting_glucose_mmol_l",
       "age_in_years_at_screening",
       "race_hispanic_origin",
       "total_number_of_people_in_the_household"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate count of participant's race/hispanic origin group within the current cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_group_counts AS
SELECT
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    COUNT("respondent_sequence_number") AS participant_race_group_count
FROM temp_inclusion_step1
WHERE "race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 1.3: Calculate fpg threshold value for each participant
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_fpg_threshold_value AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number",
    (i."age_in_years_at_screening" / 5.0) * (COALESCE(rc.participant_race_group_count, 0) / NULLIF(i."total_number_of_people_in_the_household", 0)) AS fpg_threshold_value
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
LEFT JOIN temp_race_group_counts rc ON i."race_hispanic_origin" = rc."race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 1.4: Include individuals whose fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) is not less than fpg threshold value
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN temp_fpg_threshold_value t ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = t."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE i."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= t.fpg_threshold_value;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Determine if diabetes related condition is met for each participant
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_condition_met AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR
     f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0 OR
     f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5 OR
     f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes') AS diabetes_related_condition_met
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients who do not have diabetes (i.e., diabetes_related_condition_met is FALSE)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT d."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_diabetes_condition_met d
WHERE d.diabetes_related_condition_met IS TRUE;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: age at screening (years)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening_years AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening_years"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: benchmark abc
-- STEP 4.4.1: Calculate WWI (waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight (kg)))
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."waist_circumference_cm",
       f."weight_kg",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening",
       (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "wwi"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- STEP 4.4.2: Categorize age into age groups
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       "wwi",
       "age_in_years_at_screening",
       CASE
           WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" < 30 THEN 'group 1'
           WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30 AND "age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN 'group 2'
           WHEN "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN 'group 3'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_group"
FROM temp_wwi;

-- STEP 4.4.3: Calculate 70th percentile of WWI for each age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_p70_wwi_by_age_group AS
SELECT
    "age_group",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.70) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi") AS p70_wwi
FROM temp_age_group
WHERE "wwi" IS NOT NULL AND "age_group" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "age_group";

-- STEP 4.4.4: Calculate number of participants for each age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_size_by_age_group AS
SELECT
    "age_group",
    COUNT("respondent_sequence_number") AS group_size
FROM temp_age_group
WHERE "age_group" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "age_group";

-- STEP 4.4.5: Determine the size of the largest age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_largest_group_size AS
SELECT MAX("group_size") AS largest_group_size
FROM temp_size_by_age_group;

-- STEP 4.4.6: Calculate benchmark abc
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_benchmark_abc AS
SELECT
    ag."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN ag."age_group" = 'group 1' AND p70_1.p70_wwi IS NOT NULL AND s1.group_size IS NOT NULL AND lgs.largest_group_size IS NOT NULL AND lgs.largest_group_size > 0 THEN (ag."wwi" / p70_1.p70_wwi) * (s1.group_size / lgs.largest_group_size::NUMERIC)
        WHEN ag."age_group" = 'group 2' AND p70_2.p70_wwi IS NOT NULL AND s2.group_size IS NOT NULL AND lgs.largest_group_size IS NOT NULL AND lgs.largest_group_size > 0 THEN (ag."wwi" / p70_2.p70_wwi) * (s2.group_size / lgs.largest_group_size::NUMERIC)
        WHEN ag."age_group" = 'group 3' AND p70_3.p70_wwi IS NOT NULL AND s3.group_size IS NOT NULL AND lgs.largest_group_size IS NOT NULL AND lgs.largest_group_size > 0 THEN (ag."wwi" / p70_3.p70_wwi) * (s3.group_size / lgs.largest_group_size::NUMERIC)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "benchmark_abc"
FROM temp_age_group ag
LEFT JOIN temp_p70_wwi_by_age_group p70_1 ON ag."age_group" = p70_1."age_group" AND ag."age_group" = 'group 1'
LEFT JOIN temp_p70_wwi_by_age_group p70_2 ON ag."age_group" = p70_2."age_group" AND ag."age_group" = 'group 2'
LEFT JOIN temp_p70_wwi_by_age_group p70_3 ON ag."age_group" = p70_3."age_group" AND ag."age_group" = 'group 3'
LEFT JOIN temp_size_by_age_group s1 ON ag."age_group" = s1."age_group" AND ag."age_group" = 'group 1'
LEFT JOIN temp_size_by_age_group s2 ON ag."age_group" = s2."age_group" AND ag."age_group" = 'group 2'
LEFT JOIN temp_size_by_age_group s3 ON ag."age_group" = s3."age_group" AND ag."age_group" = 'group 3'
CROSS JOIN temp_largest_group_size lgs
WHERE ag."wwi" IS NOT NULL;

-- 4.5 Outcome: presence of kidney disease
-- STEP 4.5.1: Calculate uacr (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_urine_mg_l",
       f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl",
       (100.0 * (f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / NULLIF(f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl", 0))) AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" > 0;

-- STEP 4.5.2: Map uacr (mg/g) to presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN '1'
           ELSE '0'
       END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_uacr;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  rho."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  age."age_at_screening_years",
  babc."benchmark_abc",
  pkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin rho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = rho."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening_years age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_benchmark_abc babc ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = babc."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease pkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = pkd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

The SQL uses `total_number_of_people_in_the_household` as the denominator for this ratio, which is a measure of household size, not a ratio related to race/Hispanic origin categories within the cohort.

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire, missing any of the depression questions:"have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself",  "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead. Exclude participants whose reported frequency of "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" is higher than the mean frequency calculated within their specific adult food security questionnaire category.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline) OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things" OR missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" OR missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much" OR missing "feeling tired or having little energy" OR missing "poor appetite or overeating" OR missing "feeling bad about yourself" OR missing "trouble concentrating on things" OR missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast" OR missing "thought you would be better off dead"
- reported frequency of "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" > mean frequency of "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" calculated within their specific adult food security questionnaire category

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR adult food security question = "low food security"
  - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
  - food secure: if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  - total score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead"
  - each question scored as:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
  - if value is null, assign 0

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: Determine diabetes status for inclusion
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_status_raw AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline') THEN TRUE
        WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' THEN TRUE
        WHEN f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126 THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "has_diabetes"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_inclusion_step1 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_diabetes_status_raw
WHERE "has_diabetes" = TRUE;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Check for missing PHQ-9 questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_missing_check AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NULL OR
     f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NULL OR
     f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NULL OR
     f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NULL OR
     f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NULL OR
     f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NULL OR
     f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NULL OR
     f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NULL OR
     f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NULL) AS "any_phq9_missing"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step1 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with any missing PHQ-9 questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN temp_phq9_missing_check pmc ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = pmc."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE pmc."any_phq9_missing" = FALSE;

-- STEP 2.4: Score 'feeling down, depressed, or hopeless' for exclusion comparison
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_feeling_down_score AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."adult_food_security_category",
    CASE
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 -- Assign 0 for NULL as per outcome derivation, though previous step excludes actual NULLs
    END AS "feeling_down_score"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step2 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 2.5: Calculate mean frequency of 'feeling down, depressed, or hopeless' grouped by 'adult food security category'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_feeling_down_by_food_security AS
SELECT
    "adult_food_security_category",
    AVG("feeling_down_score") AS "mean_feeling_down_score"
FROM temp_phq9_feeling_down_score
GROUP BY "adult_food_security_category";

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants whose reported frequency of "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" is higher than the mean frequency calculated within their specific adult food security questionnaire category
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_phq9_feeling_down_score s
JOIN temp_mean_feeling_down_by_food_security m
    ON s."adult_food_security_category" = m."adult_food_security_category"
WHERE s."feeling_down_score" <= m."mean_feeling_down_score";

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
        WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
        WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: depression outcome
-- Step 1: Score each of the nine PHQ-9 questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_scored_questions AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0 -- As per schema, assign 0 for NULL
    END AS "scored_have_little_interest_in_doing_things",
    CASE
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS "scored_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless",
    CASE
        WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS "scored_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much",
    CASE
        WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS "scored_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy",
    CASE
        WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS "scored_poor_appetite_or_overeating",
    CASE
        WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS "scored_feeling_bad_about_yourself",
    CASE
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS "scored_trouble_concentrating_on_things",
    CASE
        WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS "scored_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
    CASE
        WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END AS "scored_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate total score from the nine PHQ-9 questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_total_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (
        "scored_have_little_interest_in_doing_things" +
        "scored_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" +
        "scored_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" +
        "scored_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" +
        "scored_poor_appetite_or_overeating" +
        "scored_feeling_bad_about_yourself" +
        "scored_trouble_concentrating_on_things" +
        "scored_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" +
        "scored_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    ) AS "total_score"
FROM temp_phq9_scored_questions;

-- Step 3: Map total score to depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN '1'
        WHEN "total_score" < 10 THEN '0'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_phq9_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tas."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tg."gender",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  tfil."food_insecurity_level",
  tdo."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level tfil ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tfil."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome tdo ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdo."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire, missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself",  "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead. Exclude participants whose frequency of "trouble concentrating on things" is greater than the gender specific median frequency calculated among all inclusion criteria eligible participants aged less than 60 years.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.

  Each question is mapped as:
    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
    - If value is null: 0
