SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who meet diabetes criteria
-- Diabetes criteria: doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR Borderline
-- OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes
-- OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
       OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
       OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants missing any of the nine depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants whose frequency of "trouble concentrating on things" is greater than the gender specific median frequency
-- First, define the subpopulation for median calculation: inclusion criteria eligible AND age < 60
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_subpopulation AS
SELECT f."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender",
       f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
FROM final_master_table f
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20
  AND (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
       OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
       OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126)
  AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60;

-- Map 'trouble_concentrating_on_things' to numerical scores for median calculation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_subpopulation_mapped AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       "gender",
       CASE
           WHEN "trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN "trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN "trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN "trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "trouble_concentrating_score"
FROM temp_median_subpopulation
WHERE "trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL; -- Only consider non-null values for median

-- Calculate gender-specific median frequency scores
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_median_frequency AS
SELECT "gender",
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "trouble_concentrating_score") AS "median_score"
FROM temp_median_subpopulation_mapped
GROUP BY "gender";

-- Now apply the exclusion to the current cohort (temp_exclusion_step2)
-- Keep participants whose mapped score is less than or equal to the median, or if the score is null (though nulls should be excluded by step 2.2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender_median_frequency m ON f."gender" = m."gender"
WHERE
    CASE
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END <= m."median_score"
    OR f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food insecurity level (Predictor)
-- Map adult_food_security_category to food insecurity levels
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression outcome (Outcome)
-- Map each of the nine depression questions to a numerical score (0-3), with NULLs assigned 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL END, 0) AS score_q1,
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL END, 0) AS score_q2,
       COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL END, 0) AS score_q3,
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL END, 0) AS score_q4,
       COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL END, 0) AS score_q5,
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL END, 0) AS score_q6,
       COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL END, 0) AS score_q7,
       COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL END, 0) AS score_q8,
       COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL END, 0) AS score_q9
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total score by summing the mapped scores
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       (score_q1 + score_q2 + score_q3 + score_q4 + score_q5 + score_q6 + score_q7 + score_q8 + score_q9) AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Determine depression outcome: 1 if total score >= 10, else 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  t_age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  t_gender."gender",
  t_race."race_hispanic_origin",
  t_food_insecurity."food_insecurity_level",
  t_depression_outcome."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening t_age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender t_gender ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_gender."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin t_race ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_race."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level t_food_insecurity ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_food_insecurity."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome t_depression_outcome ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_depression_outcome."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 Only include participants whose waist circumference (cm) divided by weight (kg) is greater than 0.4 and whose serum creatinine (mg/dL) is less than 2.0.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Calculate waist circumference (cm) / weight (kg) and include participants where it is greater than 0.4
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table f
WHERE
  f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" != 0 AND
  (f."waist_circumference_cm" / f."weight_kg") > 0.4;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those whose serum creatinine (mg/dL) is less than 2.0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_mg_dl" < 2.0;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL OR f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who were pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing values of weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing values of waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
  f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Mexican American', 'Non-Hispanic Black', 'Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'non white'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
    ELSE '>=60'
  END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio (waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight (kg)), then quartiles)
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign wwi ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT
  r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: ≤25'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25 and ≤50'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50 and ≤75'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- Calculate eGFR (ml/min/1.73m^2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate UACR (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (100.0 * (f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl")) AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0;

-- Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN (e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" < 60) OR (u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30) THEN '1'
    ELSE '0'
  END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tg."gender",
  ta."age_at_screening",
  tw."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  tp."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio tw ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tw."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease tp ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tp."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Identify participants aged 30 or older for median HbA1c calculation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_30_plus_for_median AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    "glycohemoglobin",
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    "gender",
    "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM
    final_master_table
WHERE
    "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate median glycoprotein HbA1c (%) among participants of the same race/Hispanic origin and gender who were 30 years or older at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_hba1c_subgroup AS
SELECT
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    "gender",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "glycohemoglobin") AS "median_glycoprotein_hba1c_subgroup"
FROM
    temp_age_30_plus_for_median
GROUP BY
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    "gender";

-- STEP 1.3: Include all individuals whose age in years at screening is 30 years or older AND whose glycoprotein HbA1c (%) is not less than the calculated median
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    final_master_table f
JOIN
    temp_median_hba1c_subgroup m ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = m."race_hispanic_origin" AND f."gender" = m."gender"
WHERE
    f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30
    AND f."glycohemoglobin" >= m."median_glycoprotein_hba1c_subgroup";

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status (borderline and missing/low HbA1c)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT
(
    f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
    AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5)
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM
    temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM
    temp_cohort c
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM
    temp_cohort c
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic Origin (Covariate)
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to 'white', 'black', 'mexican american', 'other'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM
    temp_cohort c
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Serum Albumin (g/dL) (Predictor)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM
    temp_cohort c
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM
    temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT
    s."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
        WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25th percentile'
        WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25th percentile and <50th percentile'
        WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50th percentile and <75th percentile'
        ELSE 'Q4: ≥75th percentile'
    END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM
    temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.5 Diabetic Retinopathy (Outcome)
-- Map diabetic retinopathy status to 1 for 'Yes' and 0 for 'No'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
        WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM
    temp_cohort c
JOIN
    final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    g."gender",
    a."age_in_years_at_screening",
    r."race_hispanic_origin",
    sa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
    dr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM
    temp_cohort c
JOIN
    temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN
    temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN
    temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN
    temp_serum_albumin_g_dl sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN
    temp_diabetic_retinopathy dr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, categorized as <30, 31-40, and 40>, gender, race/Hispanic origin.

    <30

    40>
- gender

- food insecurity level defined based on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as:
    severe food insecurity: if reported "very low food security" or "low food security"
    mild food insecurity: if reported "marginal food security"
    food secure: if reported "full food security"

  Total score calculated from the sum of scores from the following nine questions:

  Each question value mapped as:

    More than half the days = 2
    Nearly every day = 3
  If value is null, assign 0.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome is coded as 1 for participants who cannot be classified as remaining in the protected metabolic renal state. The protected metabolic renal state is defined as follows. Participants must have a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR), calculated as 100 times urine albumin (mg/L) divided by urine creatinine (mg/dL), that is below the race/Hispanic origin specific median UACR calculated among participants in the cohort. This is conditional on satisfying this renal requirement, the participant must also have both fasting plasma glucose (mmol/L) below the gender specific 75th percentile of fasting plasma glucose and glycoprotein HbA1c (%) below the gender specific 75th percentile of HbA1c, with both percentiles calculated among the participants in the cohort. Participants who fail to satisfy either level of the protected state are coded as 1 unless it is simultaneously true that they are aged 60 years or older and are taking diabetes medication, in which case they are reclassified as 0. All remaining participants are coded as 0.
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- diabetes defined as:
    doctor told you have diabetes = Yes
    OR
    fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
    OR
    glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5%
    OR
    takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes

Exclusion Criteria:
- age at screening < 20
- pregnant at the exam = Yes
- missing weight (kg)
- missing waist circumference (cm)
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing urine albumin (mg/L)
- missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
- missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)

Covariates:
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
    white
    non white
- gender
- age at screening categorized as:
    <60
    >=60

Predictor:
- weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio calculated as:
    waist circumference (cm) / square root of weight (kg)
  categorized as quartiles:
    Q1: <=25%
    Q2: >25% and <=50%
    Q3: >50% and <=75%
    Q4: >75%

Outcome:
- outcome = 1 if cannot be classified as remaining in the protected metabolic renal state; 0 otherwise
  Protected metabolic renal state is defined as TRUE if:
    (UACR < race/Hispanic origin specific median UACR)
    AND
    (fasting plasma glucose (mmol/L) < gender specific 75th percentile of fasting plasma glucose)
    AND
    (glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) < gender specific 75th percentile of HbA1c)
  OR
    (NOT ((UACR < race/Hispanic origin specific median UACR)
    AND
    (fasting plasma glucose (mmol/L) < gender specific 75th percentile of fasting plasma glucose)
    AND
    (glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) < gender specific 75th percentile of HbA1c)))
    AND
    (age at screening >= 60 AND takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes)

  Where:
  - UACR = 100 * urine albumin (mg/L) / urine creatinine (mg/dL)
  - race/Hispanic origin specific median UACR is calculated among participants in the cohort.
  - gender specific 75th percentile of fasting plasma glucose is calculated among participants in the cohort.
  - gender specific 75th percentile of HbA1c is calculated among participants in the cohort.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Identify participants who reported 'Yes' to doctor told you have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_diabetes_condition1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.2: Identify participants with fasting plasma glucose (FPG) >= 7.0 mmol/L
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_diabetes_condition2 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0;

-- STEP 1.3: Identify participants with glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) >= 6.5%
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_diabetes_condition3 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5;

-- STEP 1.4: Identify participants who take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_diabetes_condition4 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.5: Combine all diabetes conditions using OR logic
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_diabetes_condition1
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_diabetes_condition2
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_diabetes_condition3
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_diabetes_condition4;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who are pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" != 'Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam';

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Mexican American', 'Non-Hispanic Black', 'Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'non white'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" > 0; -- Ensure weight is positive for SQRT

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q3
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_binned AS
SELECT
  r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Protected metabolic renal state
-- Step 1: Calculate UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" > 0; -- Ensure creatinine is positive to avoid division by zero

-- Step 2: Calculate race/Hispanic origin specific median UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_race_median_uacr AS
SELECT
  f."race_hispanic_origin",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY u."uacr") AS "median_uacr"
FROM temp_outcome_uacr_raw u
JOIN final_master_table f ON u."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 3: Calculate gender specific 75th percentile of fasting plasma glucose
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_gender_fpg_75th_percentile AS
SELECT
  f."gender",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l") AS "fpg_75th_percentile"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."gender";

-- Step 4: Calculate gender specific 75th percentile of HbA1c
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_gender_hba1c_75th_percentile AS
SELECT
  f."gender",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."glycohemoglobin") AS "hba1c_75th_percentile"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."gender";

-- Step 5: Determine components for protected metabolic renal state
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_protected_state_components AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (u.uacr < r.median_uacr) AS condition_A,
  (f.fasting_glucose_mmol_l < g_fpg.fpg_75th_percentile) AS condition_B,
  (f.glycohemoglobin < g_hba1c.hba1c_75th_percentile) AS condition_C,
  (f.age_in_years_at_screening >= 60) AS condition_D,
  (f.take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar = 'Yes') AS condition_E,
  f.age_in_years_at_screening,
  f.take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_uacr_raw u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_race_median_uacr r ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = r."race_hispanic_origin"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_gender_fpg_75th_percentile g_fpg ON f."gender" = g_fpg."gender"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_gender_hba1c_75th_percentile g_hba1c ON f."gender" = g_hba1c."gender";

-- Step 6: Calculate protected metabolic renal state
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_protected_state AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  (
    (condition_A AND condition_B AND condition_C)
    OR
    (NOT (condition_A AND condition_B AND condition_C) AND (condition_D AND condition_E))
  ) AS "protected_metabolic_renal_state"
FROM temp_outcome_protected_state_components;

-- Step 7: Map outcome based on protected metabolic renal state
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_final AS
SELECT
  s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."protected_metabolic_renal_state" IS TRUE THEN 0
    ELSE 1
  END AS "outcome"
FROM temp_outcome_protected_state s;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  o."outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_binned p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_final o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome is coded as 1 for participants who are not determined to belong to the low risk class. The low risk class is defined as follows: the participant must have a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR), calculated as 100 times urine albumin (mg/L) divided by urine creatinine (mg/dL), that is below the age group specific 70th percentile of UACR calculated among participants in the analytic cohort, and, conditional on meeting this albuminuria requirement, must also have a ratio of serum creatinine (mg/dL) to age at screening that is below the race/Hispanic origin specific median of this ratio calculated from the same cohort. Participants who do not satisfy this low risk class are coded as 1 unless it is additionally true that their fasting plasma glucose (mmol/L) is below 6.0 and their glycoprotein HbA1c (%) is below 6.5, in which case they are hierarchically reassigned to 0.
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- doctor told you have diabetes = Yes
OR
- fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
OR
- glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5%
OR
- takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar

Exclusion Criteria:
- age at screening < 20
- pregnant at the exam
- missing weight (kg)
- missing waist circumference (cm)
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing urine albumin (mg/L)
- missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
- missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)

Covariates:
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
    white
    non white
- gender
- age at screening categorized as:
    <60
    >=60

Predictor:
- weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio calculated as:
    waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight (kg))
  categorized into quartiles:
    Q1: <=25%
    Q2: >25% and <=50%
    Q3: >50% and <=75%
    Q4: >75%

Outcome:
- Outcome is binary (1 or 0)
- First, calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR):
    UACR = 100 * urine albumin (mg/L) / urine creatinine (mg/dL)
- Second, define `low risk class`:
    A participant is in `low risk class` if:
    (UACR < age group specific 70th percentile of UACR calculated among participants in the analytic cohort)
    AND
    (ratio of serum creatinine (mg/dL) to age at screening < race/Hispanic origin specific median of this ratio calculated from the same cohort)
- Third, initial outcome assignment:
    Outcome = 0 if `low risk class` is met
    Outcome = 1 if `low risk class` is NOT met
- Fourth, hierarchical reassignment:
    IF (Outcome = 1) AND (fasting plasma glucose (mmol/L) < 6.0) AND (glycoprotein HbA1c (%) < 6.5) THEN
        Outcome = 0

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who reported 'Yes' to doctor told you have diabetes OR
--           fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L OR
--           glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5% OR
--           takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = 'Yes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
   OR "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
   OR "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who are pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_covariate_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           ELSE 'non white'
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_covariate_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_covariate_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio to quartile groups
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Outcome (low risk class)
-- Step 1: Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" > 0;

-- Get age groups for UACR percentile calculation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_age_groups_for_uacr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_group"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate age group specific 70th percentile of UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr_percentiles AS
SELECT ag."age_group",
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.70) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY u."uacr") AS "uacr_70th_percentile"
FROM temp_outcome_uacr u
JOIN temp_outcome_age_groups_for_uacr ag ON u."respondent_sequence_number" = ag."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY ag."age_group";

-- Calculate ratio of serum creatinine (mg/dL) to age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_creatinine_ratio AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."creatinine_mg_dl" / f."age_in_years_at_screening") AS "creatinine_to_age_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 0;

-- Get race groups for creatinine ratio median calculation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_race_groups_for_creatinine AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           ELSE 'non white'
       END AS "race_group"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate race/Hispanic origin specific median of creatinine ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_creatinine_ratio_medians AS
SELECT rg."race_group",
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY cr."creatinine_to_age_ratio") AS "creatinine_ratio_median"
FROM temp_outcome_creatinine_ratio cr
JOIN temp_outcome_race_groups_for_creatinine rg ON cr."respondent_sequence_number" = rg."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY rg."race_group";

-- Step 2: Determine "low risk class"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_low_risk_class AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN u.uacr < up.uacr_70th_percentile
            AND cr.creatinine_to_age_ratio < crm.creatinine_ratio_median THEN TRUE
           ELSE FALSE
       END AS "low_risk_class"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_outcome_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_age_groups_for_uacr ag ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ag."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_uacr_percentiles up ON ag."age_group" = up."age_group"
JOIN temp_outcome_creatinine_ratio cr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = cr."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_race_groups_for_creatinine rg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = rg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_outcome_creatinine_ratio_medians crm ON rg."race_group" = crm."race_group";

-- Step 3: Initial outcome assignment
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_initial AS
SELECT lrc."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN lrc."low_risk_class" = TRUE THEN 0
           ELSE 1
       END AS "initial_outcome"
FROM temp_outcome_low_risk_class lrc;

-- Step 4: Hierarchical reassignment
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_final AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN i."initial_outcome" = 1
            AND f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" < 6.0
            AND f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5 THEN 0
           ELSE i."initial_outcome"
       END AS "outcome"
FROM temp_outcome_initial i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  cov_race."race_hispanic_origin",
  cov_gender."gender",
  cov_age."age_at_screening",
  pred_wwi."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  outc."outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_covariate_race_hispanic_origin cov_race ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = cov_race."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_covariate_gender cov_gender ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = cov_gender."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_covariate_age_at_screening cov_age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = cov_age."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_predictor_wwi_ratio pred_wwi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = pred_wwi."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_final outc ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = outc."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The predictor is a binary Indicator ABC, defined as 1 if the participant reports very low or low food security and also reports reports any frequency other than "Not at all" for question thoughts of being better off dead and 0 otherwise.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline) OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things" OR missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" OR missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much" OR missing "feeling tired or having little energy" OR missing "poor appetite or overeating" OR missing "feeling bad about yourself" OR missing "trouble concentrating on things" OR missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast" OR missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- Indicator ABC = 1 if (participant reports very low food security OR participant reports low food security) AND (question "thought you would be better off dead" is not "Not at all"); 0 otherwise

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  Total score calculated as the sum of the following nine questions, where each question is mapped as:
    Not at all = 0
    Several days = 1
    More than half the days = 2
    Nearly every day = 3
    If value is null, assign 0:
  - "have little interest in doing things"
  - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
  - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
  - "feeling tired or having little energy"
  - "poor appetite or overeating"
  - "feeling bad about yourself"
  - "trouble concentrating on things"
  - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
  - "thought you would be better off dead"
