SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes condition or treatment
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questionnaire items
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Indicator ABC
-- Step 1: Determine food security condition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_security_condition AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD very low food security: 6-10' OR f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD low food security: 3-5' THEN TRUE
           ELSE FALSE
       END AS "food_security_condition"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2 & 3: Determine Indicator ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_indicator_abc AS
SELECT tfs."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN tfs."food_security_condition" = TRUE AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" != 'Not at all' THEN '1'
           ELSE '0'
       END AS "indicator_abc"
FROM temp_food_security_condition tfs
JOIN final_master_table f ON tfs."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression outcome
-- Step 1: Map each of the nine depression questions to a numerical score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_q1,
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_q2,
       COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_q3,
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_q4,
       COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_q5,
       COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_q6,
       COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_q7,
       COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_q8,
       COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 END, 0) AS score_q9
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       (score_q1 + score_q2 + score_q3 + score_q4 + score_q5 + score_q6 + score_q7 + score_q8 + score_q9) AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Step 3: Map total score to depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN '1'
           ELSE '0'
       END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  pred."indicator_abc",
  outc."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_indicator_abc pred ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = pred."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome outc ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = outc."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is a capped weight-adjusted-waist ratio (cWWI), calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight (kg), with values above 20 set to 20 and values below 2 set to 2, used as a continuous variable.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR
- fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L OR
- glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5% OR
- takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar

Exclusion Criteria:
- age at screening < 20
- pregnant at the exam
- missing weight (kg)

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who meet any of the diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE
    "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
    OR "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
    OR "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
    OR "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who are pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" != 'Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam';

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: race/hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    ELSE 'non white'
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
    ELSE '>=60'
  END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: capped weight-adjusted-waist ratio (cwii)
-- Calculate raw cWWI
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cwii_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."waist_circumference_cm" / POWER(f."weight_kg", 0.5) AS "cwii_raw_value"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Apply capping to cWWI
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_capped_weight_adjusted_waist_ratio_cwii AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "cwii_raw_value" > 20 THEN 20.0
    WHEN "cwii_raw_value" < 2 THEN 2.0
    ELSE "cwii_raw_value"
  END AS "capped_weight_adjusted_waist_ratio_cwii"
FROM temp_cwii_raw;

-- 4.5 Outcome: presence of kidney disease
-- Step 1: Calculate eGFR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr_calc AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_calc AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  100.0 * (f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 3: Determine intermediate kidney disease condition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_intermediate_kidney_disease_condition AS
SELECT
  e."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m2" < 60 OR u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS "intermediate_kidney_disease_condition"
FROM temp_egfr_calc e
JOIN temp_uacr_calc u ON e."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4: Map intermediate condition to 1/0 for presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "intermediate_kidney_disease_condition" THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_intermediate_kidney_disease_condition;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  tg."gender",
  tas."age_at_screening",
  tcwii."capped_weight_adjusted_waist_ratio_cwii",
  tpkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_capped_weight_adjusted_waist_ratio_cwii tcwii ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tcwii."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease tpkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tpkd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is coded as 1 only if the participant meets two sequential conditions: first, the depression total score which is based on the total score from the nine depression questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3) must be more than 10. Among those meeting this score, the participants fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) must be greater than or equal to the gender specific median calculated among the cohort. Participants failing either step are coded as 0.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = "Yes" OR doctor told you have diabetes = "Borderline") OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = "Yes" OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things"
- missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
- missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
- missing "feeling tired or having little energy"
- missing "poor appetite or overeating"
- missing "feeling bad about yourself"
- missing "trouble concentrating on things"
- missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
- missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR adult food security question = "low food security"
  - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
  - food secure: if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- outcome = 1 if (depression total score > 10 AND fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >= gender specific median of fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl)); 0 otherwise
  - depression total score calculated as the sum of the following nine questions, where each question is mapped:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
    - Questions:
      - "have little interest in doing things"
      - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
      - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
      - "feeling tired or having little energy"
      - "poor appetite or overeating"
      - "feeling bad about yourself"
      - "trouble concentrating on things"
      - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
      - "thought you would be better off dead"

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes or high fasting plasma glucose
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing values in "have little interest in doing things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling tired or having little energy"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing values in "poor appetite or overeating"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling bad about yourself"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble concentrating on things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing values in "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with missing values in "thought you would be better off dead"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: outcome
-- Step 1: Map each depression question response to a numeric score.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
       f."gender",
       CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things",
       CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless",
       CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much",
       CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy",
       CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating",
       CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself",
       CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things",
       CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
       CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
           WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate depression total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_total_score AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       "fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
       "gender",
       (COALESCE("score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things", 0) +
        COALESCE("score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless", 0) +
        COALESCE("score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much", 0) +
        COALESCE("score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy", 0) +
        COALESCE("score_poor_appetite_or_overeating", 0) +
        COALESCE("score_feeling_bad_about_yourself", 0) +
        COALESCE("score_trouble_concentrating_on_things", 0) +
        COALESCE("score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast", 0) +
        COALESCE("score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead", 0)) AS "depression_total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Step 3: Calculate gender-specific median of fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl)
-- First, calculate gender-specific medians separately
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_medians AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "fasting_glucose_mg_dl") AS "median_fasting_glucose"
FROM temp_depression_total_score
GROUP BY "gender";

-- Then, join the medians to the main table
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_fasting_glucose_median AS
SELECT
    tdts."respondent_sequence_number",
    tdts."depression_total_score",
    tdts."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
    tdts."gender",
    tgm."median_fasting_glucose" AS "gender_specific_median_fasting_glucose"
FROM temp_depression_total_score tdts
JOIN temp_gender_medians tgm ON tdts."gender" = tgm."gender";

-- Step 4: Determine outcome_condition_met
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_condition_met AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "depression_total_score" > 10 AND "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= "gender_specific_median_fasting_glucose" THEN TRUE
           ELSE FALSE
       END AS "outcome_condition_met"
FROM temp_gender_fasting_glucose_median;

-- Step 5: Map outcome_condition_met to 1 or 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "outcome_condition_met" IS TRUE THEN 1

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg) multiplied by 2, categorized as binary using median(%) group1: ≤50, group2: >50.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Adjust the analysis for race/Hispanic origin (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who reported 'Yes' to doctor told you have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.2: Include participants with fasting plasma glucose (FPG) >= 7.0 mmol/L
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0;

-- STEP 1.3: Include participants with glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) >= 6.5%
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5;

-- STEP 1.4: Include participants who take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step4 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.5: Combine all inclusion criteria (OR logic)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step1
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step2
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step3
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step4;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    ELSE 'non white'
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."waist_circumference_cm" / (SQRT(f."weight_kg") * 2.0) AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- Compute median for weight-adjusted-waist ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_median AS
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS median_val
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign binary groups for weight-adjusted-waist ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT
  r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT median_val FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_median) THEN 'group1: <= median(%)'
    WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" > (SELECT median_val FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_median) THEN 'group2: > median(%)'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- Calculate eGFR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_egfr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0; -- Avoid division by zero

-- Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN (e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" < 60) OR (u."urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g" >= 30) THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  o."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_predictor_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_presence_of_kidney_disease o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .
 Consider race/Hispanic origin, gender, age at screening, and define the covariate Index ABC as follows: compute UACR (mg/g) as 100 times urine albumin (mg/L) divided by urine creatinine (mg/dL); compute a renal ratio as serum creatinine (mg/dL) divided by age at screening; define high albuminuria as UACR greater than or equal to the race/Hispanic origin specific 75th percentile of UACR calculated among inclusion-eligible participants; the covariate Index ABC is defined as renal ratio multiplied by glycoprotein HbA1c (%), and this value is not left unmodified unless it is not true that the participant cannot be determined to not belong to the high-albuminuria class; if the participant is treated as belonging to the high-albuminuria class, multiply the covariate Index ABC by 2 for participants who are not younger than 60 years and multiply by 1 otherwise.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2007-2008

Inclusion Criteria:
- diabetes defined as:
    - doctor told you have diabetes = Yes
    OR
    - fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
    OR
    - glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5%
    OR
    - takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar

Exclusion Criteria:
- age at screening < 20
- pregnant at the exam
- missing weight (kg)
- missing waist circumference (cm)
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing urine albumin (mg/L)
- missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
- missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)

Covariates:
- race/Hispanic origin
- gender
- age at screening
- Index ABC calculated as:
  1. Calculate Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) = 100 * urine albumin (mg/L) / urine creatinine (mg/dL)
  2. Calculate renal ratio = serum creatinine (mg/dL) / age at screening
  3. Define high albuminuria: UACR (mg/g) >= race/Hispanic origin specific 75th percentile of UACR (mg/g) calculated among inclusion-eligible participants.
  4. Initial Index ABC = renal ratio * glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%)
  5. If participant is determined to belong to the high-albuminuria class:
     - If age at screening >= 60 years, then Index ABC = Initial Index ABC * 2
     - Else (age at screening < 60 years), then Index ABC = Initial Index ABC * 1
  6. Else (participant is not determined to belong to the high-albuminuria class), then Index ABC = Initial Index ABC

Predictor:
- weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio calculated as:
  waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight (kg))
  categorized into quartiles:
    Q1: <=25%
    Q2: >25% and <=50%
    Q3: >50% and <=75%
    Q4: >75%

Outcome:
- presence of kidney disease = 1 if (eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 OR UACR (mg/g) >= 30 mg/g); 0 otherwise
  - eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2) calculated as:
    - If gender = female AND race/Hispanic origin = Non-Hispanic Black:
      141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
    - If gender = female AND race/Hispanic origin != Non-Hispanic Black:
      141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
    - If gender = male AND race/Hispanic origin = Non-Hispanic Black:
      141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
    - If gender = male AND race/Hispanic origin != Non-Hispanic Black:
      141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
  - Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) calculated as:
    100 * (urine albumin (mg/L) / urine creatinine (mg/dL))

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Identify participants with diabetes based on any of the specified criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
   OR "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
   OR "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" != 'Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam';

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Index ABC
-- Step 4.4.1: Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (uacr) (mg/g) for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_cohort AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0;

-- Step 4.4.2: Calculate race/Hispanic origin specific 75th percentile of UACR among inclusion-eligible participants
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_percentiles AS
SELECT
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "uacr_mg_g") AS "p75_uacr"
FROM temp_uacr_cohort
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 4.4.3: Determine high albuminuria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_high_albuminuria AS
SELECT
    tuc."respondent_sequence_number",
    tuc."uacr_mg_g",
    tuc."race_hispanic_origin",
    CASE
        WHEN tuc."uacr_mg_g" >= tp."p75_uacr" THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "high_albuminuria"
FROM temp_uacr_cohort tuc
JOIN temp_uacr_percentiles tp ON tuc."race_hispanic_origin" = tp."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 4.4.4: Calculate renal ratio and initial index abc
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_initial_index_abc_calc AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."creatinine_mg_dl",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening",
    f."glycohemoglobin",
    (f."creatinine_mg_dl" / f."age_in_years_at_screening") AS "renal_ratio",
    (f."creatinine_mg_dl" / f."age_in_years_at_screening") * f."glycohemoglobin" AS "initial_index_abc"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" != 0;

-- Step 4.4.5: Calculate final Index ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_index_abc AS
SELECT
    tiic."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN tha."high_albuminuria" = TRUE AND tiic."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN tiic."initial_index_abc" * 2
        WHEN tha."high_albuminuria" = TRUE AND tiic."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN tiic."initial_index_abc" * 1
        WHEN tha."high_albuminuria" = FALSE THEN tiic."initial_index_abc"
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "index_abc"
FROM temp_initial_index_abc_calc tiic
JOIN temp_high_albuminuria tha ON tiic."respondent_sequence_number" = tha."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Step 4.5.1: Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- Step 4.5.2: Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Step 4.5.3: Assign wwi ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
    WHEN "wwi_ratio" > (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) AND "wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
    WHEN "wwi_ratio" > (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) AND "wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- 4.6 Presence of kidney disease
-- Step 4.6.1: Calculate eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender",
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    f."creatinine_mg_dl",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening",
    CASE
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.6.2: Determine presence of kidney disease (boolean)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_kidney_disease_boolean AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN t_egfr."egfr_ml_min_1_73m2" < 60 OR t_uacr."uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "has_kidney_disease_boolean"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_egfr t_egfr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_egfr."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_uacr_cohort t_uacr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_uacr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.6.3: Map to '1' or '0'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "has_kidney_disease_boolean" = TRUE THEN '1'
        WHEN "has_kidney_disease_boolean" = FALSE THEN '0'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_kidney_disease_boolean;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tro."race_hispanic_origin",
  tg."gender",
  tas."age_at_screening",
  tia."index_abc",
  tww."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  tpkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tro ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tro."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_index_abc tia ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tia."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio tww ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tww."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease tpkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tpkd."respondent_sequence_number";

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 30 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with non-missing serum albumin (g/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with non-missing diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients with age in years at screening less than 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude patients with age when first told diabetes less than 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude patients with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5));

-- STEP 2.11: Exclude patients with log-transformed Metric ABC less than or equal to 0
-- First, calculate Metric ABC for the current cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_metric_abc_for_exclusion AS
SELECT
    i."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."albumin_g_dl" / f."glycohemoglobin") *
    (CASE
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' THEN 1.1
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' THEN 0.9
        ELSE NULL
    END) AS "metric_abc_val"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Then, exclude based on the calculated metric_abc_val
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step11 AS
SELECT m."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_metric_abc_for_exclusion m
WHERE m."metric_abc_val" IS NOT NULL AND m."metric_abc_val" > 0;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step11;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening
-- Extract age in years at screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to specified categories
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Log-transformed Metric ABC (Predictor)
-- Calculate Metric ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_metric_abc AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."albumin_g_dl" / f."glycohemoglobin") *
    (CASE
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' THEN 1.1
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' THEN 0.9
        ELSE NULL
    END) AS "metric_abc_val"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate log-transformed Metric ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_log_transformed_metric_abc AS
SELECT
    m."respondent_sequence_number",
    LN(m."metric_abc_val") AS "log_transformed_metric_abc"
FROM temp_metric_abc m
WHERE m."metric_abc_val" IS NOT NULL AND m."metric_abc_val" > 0; -- Ensure positive value for LN

-- 4.5 Diabetic Retinopathy (Outcome)
-- Map diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy to 1/0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
    WHEN 'Yes' THEN 1
    WHEN 'No' THEN 0
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  l."log_transformed_metric_abc",
  d."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_log_transformed_metric_abc l ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = l."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy d ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;
AI Complementary Review:
The SQL is incorrect.

The exclusion for "participants with log-transformed Metric ABC less than or equal to 0 will be excluded" is not correctly implemented.
`temp_exclusion_step11` filters `WHERE m."metric_abc_val" IS NOT NULL AND m."metric_abc_val" > 0;`. This condition only ensures that the `metric_abc_val` is positive, allowing the `LN` function to be applied without error. However, it does not exclude participants where the *log-transformed* value is less than or equal to 0.
To correctly implement this, the condition should be `WHERE LN(m."metric_abc_val") > 0` or, equivalently, `WHERE m."metric_abc_val" > 1`.

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire, missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself",  "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead. Exclude participants who are not in the set of individuals who cannot be determined to not violate the glucose security constraint, where the constraint is defined as having fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) not less than the mean glucose within their adult food security questionnaire group; participants below this mean are nevertheless retained unless their frequency of "feeling tired or having little energy" exceeds the 75th percentile for their age group when age group is defined as age in years at screening <=50 and >50.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- has diabetes defined as meeting any of the following:
    - doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline
    - reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes
    - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead
- participants where:
    - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) < mean(fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl)) within their adult food security questionnaire group
    AND
    - frequency of "feeling tired or having little energy" > 75th percentile for their age group, where age group is defined as:
        - age in years at screening <= 50
        - age in years at screening > 50

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity if adult food security question = very low food security OR adult food security question = low food security
    - mild food insecurity if adult food security question = marginal food security
    - food secure if adult food security question = full food security

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  total score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead
  Each question's value is assigned as:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
    - If value is null, assign 0
