SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: Determine 'doctor_diagnosis_or_borderline' and 'has_diabetes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2_diabetes_conditions AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS "doctor_diagnosis_or_borderline",
  CASE
    WHEN (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline')
      OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
      OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126 THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS "has_diabetes"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_inclusion_step1 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those who 'has_diabetes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT d."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2_diabetes_conditions d
WHERE d."has_diabetes" = TRUE;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security question
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."hh_adults_cut_size_or_skip_meals" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Determine if any of the depression questions are missing
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2_depression_missing AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number",
  (f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NULL OR
   f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NULL OR
   f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NULL OR
   f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NULL OR
   f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NULL OR
   f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NULL OR
   f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NULL OR
   f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NULL OR
   f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NULL) AS "missing_any_depression_questions"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step1 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN temp_exclusion_step2_depression_missing dm ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = dm."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE dm."missing_any_depression_questions" = FALSE;

-- STEP 2.4: Prepare for complex exclusion condition
-- Assign numerical scores for 'feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy' for percentile calculation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_complex_exclusion_prep_tired_scores AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL -- Should not be NULL due to previous exclusion, but for safety
  END AS "feeling_tired_score"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step3 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate mean fasting glucose by adult food security group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_complex_exclusion_prep_mean_glucose AS
SELECT
  f."hh_adults_cut_size_or_skip_meals",
  AVG(f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl") AS "mean_fasting_glucose_by_food_security_group"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step3 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."hh_adults_cut_size_or_skip_meals" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY f."hh_adults_cut_size_or_skip_meals";

-- Define age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_complex_exclusion_prep_age_group AS
SELECT
  f."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 50 THEN 'age in years at screening <= 50'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 50 THEN 'age in years at screening > 50'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_group"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_exclusion_step3 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate 75th percentile of 'feeling_tired_score' by age group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_complex_exclusion_prep_p75_tired AS
SELECT
  ag."age_group",
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY ts."feeling_tired_score") AS "p75_tired_by_age_group"
FROM temp_complex_exclusion_prep_tired_scores ts
JOIN temp_complex_exclusion_prep_age_group ag ON ts."respondent_sequence_number" = ag."respondent_sequence_number"

Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75.
 The outcome variable will be retinopathy risk status, coded as 1 if the participant answered yes to diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy and their glycoprotein HbA1c (%) is not less than the 75th percentile of  glycoprotein HbA1c (%) calculated among study participants of the same gender and whose age in years at screening is greater than or equal to 60, and coded as 0 otherwise.
 We will adjust for gender, race/Hispanic origin, age in years at screening, and two glycemic covariates: (1) an age standardized glycoprotein HbA1c (%) covariate defined as the participants glycoprotein HbA1c (%) minus the mean glycoprotein HbA1c (%) among study participants whose age in years at screening is greater than or equal to 45 and less than or equal to 59, and (2) a race standardized HbA1c indicator defined as whether the participants HbA1c (%) is greater than or equal to the median HbA1c (%) within their race/Hispanic origin group.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include all patients whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 30);

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" < 30);

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
-- Uncertainty is based on their answer to "Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?" is "borderline"
-- AND either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value OR an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
    f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
    AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5)
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Race Hispanic Origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Age standardized glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
-- Calculate mean glycoprotein HbA1c (%) among study participants whose age in years at screening >= 45 AND age in years at screening <= 59
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_hba1c_age_group AS
SELECT AVG(f."glycohemoglobin") AS mean_hba1c_45_59
FROM final_master_table f
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 45
  AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 59
  AND f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- Calculate age standardized glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."glycohemoglobin" - (SELECT mean_hba1c_45_59 FROM temp_mean_hba1c_age_group)) AS "age_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Race standardized HbA1c indicator
-- Calculate median glycoprotein HbA1c (%) within each race hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_hba1c_race_group AS
SELECT f."race_hispanic_origin",
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."glycohemoglobin") AS median_hba1c
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Determine if participant's HbA1c (%) is greater than or equal to the median HbA1c (%) within their race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_standardized_hba1c_indicator AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."glycohemoglobin" >= m.median_hba1c THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "race_standardized_hba1c_indicator"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_median_hba1c_race_group m ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = m."race_hispanic_origin";

-- 4.6 Serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Extract raw serum albumin for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25% and <50%'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50% and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.7 Retinopathy risk status
-- Calculate 75th percentile of glycoprotein HbA1c (%) among study participants of the same gender and whose age in years at screening >= 60
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_75th_percentile_gender_age_group AS
SELECT f."gender",
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."glycohemoglobin") AS hba1c_75th_percentile
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60
  AND f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY f."gender";

-- Determine retinopathy risk status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_retinopathy_risk_status AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes'
                AND f."glycohemoglobin" >= p.hba1c_75th_percentile THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "retinopathy_risk_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_hba1c_75th_percentile_gender_age_group p ON f."gender" = p."gender";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening",
  ash."age_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c",
  rsh."race_standardized_hba1c_indicator",
  sa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  rrs."retinopathy_risk_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_standardized_glycoprotein_hba1c ash ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ash."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_standardized_hba1c_indicator rsh ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = rsh."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_g_dl sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_retinopathy_risk_status rrs ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = rrs."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The predictor is Index ABC defined as the individual food security score divided by the gender specific mean score calculated among the eligible cohort, and then divided again by the age group specific median (aged <60 vs ≥60) calculated from the same eligible cohort. The individual food security score is derived from the adult food security questionnaire, categorized as very low (0), low (1), marginal (2), or full food security (3).
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline) OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- Index ABC calculated as:
  (individual food security score / gender specific mean score) / age group specific median
  where:
    - individual food security score is derived from the adult food security questionnaire, categorized as:
        - very low (0)
        - low (1)
        - marginal (2)
        - full food security (3)
    - gender specific mean score is calculated among the eligible cohort
    - age group specific median is calculated among the eligible cohort, for age groups:
        - <60 years
        - >=60 years

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  where total score is calculated from the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead
  Each question is scored as:
    - Not at all (0)
    - Several days (1)
    - More than half the days (2)
    - Nearly every day (3)
  If value is null, assign (0).

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: Derive diabetes or high glucose condition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_condition AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "diabetes_diagnosis_or_borderline",
    CASE
        WHEN (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline')
             OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
             OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126.0 THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "diabetes_or_high_glucose_condition"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_inclusion_step1 i ON f."respondent_sequence_number" = i."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those who meet the diabetes or high glucose condition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN temp_diabetes_condition d ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE d."diabetes_or_high_glucose_condition" = TRUE;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionnaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing values in "have little interest in doing things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling tired or having little energy"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing values in "poor appetite or overeating"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing values in "feeling bad about yourself"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing values in "trouble concentrating on things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing values in "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with missing values in "thought you would be better off dead"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Index ABC
-- Step 4.4.1: Derive individual food security score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_individual_food_security_score AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE f."adult_food_security_category"
        WHEN 'AD very low food security: 6-10' THEN 0
        WHEN 'AD low food security: 3-5' THEN 1
        WHEN 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 2
        WHEN 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "individual_food_security_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.4.2: Calculate gender specific mean score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_specific_mean_score AS
SELECT
    f."gender",
    AVG(ifs."individual_food_security_score") AS "gender_specific_mean_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_individual_food_security_score ifs ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ifs."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."gender";

-- Step 4.4.3: Derive age group for median calculation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60 years'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60 years'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_group"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.4.4: Calculate age group specific median
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_group_specific_median AS
SELECT
    ag."age_group",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY ifs."individual_food_security_score") AS "age_group_specific_median"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_individual_food_security_score ifs ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ifs."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_group ag ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ag."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY ag."age_group";

-- Step 4.4.5: Calculate Index ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_index_abc AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (ifs."individual_food_security_score" / gsms."gender_specific_mean_score") / agsm."age_group_specific_median" AS "index_abc"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_individual_food_security_score ifs ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ifs."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN final_master_table f_gender ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f_gender."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender_specific_mean_score gsms ON f_gender."gender" = gsms."gender"
JOIN temp_age_group ag ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ag."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_group_specific_median agsm ON ag."age_group" = agsm."age_group";

-- 4.5 Outcome: Depression Outcome
-- Step 4.5.1-9: Score each depression question, assigning 0 for NULLs
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_little_interest",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_down",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_trouble_sleeping",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_tired",
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_poor_appetite",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_bad",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_trouble_concentrating",
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_moving_speaking",
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END, 0) AS "score_better_off_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.10: Calculate total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    ("score_little_interest" + "score_feeling_down" + "score_trouble_sleeping" + "score_feeling_tired" +
     "score_poor_appetite" + "score_feeling_bad" + "score_trouble_concentrating" + "score_moving_speaking" +
     "score_better_off_dead") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- Step 4.5.11: Derive depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  idx."index_abc",
  dep."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_index_abc idx ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = idx."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome dep ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dep."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

 Only include participants who has information on gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin mg/L, urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl), weight (kg), waist circumference (cm).

 Define the predictor benchmark ABC as the participant s weight-adjusted waist ratio (WWI), calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight (kg), divided by the 70th percentile of WWI within the participants age group, where age groups are defined as younger than 30 years, 30 years or older and younger than 60 years, and 60 years or older, and then multiplied by the ratio of the size of the participants age group to the size of the largest age group in the cohort.
 The outcome is the presence of kidney disease, defined as a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater  when the urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).
 Consider race/Hispanic origin, gender, and age at screening in the analysis.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants with non-null gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include those with non-null race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include those with non-null urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.4: From the above group, include those with non-null urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.5: From the above group, include those with non-null serum creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.6: From the above group, include those with non-null weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.7: From the above group, include those with non-null waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients who do not have diabetes
-- Diabetes is defined as meeting any of the criteria:
-- doctor told you have diabetes = 'Yes'
-- OR fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
-- OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5%
-- OR take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = 'Yes'
-- We keep patients who meet any of these criteria (i.e., have diabetes).
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes')
   OR (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0)
   OR (f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5)
   OR (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes');

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: predictor benchmark abc
-- Step 1: Calculate weight-adjusted waist ratio (WWI) and assign age groups
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_abc_wii_age_group AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "wii",
    f."age_in_years_at_screening",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 30 THEN 'younger than 30 years'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '30 years or older and younger than 60 years'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '60 years or older'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_group"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- Step 2: Calculate 70th percentile of WWI and size for each age group, and the largest age group size
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_abc_cohort_stats AS
SELECT
    "age_group",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.70) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wii") AS "wii_70th_percentile",
    COUNT("respondent_sequence_number") AS "age_group_size"
FROM temp_predictor_abc_wii_age_group
GROUP BY "age_group";

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_largest_age_group_size AS
SELECT MAX("age_group_size") AS "largest_age_group_size"
FROM temp_predictor_abc_cohort_stats;

-- Step 3: Calculate Predictor benchmark ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_benchmark_abc AS
SELECT
    t1."respondent_sequence_number",
    (t1."wii" / t2."wii_70th_percentile") * (t2."age_group_size"::NUMERIC / (SELECT "largest_age_group_size" FROM temp_largest_age_group_size)) AS "predictor_benchmark_abc"
FROM temp_predictor_abc_wii_age_group t1
JOIN temp_predictor_abc_cohort_stats t2 ON t1."age_group" = t2."age_group";

-- 4.5 Outcome: presence of kidney disease
-- Step 1: Calculate UACR (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    100.0 * (f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0; -- Avoid division by zero

-- Step 2: Define presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_uacr;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  p."predictor_benchmark_abc",
  k."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_predictor_benchmark_abc p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease k ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = k."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age when first told the patient had diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients if age in years at screening is less than 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude patients if age when first told the patient had diabetes is less than 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude patients if age when first told the patient had diabetes is greater than age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" <= f."age_in_years_at_screening";

-- STEP 2.11: Exclude patients if diabetes status is uncertain
-- First, determine condition_glycohemoglobin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_condition_glycohemoglobin AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5) AS "condition_glycohemoglobin"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Then apply the boolean AND for diabetes status uncertain
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step11 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_condition_glycohemoglobin cg ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = cg."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND cg."condition_glycohemoglobin");

-- STEP 2.12: Exclude patients if glycohemoglobin HbA1c (%) is outside the allowable range of 3.0 to 20.0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step12 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step11 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" >= 3.0 AND f."glycohemoglobin" <= 20.0;

-- STEP 2.13: Exclude patients if serum albumin (g/dL) is outside the allowable range of 1.0 to 6.0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step13 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step12 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" >= 1.0 AND f."albumin_g_dl" <= 6.0;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step13;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening
-- Extract age in years at screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to specified categories
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Hispanic' THEN 'other'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial' THEN 'other'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25 and <50%'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50 and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- 4.5 Diabetic retinopathy
-- Map diabetic retinopathy status to 1 for yes and 0 for no
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
    WHEN 'Yes' THEN 1
    WHEN 'No' THEN 0
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  sa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  dr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy dr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening more than 30 and less than 60, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126..
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening > 30 AND age at screening < 60
- (doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline) OR (taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = Yes) OR (fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126)

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things" OR missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" OR missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much" OR missing "feeling tired or having little energy" OR missing "poor appetite or overeating" OR missing "feeling bad about yourself" OR missing "trouble concentrating on things" OR missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast" OR missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  severe food insecurity: adult food security question = "very low food security" OR adult food security question = "low food security"
  mild food insecurity: adult food security question = "marginal food security"
  food secure: adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  total score calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    "have little interest in doing things",
    "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless",
    "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much",
    "feeling tired or having little energy",
    "poor appetite or overeating",
    "feeling bad about yourself",
    "trouble concentrating on things",
    "moving or speaking slowly or too fast",
    "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question is scored as:
    Not at all = 0
    Several days = 1
    More than half the days = 2
    Nearly every day = 3
  If a question's value is null, assign 0.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients aged between 30 and 60 (exclusive)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" > 30
  AND "age_in_years_at_screening" < 60;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who have diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IN ('Yes', 'Borderline')
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security category
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with any missing PHQ9 depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" IN ('AD very low food security: 6-10', 'AD low food security: 3-5') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Depression outcome
-- Calculate scores for each PHQ9 question
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    COALESCE(CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy",
    COALESCE(CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating",
    COALESCE(CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself",
    COALESCE(CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things",
    COALESCE(CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
    COALESCE(CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE 0
    END, 0) AS "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total PHQ9 score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_total_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (
        "score_have_little_interest_in_doing_things" +
        "score_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" +
        "score_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" +
        "score_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" +
        "score_poor_appetite_or_overeating" +
        "score_feeling_bad_about_yourself" +
        "score_trouble_concentrating_on_things" +
        "score_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" +
        "score_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    ) AS "total_score"
FROM temp_phq9_scores;

-- Map total score to depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN '1'
        ELSE '0'
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_phq9_total_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  fi."food_insecurity_level",
  d."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening age ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_food_insecurity_level fi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = fi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome d ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2012. 
 The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy.
 Exclude participants if any required field is missing (serum albumin, glycoprotein HbA1c (%), diabetic retinopathy status, gender, race/Hispanic origin, age at screening, or age when first told diabetes. Participants will also be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
 The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75.
 The outcome variable will be elevated diabetic retinopathy risk, coded as 1 for participants who are not classified as lacking evidence of diabetes-related eye disease, where lack of evidence is defined as answering no to diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy or having glycoprotein HbA1c (%) below the 75th percentile of HbA1c (%) calculated within the participants race/Hispanic origin group using the analytic cohort; all other participants are coded as 0.
 We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/Hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age in years at screening >= 30 years
- complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
- complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing serum albumin
- missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
- missing diabetic retinopathy status
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing age at screening
- missing age when first told diabetes
- age in years at screening < 30 years old
- age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
- uncertain diabetes status, defined as:
    - “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” = "borderline" AND (missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) < 6.5%)

Covariates:

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those with complete data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include only those with complete data on diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5));

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening
-- Extract age in years at screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to specified categories
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Extract serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5)  WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25 and <50'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50 and <75'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- 4.5 Elevated diabetic retinopathy risk
-- Step 1: Calculate 75th percentile of glycoprotein HbA1c (%%) within each race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_percentiles_by_race_calc AS
SELECT
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."glycohemoglobin") AS "hba1c_75th_percentile_by_race"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 2: Join Percentiles to Cohort Data
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_percentiles AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."glycohemoglobin",
    f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy",
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    hpc."hba1c_75th_percentile_by_race"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_hba1c_percentiles_by_race_calc hpc ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = hpc."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 3: Determine elevated diabetic retinopathy risk_boolean
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk_boolean AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' AND "glycohemoglobin" >= "hba1c_75th_percentile_by_race" THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk_boolean"
FROM temp_hba1c_percentiles;

-- Step 4: Map boolean result to 1 or 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk_boolean" = TRUE THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk"
FROM temp_elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk_boolean;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tsa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tedr."elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_serum_albumin_g_dl tsa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsa."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk tedr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tedr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;
