-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5));

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening
-- Extract age in years at screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to specified categories
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Extract serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl" AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5)  WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "serum_albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25 and <50'
    WHEN "serum_albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50 and <75'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- 4.5 Elevated diabetic retinopathy risk
-- Step 1: Calculate 75th percentile of glycoprotein HbA1c (%%) within each race/Hispanic origin group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_percentiles_by_race_calc AS
SELECT
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."glycohemoglobin") AS "hba1c_75th_percentile_by_race"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 2: Join Percentiles to Cohort Data
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_percentiles AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."glycohemoglobin",
    f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy",
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    hpc."hba1c_75th_percentile_by_race"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_hba1c_percentiles_by_race_calc hpc ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = hpc."race_hispanic_origin";

-- Step 3: Determine elevated diabetic retinopathy risk_boolean
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk_boolean AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' AND "glycohemoglobin" >= "hba1c_75th_percentile_by_race" THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk_boolean"
FROM temp_hba1c_percentiles;

-- Step 4: Map boolean result to 1 or 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk_boolean" = TRUE THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk"
FROM temp_elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk_boolean;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  ta."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tsa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tedr."elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening ta ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ta."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_serum_albumin_g_dl tsa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsa."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_elevated_diabetic_retinopathy_risk tedr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tedr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Pre-computation for diabetes related exclusion: Calculate race/Hispanic origin specific median HbA1c (%) from the post-inclusion cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_hba1c_medians AS
SELECT
    f."race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY f."glycohemoglobin") AS "median_hba1c"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY f."race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 2.2: Identify participants who meet the diabetes-status inconsistency criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_inconsistency_candidates AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_hba1c_medians m ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = m."race_hispanic_origin"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
  AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < m."median_hba1c");

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants identified in temp_diabetes_inconsistency_candidates
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
WHERE i."respondent_sequence_number" NOT IN (SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_diabetes_inconsistency_candidates);

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing diabetes affected eyes/had retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic Origin (Covariate)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           ELSE 'other'
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Serum Albumin (g/dL) (Predictor)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile groups
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT
  ar."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN ar."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN ar."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN ar."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25 and <50%'
    WHEN ar."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50 and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_albumin_raw ar;

-- 4.5 Diabetic Retinopathy (Outcome)
-- Map diabetic retinopathy status to 1 or 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  alb."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  dr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_albumin_quartiles alb ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = alb."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy dr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
 The outcome is coded as 1 for participants who cannot be classified as "Low Symptom" defined as having a depression total score (which is based on the total score from the nine depression questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3)) below the gender specific 75th percentile. Participants failing this are coded as 1 unless it is simultaneously true that they are aged <50 and their fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) is below the 50th percentile for the "Full" Food Security group, in which case they are reassigned to 0.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline) OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: reported very low food security OR low food security
    - mild food insecurity: reported marginal food security
    - food secure: reported full food security

Outcome:
- outcome = 1 if (depression total score >= gender specific 75th percentile) AND NOT (age at screening < 50 AND fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) < 50th percentile for the "Full" Food Security group); 0 otherwise
  where:
    - depression total score is calculated as the sum of scores from the nine depression questions, with each question scored as:
        - Not at all: 0
        - Several days: 1
        - More than half the days: 2
        - Nearly every day: 3
    - gender specific 75th percentile is the 75th percentile of the depression total score, calculated separately for each gender.
    - 50th percentile for the "Full" Food Security group is the 50th percentile of fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) among participants categorized as "food secure".

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age at screening >= 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: Includes patients who meet any of the diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline')
   OR (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes')
   OR (f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing values in adult food security category
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing values in "have little interest in doing things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing values in "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing values in "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing values in "feeling tired or having little energy"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing values in "poor appetite or overeating"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing values in "feeling bad about yourself"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients with missing values in "trouble concentrating on things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude patients with missing values in "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude patients with missing values in "thought you would be better off dead"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: race/hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: food insecurity level
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_food_insecurity_level AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."household_food_security_category" IN ('HH very low food security: 6-10 (HH w/o child) / 8-18 (HH w/ child)', 'HH low food security: 3-5 (HH w/o child) / 3-7 (HH w/ child)') THEN 'severe food insecurity'
           WHEN f."household_food_security_category" = 'HH marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 'mild food insecurity'
           WHEN f."household_food_security_category" = 'HH full food security: 0' THEN 'food secure'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Outcome: outcome
-- STEP 4.5.1: Map each depression question response to a score (0-3)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_question_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_interest_in_things",
    CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_down_depressed_hopeless",
    CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_trouble_sleeping",
    CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1

     COALESCE("score_trouble_sleeping", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_tired_little_energy", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_poor_appetite_overeating", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_bad_about_yourself", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_trouble_concentrating", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_moving_speaking_slowly_fast", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_better_off_dead", 0)) AS "depression_total_score"
FROM temp_depression_question_scores;

-- STEP 4.5.3.1: Calculate gender specific 75th percentile of depression total score for each gender

           ELSE NULL
       END AS "food_insecurity_level"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

JOIN temp_outcome_food_insecurity_level tfil ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tfil."respondent_sequence_number"

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 Only include participants whose fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) is not less than the median fasting plasma glucose calculated within their gender group among participants aged 20 years or older.
 We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm). We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl).
 The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
 The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159. 2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018. 3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159. 4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening). SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Identify a sub-population of participants with age at screening >= 20 for median FPG calculation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_age_20_plus AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    "gender",
    "fasting_glucose_mmol_l",
    "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20
  AND "gender" IS NOT NULL -- Ensure gender is not null for grouping
  AND "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NOT NULL; -- Ensure FPG is not null for median calculation

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate median FPG for males and females in the sub-population
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_fpg_by_gender AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "fasting_glucose_mmol_l") AS "median_fpg"
FROM temp_inclusion_age_20_plus
GROUP BY "gender";

-- STEP 1.3: Include participants whose fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) is not less than the median FPG calculated within their gender group among participants aged 20 years or older.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_median_fpg_by_gender mfg ON f."gender" = mfg."gender"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20
  AND f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."gender" IS NOT NULL
  AND (
        (f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= mfg."median_fpg")
     OR (f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= mfg."median_fpg")
  );

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants who are pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin (mapped to white/non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
        ELSE 'non white'
    END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (binned into <60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio (raw calculation)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0; -- Ensure weight is positive for SQRT

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for wwi ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign wwi ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_pred_wwi_ratio_quartiles AS
SELECT
    r."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN r."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
        WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: ≤25th percentile'
        WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25th percentile and ≤50th percentile'
        WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50th percentile and ≤75th percentile'
        ELSE 'Q4: >75th percentile'
    END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_pred_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease (eGFR calculation)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_egfr_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."gender" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- 4.6 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease (UACR calculation)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" > 0; -- Avoid division by zero

-- 4.7 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease (condition met)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_kidney_disease_condition AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m2" < 60 OR u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "kidney_disease_condition_met"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_egfr_raw e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_uacr_raw u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m2" IS NOT NULL OR u."uacr_mg_g" IS NOT NULL; -- Only consider if at least one component is available

-- 4.8 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease (mapped to 1/0)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
    k."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN k."kidney_disease_condition_met" = TRUE THEN 1
        WHEN k."kidney_disease_condition_met" = FALSE THEN 0
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_outcome_kidney_disease_condition k;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    r."race_hispanic_origin",
    g."gender",
    a."age_at_screening",
    p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
    o."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_pred_wwi_ratio_quartiles p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_kidney_disease o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked doctor told you have diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or who takes diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who meet any of the diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE
    "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
    OR "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
    OR "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
    OR "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL OR f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who are pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" != 'Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam';

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Race/Hispanic Origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age at Screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Composite Glycemic Status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_composite_glycemic_status AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' THEN (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" + f."glycohemoglobin") * 1.1
    WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'No' THEN (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" + f."glycohemoglobin") * 0.9
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "composite_glycemic_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Weight-Adjusted-Waist (WWI) Ratio
-- Calculate raw WWI ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0; -- Ensure no division by zero or sqrt of negative

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for WWI ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign WWI ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT
  r."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
    WHEN r."wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
  END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.6 Outcome
-- Step 1: Calculate UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "UACR"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" > 0; -- Avoid division by zero

-- Step 2: Determine intermediate_outcome_condition
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_intermediate_outcome_condition AS
SELECT
  u."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN u."UACR" >= 30 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 45 THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS "intermediate_outcome_condition"
FROM temp_uacr_raw u
JOIN final_master_table f ON u."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 3: Map to final outcome (1 or 0)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome AS
SELECT
  i."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN i."intermediate_outcome_condition" THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "outcome"

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes participants who meet any of the diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR "fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0
   OR "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
   OR "take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20 OR f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Race/Hispanic Origin (Covariate)
-- Map race/Hispanic origin to 'white' or 'non white'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Mexican American', 'Non-Hispanic Black', 'Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'non white'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender (Covariate)
-- Extract gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age at Screening (Covariate)
-- Bin age at screening into '<60' and '>=60'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Log-transformed Index (Predictor)
-- Calculate log-transformed index
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_log_transformed_index AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       LN(1 + f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "log_transformed_index"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Presence of Kidney Disease (Outcome)
-- STEP 4.5.1: Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.2: Calculate eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.5.3: Determine presence of kidney disease based on eGFR and UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" < 60 OR u."urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  l."log_transformed_index",
  pkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_log_transformed_index l ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = l."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease pkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = pkd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL) .

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Calculate mean serum creatinine (mg/dl) within race/hispanic origin group among participants aged less than 60 years
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_creatinine_for_inclusion AS
SELECT
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    AVG("creatinine_mg_dl") AS "mean_creatinine"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" < 60
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate median waist circumference (cm) within gender group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_median_waist_for_inclusion AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "waist_circumference_cm") AS "median_waist"
FROM final_master_table
GROUP BY "gender";

-- STEP 1.3: Includes patients whose serum creatinine (mg/dL) is not greater than the mean calculated within their race/Hispanic origin group among participants aged less than 60 years
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT f."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table f
JOIN temp_mean_creatinine_for_inclusion mc ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = mc."race_hispanic_origin"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" <= mc."mean_creatinine";

-- STEP 1.4: From the above group, include only those whose waist circumference (cm) is not smaller than the median calculated within their gender group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_median_waist_for_inclusion mw ON f."gender" = mw."gender"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" >= mw."median_waist";

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who are pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" != 'Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam';

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants who are missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants who are missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants who are missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants who are missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants who are missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: Race/Hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
        ELSE 'non white'
    END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: Age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg")) AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0;

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio AS
SELECT
    r."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" IS NULL THEN NULL
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: ≤25th percentile'
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25th and ≤50th percentile'
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50th and ≤75th percentile'
        ELSE 'Q4: >75th percentile'
    END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- 4.5.1 Calculate eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        -- Case 1: female and Non-Hispanic Black
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
        -- Case 2: female and not Non-Hispanic Black
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
        -- Case 3: male and Non-Hispanic Black
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
        -- Case 4: male and not Non-Hispanic Black
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "eGFR_ml_min_1_73m2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5.2 Calculate UACR (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "UACR_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" > 0;

-- 4.5.3 Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN (e."eGFR_ml_min_1_73m2" < 60) OR (u."UACR_mg_g" >= 30) THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  a."age_at_screening",
  wwi."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
  pkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio wwi ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = wwi."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease pkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = pkd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with missing serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing glycoprotein HbA1c (%)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
-- Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?”
-- and if they answer as "borderline" and either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
    f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND
    (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5)
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 49 THEN '30-49'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 50 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 60 THEN '45-60' -- Label '45-60' for range 50-60 to avoid overlap with '30-49'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 61 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 74 THEN '61-74'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 75 THEN '>=75'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Covariate: age when first told diabetes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_when_first_told_diabetes AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30 AND f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" <= 39 THEN '30-39'
           WHEN f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 40 AND f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" <= 49 THEN '40-49'
           WHEN f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 50 THEN '>=50'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_when_first_told_diabetes"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.5 Covariate: interaction covariate (HbA1c * female indicator)
-- Step 1: Create gender_binary
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_binary AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' THEN 1.0
           ELSE 0.0
       END AS "gender_binary"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate interaction covariate
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_interaction_covariate AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."glycohemoglobin" * g."gender_binary") AS "interaction_covariate"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender_binary g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.6 Predictor: serum albumin (g/dL) categorized into quartiles
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'q1: <25%'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'q2: ≥25 and <50%'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'q3: ≥50 and <75%'
    ELSE 'q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.7 Outcome: diabetic retinopathy
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tg."gender",
  tr."race_hispanic_origin",
  tas."age_in_years_at_screening",
  tad."age_when_first_told_diabetes",
  tic."interaction_covariate",
  tsa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin tr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tr."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_when_first_told_diabetes tad ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tad."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_interaction_covariate tic ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tic."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_serum_albumin_g_dl tsa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsa."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2008. 
 Only include participants who has information on gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin mg/L, urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl), weight (kg), waist circumference (cm).
 Exclude patients who do not have diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the criteria: who reported yes to doctor told you have diabetes  or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) not less than 7.0 mmol/L or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) not smaller than 6.5%, or take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar.
 The predictor is adjusted weight, which is the the square root of weight(kg) for gender that is less common and we will do *2 to this value for gender that is more common..
 The outcome is the presence of kidney disease, defined as a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater  when the urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).
 The covariates include "race/Hispanic origin, gender, age at screening, and Score ABC. Score ABC is calculated by first finding the Glucose-to-Waist Ratio (GWR) as fasting glucose (mmol/L) divided by waist circumference (cm). This GWR is then conditionally adjusted: if the participant is not taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, the GWR is multiplied by the difference between their glycoprotein HbA1C (%) and 7(%); if the participant is taking diabetic pills, the GWR is divided by the same difference. Score ABC value is the absolute value of this conditional result.
