SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants with non-missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include those with non-missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include those with non-missing urine albumin mg/L
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.4: From the above group, include those with non-missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.5: From the above group, include those with non-missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.6: From the above group, include those with non-missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.7: From the above group, include those with non-missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients who do not have diabetes
-- Diabetes is defined as meeting any of the criteria:
--   - doctor told you have diabetes = 'Yes'
--   - fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
--   - glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%%) >= 6.5%%
--   - take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = 'Yes'
-- We keep patients who meet at least one of these criteria (i.e., have diabetes).
-- We exclude patients who do NOT meet any of these criteria.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes') OR
    (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NULL OR f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0) OR
    (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5) OR
    (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes');

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Race/Hispanic Origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age at Screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Score ABC
-- Calculate Glucose-to-Waist Ratio (GWR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gwr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" / f."waist_circumference_cm") AS "gwr"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."waist_circumference_cm" != 0; -- Avoid division by zero

-- Calculate Score ABC based on diabetic pill usage
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_abc AS
SELECT g."respondent_sequence_number",
       ABS(
           CASE
               WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'No' THEN g."gwr" * (f."glycohemoglobin" - 7.0)
               WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' THEN
                   CASE
                       WHEN (f."glycohemoglobin" - 7.0) = 0 THEN NULL -- Handle division by zero
                       ELSE g."gwr" / (f."glycohemoglobin" - 7.0)
                   END
               ELSE NULL
           END
       ) AS "score_abc"
FROM temp_gwr g
JOIN final_master_table f ON g."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" IS NOT NULL;

-- 4.5 Adjusted Weight
-- Determine gender frequencies to identify more/less common gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_counts AS
SELECT f."gender",
       COUNT(c."respondent_sequence_number") AS "count"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."gender";

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_frequency AS
SELECT
    (SELECT "gender" FROM temp_gender_counts ORDER BY "count" ASC LIMIT 1) AS "gender_less_common",
    (SELECT "gender" FROM temp_gender_counts ORDER BY "count" DESC LIMIT 1) AS "gender_more_common";

-- Calculate adjusted weight
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_adjusted_weight AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."gender" = (SELECT "gender_less_common" FROM temp_gender_frequency) THEN SQRT(f."weight_kg")
           WHEN f."gender" = (SELECT "gender_more_common" FROM temp_gender_frequency) THEN SQRT(f."weight_kg") * 2.0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "adjusted_weight"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- 4.6 Presence of Kidney Disease
-- Calculate UACR (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0; -- Avoid division by zero

-- Map UACR to presence of kidney disease (1 or 0)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT u."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30.0 THEN 1
           WHEN u."uacr_mg_g" < 30.0 THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_uacr u;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  rho."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  aas."age_at_screening",
  sa."score_abc",
  aw."adjusted_weight",
  pkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin rho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = rho."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening aas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = aas."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_score_abc sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_adjusted_weight aw ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = aw."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease pkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = pkd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants with non-missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include those with non-missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.3: From the above group, include those with non-missing urine albumin mg/L
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.4: From the above group, include those with non-missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.5: From the above group, include those with non-missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.6: From the above group, include those with non-missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 1.7: From the above group, include those with non-missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients who do not have diabetes
-- Diabetes is defined as meeting any of the criteria:
--   - doctor told you have diabetes = 'Yes'
--   - fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L
--   - glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%%) >= 6.5%%
--   - take diabetic pills to lower blood sugar = 'Yes'
-- We keep patients who meet at least one of these criteria (i.e., have diabetes).
-- We exclude patients who do NOT meet any of these criteria.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE
    (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes') OR
    (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NULL OR f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0) OR
    (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5) OR
    (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes');

-- ========== STEP 3: Final Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Race/Hispanic Origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Age at Screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Score ABC
-- Calculate Glucose-to-Waist Ratio (GWR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gwr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" / f."waist_circumference_cm") AS "gwr"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."waist_circumference_cm" != 0; -- Avoid division by zero

-- Calculate Score ABC based on diabetic pill usage
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_abc AS
SELECT g."respondent_sequence_number",
       ABS(
           CASE
               WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'No' THEN g."gwr" * (f."glycohemoglobin" - 7.0)
               WHEN f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' THEN
                   CASE
                       WHEN (f."glycohemoglobin" - 7.0) = 0 THEN NULL -- Handle division by zero
                       ELSE g."gwr" / (f."glycohemoglobin" - 7.0)
                   END
               ELSE NULL
           END
       ) AS "score_abc"
FROM temp_gwr g
JOIN final_master_table f ON g."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" IS NOT NULL;

-- 4.5 Adjusted Weight
-- Determine gender frequencies to identify more/less common gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_counts AS
SELECT f."gender",
       COUNT(c."respondent_sequence_number") AS "count"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
GROUP BY f."gender";

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender_frequency AS
SELECT
    (SELECT "gender" FROM temp_gender_counts ORDER BY "count" ASC LIMIT 1) AS "gender_less_common",
    (SELECT "gender" FROM temp_gender_counts ORDER BY "count" DESC LIMIT 1) AS "gender_more_common";

-- Calculate adjusted weight
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_adjusted_weight AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."gender" = (SELECT "gender_less_common" FROM temp_gender_frequency) THEN SQRT(f."weight_kg")
           WHEN f."gender" = (SELECT "gender_more_common" FROM temp_gender_frequency) THEN SQRT(f."weight_kg") * 2.0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "adjusted_weight"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- 4.6 Presence of Kidney Disease
-- Calculate UACR (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" != 0; -- Avoid division by zero

-- Map UACR to presence of kidney disease (1 or 0)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_presence_of_kidney_disease AS
SELECT u."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30.0 THEN 1
           WHEN u."uacr_mg_g" < 30.0 THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_uacr u;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  rho."race_hispanic_origin",
  g."gender",
  aas."age_at_screening",
  sa."score_abc",
  aw."adjusted_weight",
  pkd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin rho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = rho."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_age_at_screening aas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = aas."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_score_abc sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_adjusted_weight aw ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = aw."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_presence_of_kidney_disease pkd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = pkd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

The problem states: "fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) not less than 7.0 mmol/L" and "glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) not smaller than 6.5%".
The SQL includes `IS NULL` in these conditions:
`f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NULL OR f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0`
`f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5`

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Identify participants aged 20 or older for FPG median calculation
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_fpg_eligible_for_median AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    "fasting_glucose_mmol_l",
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: Calculate race/Hispanic origin specific median fasting plasma glucose (FPG) for participants aged 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_fpg_medians AS
SELECT
    "race_hispanic_origin",
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "fasting_glucose_mmol_l") AS median_fpg
FROM temp_fpg_eligible_for_median
GROUP BY "race_hispanic_origin";

-- STEP 1.3: Include participants who cannot be classified as failing specific diabetes criteria
-- Failure is defined as: (FPG < race/Hispanic origin specific median) AND (HbA1c < 6.5) AND (NOT taking diabetic pills)
-- We include if NOT (failure criteria). This handles NULLs by including them if they cannot be definitively classified as failing.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT
    f."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table f
LEFT JOIN temp_fpg_medians m ON f."race_hispanic_origin" = m."race_hispanic_origin"
WHERE NOT (
    (f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" IS NOT NULL AND m.median_fpg IS NOT NULL AND f."fasting_glucose_mmol_l" < m.median_fpg)
    AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL AND f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5)
    AND (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" IS NOT NULL AND f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'No')
);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20 OR f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL; -- Keep NULLs unless specified to exclude

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants pregnant at the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing weight (kg)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing waist circumference (cm)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: race/hispanic origin (white and non white)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
        ELSE 'non white'
    END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Covariate: gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_gender AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Covariate: age at screening (<60 and >=60)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cov_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '<60'
        WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 THEN '>=60'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
-- Calculate raw weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    f."waist_circumference_cm" / SQRT(f."weight_kg") AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."waist_circumference_cm" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" IS NOT NULL AND f."weight_kg" > 0; -- Ensure weight is positive for SQRT

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw") AS q3
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw;

-- Assign weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_quartiles AS
SELECT
    r."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" IS NULL THEN NULL
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <=25%'
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and <=50%'
        WHEN r."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio_raw" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and <=75%'
        ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
    END AS "weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio"
FROM temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_raw r;

-- 4.5 Outcome: presence of kidney disease
-- Step 4.5.1: Calculate eGFR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_egfr AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.2: Calculate Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_uacr AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" > 0; -- Avoid division by zero

-- Step 4.5.3: Determine presence of kidney disease (boolean)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_kidney_disease_boolean AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" < 60 OR u."uacr_mg_g" >= 30 THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS "has_kidney_disease_boolean"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.4: Map presence of kidney disease to 1 or 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_outcome_kidney_disease AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "has_kidney_disease_boolean" = TRUE THEN 1
        WHEN "has_kidney_disease_boolean" = FALSE THEN 0
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_outcome_kidney_disease_boolean;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    r."race_hispanic_origin",
    g."gender",
    a."age_at_screening",
    p."weight_adjusted_waist_wwi_ratio",
    o."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_cov_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_predictor_wwi_ratio_quartiles p ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = p."respondent_sequence_number"
LEFT JOIN temp_outcome_kidney_disease o ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = o."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2011-2012.
 The study will include age at screening >=20, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said yes or borderline when asked whether doctor told you have diabetes, reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126.
 Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead.
 The predictor is Level ABC, defined as the food security score divided by the mean fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) calculated among participants of the same gender who have been told by a doctor they have diabetes. The individual food security score is an ordinal measure of food stability derived from the adult food security questionnaire, categorized as very low (0), low (1), marginal (2), or full food security (3).
 The outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
 Covariates include age in years at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Question Structured:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2012

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- (doctor told you have diabetes = Yes OR doctor told you have diabetes = Borderline) OR reported taking diabetic pills to lower blood sugar OR fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things" OR missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" OR missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much" OR missing "feeling tired or having little energy" OR missing "poor appetite or overeating" OR missing "feeling bad about yourself" OR missing "trouble concentrating on things" OR missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast" OR missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age in years at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- Level ABC calculated as:
  individual food security score / mean fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl)
  where:
    individual food security score is an ordinal measure of food stability derived from the adult food security questionnaire, categorized as:
      very low (0)
      low (1)
      marginal (2)
      full food security (3)
    mean fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) is calculated among participants of the same gender who have been told by a doctor they have diabetes = Yes

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  where total score is the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    "have little interest in doing things"
    "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    "feeling tired or having little energy"
    "poor appetite or overeating"
    "feeling bad about yourself"
    "trouble concentrating on things"
    "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question is scored as:
    Not at all (0)
    Several days (1)
    More than half the days (2)
    Nearly every day (3)
  If a question value is null, assign (0).

SQL:
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients age_in_years_at_screening 20 or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- STEP 1.2: From the above group, include only those who meet the diabetes or high glucose criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
    OR f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline'
    OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
    OR f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" > 126);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."adult_food_security_category" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing any of the depression questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age in years at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Predictor: Level ABC
-- Step 4.4.1: Derive individual food security score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_individual_food_security_score AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD very low food security: 6-10' THEN 0
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD low food security: 3-5' THEN 1
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD marginal food security: 1-2' THEN 2
           WHEN f."adult_food_security_category" = 'AD full food security: 0' THEN 3
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "individual_food_security_score",
       f."gender",
       f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
       f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.4.2: Calculate mean fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) among participants of the same gender who have been told by a doctor they have diabetes = Yes
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_mean_fasting_glucose_by_gender AS
SELECT
    "gender",
    AVG("fasting_glucose_mg_dl") AS "mean_fasting_glucose_mg_dl"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
GROUP BY "gender";

-- Step 4.4.3: Calculate Level ABC
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_level_abc AS
SELECT ifs."respondent_sequence_number",
       (ifs."individual_food_security_score" / mfg."mean_fasting_glucose_mg_dl") AS "level_abc"
FROM temp_individual_food_security_score ifs
JOIN temp_mean_fasting_glucose_by_gender mfg ON ifs."gender" = mfg."gender";

-- 4.5 Outcome: Depression Outcome
-- Step 4.5.1: Score for 'have little interest in doing things'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_interest AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0 -- As per schema, if value is null assign 0
       END AS "have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.2: Score for 'feeling down, depressed, or hopeless'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_depressed AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.3: Score for 'trouble sleeping or sleeping too much'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_sleep AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.4: Score for 'feeling tired or having little energy'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_energy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.5: Score for 'poor appetite or overeating'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_appetite AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "poor_appetite_or_overeating_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.6: Score for 'feeling bad about yourself'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_self_bad AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "feeling_bad_about_yourself_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.7: Score for 'trouble concentrating on things'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_concentration AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "trouble_concentrating_on_things_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.8: Score for 'moving or speaking slowly or too fast'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_movement AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.9: Score for 'thought you would be better off dead'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_score_dead AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Not at all' THEN 0
           WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Several days' THEN 1
           WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'More than half the days' THEN 2
           WHEN f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" = 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
           ELSE 0
       END AS "thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.10: Calculate total score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_total_depression_score AS
SELECT
    s_int."respondent_sequence_number",
    (s_int."have_little_interest_in_doing_things_score" +
     s_dep."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless_score" +
     s_sleep."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much_score" +
     s_energy."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy_score" +
     s_app."poor_appetite_or_overeating_score" +
     s_self."feeling_bad_about_yourself_score" +
     s_conc."trouble_concentrating_on_things_score" +
     s_move."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast_score" +
     s_dead."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead_score") AS "total_score"
FROM temp_score_interest s_int
JOIN temp_score_depressed s_dep ON s_int."respondent_sequence_number" = s_dep."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_sleep s_sleep ON s_int."respondent_sequence_number" = s_sleep."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_energy s_energy ON s_int."respondent_sequence_number" = s_energy."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_appetite s_app ON s_int."respondent_sequence_number" = s_app."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_self_bad s_self ON s_int."respondent_sequence_number" = s_self."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_concentration s_conc ON s_int."respondent_sequence_number" = s_conc."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_movement s_move ON s_int."respondent_sequence_number" = s_move."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_score_dead s_dead ON s_int."respondent_sequence_number" = s_dead."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 4.5.11: Determine depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN "total_score" >= 10 THEN 1
           ELSE 0
       END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_total_depression_score;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  tc."respondent_sequence_number",
  t_age."age_in_years_at_screening",
  t_gender."gender",
  t_race."race_hispanic_origin",
  t_level_abc."level_abc",
  t_dep_outcome."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort tc
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening t_age ON tc."respondent_sequence_number" = t_age."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender t_gender ON tc."respondent_sequence_number" = t_gender."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin t_race ON tc."respondent_sequence_number" = t_race."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_level_abc t_level_abc ON tc."respondent_sequence_number" = t_level_abc."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome t_dep_outcome ON tc."respondent_sequence_number" = t_dep_outcome."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;
